flows.name: Dual Hydraulic Failure (HYD)

sop_intro.t: Dual Hydraulic Failure. The A320 has three hydraulic systems — Green, Blue and Yellow. Lose two and what remains decides how you fly and land. We will identify the case, run the E-CAM, work the Q-R-H summary, review the status, decide, and brief the approach. The actions are drilled; the consequences are narrated.

flows.source: QRH Rev 2018-08-13 § ABN HYD — HYD B+Y / G+B / G+Y SYS LO PR Summaries (p.83-86) + FCTM § AOP-30 (QRH summary usage, decision, briefing)

sop_intro.t: First, identify which two systems you have lost — that is the whole game.

items.why: Two low-pressure cautions mean a dual hydraulic failure. Which two you lose decides everything. Three cases: Blue and Yellow lost leaves Green — the best case. Green and Blue lost leaves Yellow. Green and Yellow lost leaves Blue — the worst case, with no stabilizer.

brief_line.t: Two hydraulic systems low pressure. IDENTIFY WHICH TWO ARE LOST. Two low-pressure cautions mean a dual hydraulic failure. Which two you lose decides everything. Three cases: Blue and Yellow lost leaves Green — the best case. Green and Blue lost leaves Yellow. Green and Yellow lost leaves Blue — the worst case, with no stabilizer.

sop_intro.t: First, identify which two systems you have lost — that is the whole game.

items.why: Handle the two low-pressure cautions on the E-CAM as normal — master caution, E-CAM actions, clear, and read the status page. The detailed tasksharing loop is the one you drilled in Abnormal Management.

brief_line.t: Run the E-CAM first. E-CAM ACTIONS, THEN STATUS. Handle the two low-pressure cautions on the E-CAM as normal — master caution, E-CAM actions, clear, and read the status page. The detailed tasksharing loop is the one you drilled in Abnormal Management.

sop_intro.t: First, identify which two systems you have lost — that is the whole game.

items.why: After the E-CAM, go to the Q-R-H hydraulic summary for your case. It is split into cruise, approach, landing and go-around, and it links to the performance section for your landing distance.

brief_line.t: Then the Q-R-H summary. WORK CRUISE, APPROACH, LANDING, GO-AROUND. After the E-CAM, go to the Q-R-H hydraulic summary for your case. It is split into cruise, approach, landing and go-around, and it links to the performance section for your landing distance.

sop_intro.t: The best case. Green remains, so flight controls stay in normal law and braking is normal.

items.action: MAX SPEED 320 KNOTS / MACH 0.77, MANEUVER WITH CARE
items.why: Flight controls remain in normal law. Expect increased fuel consumption — use the fuel penalty tables — and assess landing performance from the Q-R-H performance chapter.

brief_line.t: Cruise. MAX SPEED 320 KNOTS or MACH 0.77, MANEUVER WITH CARE. Flight controls remain in normal law. Expect increased fuel consumption — use the fuel penalty tables — and assess landing performance from the Q-R-H performance chapter.

sop_intro.t: The best case. Green remains, so flight controls stay in normal law and braking is normal.

items.action: CAT 2 INOP, SLATS AND FLAPS SLOW, GEAR BY GRAVITY
items.why: Category 2 is inoperative and slats and flaps move slowly. The landing gear is extended by gravity: pull and turn the gravity gear handcrank three turns to the stop, gear lever down, check gear down.

brief_line.t: Approach. CAT 2 INOP, SLATS AND FLAPS SLOW, GEAR BY GRAVITY. Category 2 is inoperative and slats and flaps move slowly. The landing gear is extended by gravity: pull and turn the gravity gear handcrank three turns to the stop, gear lever down, check gear down.

sop_intro.t: The best case. Green remains, so flight controls stay in normal law and braking is normal.

items.action: ENGINE 1 REVERSER ONLY, BRAKING NORMAL, NO NOSEWHEEL STEERING
items.why: Only one elevator and two spoilers per wing. Only the engine one reverser. Braking is normal, but there is no nosewheel steering — plan to track the centreline with rudder and differential braking.

brief_line.t: Landing. ENGINE 1 REVERSER ONLY, BRAKING NORMAL, NO NOSEWHEEL STEERING. Only one elevator and two spoilers per wing. Only the engine one reverser. Braking is normal, but there is no nosewheel steering — plan to track the centreline with rudder and differential braking.

sop_intro.t: The best case. Green remains, so flight controls stay in normal law and braking is normal.

items.action: MAX PITCH 15 DEGREES, NO GEAR RETRACTION
items.why: If you go around, limit pitch to 15 degrees and do not retract the gear. Expect increased fuel consumption.

brief_line.t: Go-around. MAX PITCH 15 DEGREES, NO GEAR RETRACTION. If you go around, limit pitch to 15 degrees and do not retract the gear. Expect increased fuel consumption.

sop_intro.t: Yellow remains. Alternate law, alternate braking, and you land flap 3.

items.action: SPEEDBRAKES DO NOT USE, MAX 320 / MACH 0.77, ALTERNATE LAW
items.why: Do not use speedbrakes. Flight controls are in alternate law — protections are lost. Increased fuel consumption; assess landing performance from the performance chapter.

brief_line.t: Cruise. SPEEDBRAKES DO NOT USE, MAX 320 or MACH 0.77, ALTERNATE LAW. Do not use speedbrakes. Flight controls are in alternate law — protections are lost. Increased fuel consumption; assess landing performance from the performance chapter.

sop_intro.t: Yellow remains. Alternate law, alternate braking, and you land flap 3.

items.action: CAT 2 INOP, AUTOTHRUST OFF, FOR LANDING FLAP 3, GPWS LANDING FLAP 3 ON
items.why: Slats are jammed, flaps move slowly. Autothrust off. Land flap 3 and set GPWS landing flap 3 on. For flap extension, select V-F-E next minus 5 knots.

brief_line.t: Approach. CAT 2 INOP, AUTOTHRUST OFF, FOR LANDING FLAP 3, G-P-W-S LANDING FLAP 3 ON. Slats are jammed, flaps move slowly. Autothrust off. Land flap 3 and set G-P-W-S landing flap 3 on. For flap extension, select V-F-E next minus 5 knots.

sop_intro.t: Yellow remains. Alternate law, alternate braking, and you land flap 3.

items.action: WHEN SPEED 200, GEAR BY GRAVITY; WHEN GEAR DOWN, MANUAL PITCH TRIM
items.why: When at 200 knots, extend the gear by gravity — handcrank three turns, gear lever down, check gear down. When the gear is down, use manual pitch trim. Then decelerate to your calculated V-app.

brief_line.t: Gear and trim. WHEN SPEED 200, GEAR BY GRAVITY; WHEN GEAR DOWN, MANUAL PITCH TRIM. When at 200 knots, extend the gear by gravity — handcrank three turns, gear lever down, check gear down. When the gear is down, use manual pitch trim. Then decelerate to your calculated V-app.

sop_intro.t: Yellow remains. Alternate law, alternate braking, and you land flap 3.

items.action: ENGINE 2 REVERSER ONLY, ALTERNATE BRAKING; GO-AROUND MAX PITCH 15, NO GEAR RETRACTION
items.why: Only one elevator and two spoilers per wing, no ailerons — the aircraft is slightly sluggish, in direct law at flare. Only the engine two reverser. Alternate braking. On a go-around, max pitch 15 degrees and do not retract the gear.

brief_line.t: Landing and go-around. ENGINE 2 REVERSER ONLY, ALTERNATE BRAKING; GO-AROUND MAX PITCH 15, NO GEAR RETRACTION. Only one elevator and two spoilers per wing, no ailerons — the aircraft is slightly sluggish, in direct law at flare. Only the engine two reverser. Alternate braking. On a go-around, max pitch 15 degrees and do not retract the gear.

items.subflow: Case 3 — Green + Yellow Lost (Blue remains) — worst case

sop_intro.t: The worst case. Blue remains — no stabilizer, manual trim unusable, and accumulator-only braking.

items.action: MAX 320 / MACH 0.77, NO STABILIZER, ALTERNATE LAW
items.why: There is no stabilizer — pitch authority is reduced and manual trim is unusable. Alternate law, protections lost. Maneuver with care; increased fuel consumption.

brief_line.t: Cruise. MAX 320 or MACH 0.77, NO STABILIZER, ALTERNATE LAW. There is no stabilizer — pitch authority is reduced and manual trim is unusable. Alternate law, protections lost. Maneuver with care; increased fuel consumption.

items.subflow: Case 3 — Green + Yellow Lost (Blue remains) — worst case

sop_intro.t: The worst case. Blue remains — no stabilizer, manual trim unusable, and accumulator-only braking.

items.action: CAT 2 INOP, FOR LANDING FLAP 3, GPWS FLAP MODE OFF
items.why: Slats slow, flaps jammed. Land flap 3 and set the GPWS flap mode off. For flap extension, select V-F-E next minus 5 knots. In configuration 3, decelerate to V-app and stabilise before gear down so you are trimmed for the approach.

brief_line.t: Approach. CAT 2 INOP, FOR LANDING FLAP 3, G-P-W-S FLAP MODE OFF. Slats slow, flaps jammed. Land flap 3 and set the G-P-W-S flap mode off. For flap extension, select V-F-E next minus 5 knots. In configuration 3, decelerate to V-app and stabilise before gear down so you are trimmed for the approach.

items.subflow: Case 3 — Green + Yellow Lost (Blue remains) — worst case

sop_intro.t: The worst case. Blue remains — no stabilizer, manual trim unusable, and accumulator-only braking.

items.action: GEAR BY GRAVITY; DISREGARD USE MANUAL PITCH TRIM — TRIM UNUSABLE
items.why: Extend the gear by gravity — handcrank three turns, gear lever down, check gear down. Disregard the use-manual-pitch-trim message: the manual trim is unusable.

brief_line.t: Gear and trim. GEAR BY GRAVITY; DISREGARD USE MANUAL PITCH TRIM — TRIM UNUSABLE. Extend the gear by gravity — handcrank three turns, gear lever down, check gear down. Disregard the use-manual-pitch-trim message: the manual trim is unusable.

items.subflow: Case 3 — Green + Yellow Lost (Blue remains) — worst case

sop_intro.t: The worst case. Blue remains — no stabilizer, manual trim unusable, and accumulator-only braking.

items.action: NO REVERSER, BRAKE ACCUMULATOR ONLY — 7 APPLICATIONS, MAX 1000 PSI
items.why: Pitch authority is reduced — watch for tailstrike with flaps near zero. Only one spoiler per wing, direct law. No reverser. Braking is on the yellow accumulator only — about seven applications, max 1000 P-S-I — and no nosewheel steering. On a go-around, do not retract the gear.

brief_line.t: Landing and go-around. NO REVERSER, BRAKE ACCUMULATOR ONLY — 7 APPLICATIONS, MAX 1000 PSI. Pitch authority is reduced — watch for tailstrike with flaps near zero. Only one spoiler per wing, direct law. No reverser. Braking is on the yellow accumulator only — about seven applications, max 1000 P-S-I — and no nosewheel steering. On a go-around, do not retract the gear.

sop_intro.t: The discrete crew actions for a dual hydraulic failure — drilled. I give the cue, you give the action.

items.why: With the systems lost, extend the gear by gravity: pull and turn the gravity gear handcrank three turns clockwise to the mechanical stop, gear lever down, check gear down. The L/G or BRAKES fault may trigger spuriously after a gravity extension.
items.cite: QRH HYD p.83-85 / L/G Gravity Extension p.89

reveal.t: The answer is GRAVITY EXTENSION. Callout: GRAVITY GEAR EXTENSION.

correct_line.t: Correct. Landing gear — normal extension lost, GRAVITY EXTENSION.

explain.t: With the systems lost, extend the gear by gravity: pull and turn the gravity gear handcrank three turns clockwise to the mechanical stop, gear lever down, check gear down. The landing gear or BRAKES fault may trigger spuriously after a gravity extension. Source: Q-R-H HYD p.83-85 / landing gear Gravity Extension p.89.

sop_intro.t: The discrete crew actions for a dual hydraulic failure — drilled. I give the cue, you give the action.

items.item: For landing, when a green-loss case (flaps affected)
items.why: In the Green-plus-Blue and Green-plus-Yellow cases you land flap 3, because the flaps are slow or jammed.
items.cite: QRH HYD G+B p.84 / G+Y p.85

prompt.t: For landing, when a green-loss case (flaps affected)

reveal.t: The answer is FLAP 3. Callout: FLAP 3.

correct_line.t: Correct. For landing, when a green-loss case (flaps affected), FLAP 3.

explain.t: In the Green-plus-Blue and Green-plus-Yellow cases you land flap 3, because the flaps are slow or jammed. Source: Q-R-H HYD G+B p.84 / G+Y p.85.

sop_intro.t: The discrete crew actions for a dual hydraulic failure — drilled. I give the cue, you give the action.

items.why: Green-plus-Blue: set GPWS landing flap 3 on, so the flap 3 landing does not trigger a too-low-flaps alert.

reveal.t: The answer is LANDING FLAP 3 ON. Callout: G-P-W-S LANDING FLAP 3 ON.

correct_line.t: Correct. G-P-W-S, Green + Blue case, LANDING FLAP 3 ON.

explain.t: Green-plus-Blue: set G-P-W-S landing flap 3 on, so the flap 3 landing does not trigger a too-low-flaps alert. Source: Q-R-H HYD G+B p.84.

sop_intro.t: The discrete crew actions for a dual hydraulic failure — drilled. I give the cue, you give the action.

items.why: Green-plus-Yellow: set the GPWS flap mode off.

reveal.t: The answer is FLAP MODE OFF. Callout: G-P-W-S FLAP MODE OFF.

correct_line.t: Correct. G-P-W-S, Green + Yellow case, FLAP MODE OFF.

explain.t: Green-plus-Yellow: set the G-P-W-S flap mode off. Source: Q-R-H HYD G+Y p.85.

sop_intro.t: The discrete crew actions for a dual hydraulic failure — drilled. I give the cue, you give the action.

items.why: Extend each stage at V-F-E next minus 5 knots, because the surfaces move slowly.
items.cite: QRH HYD G+B p.84 / G+Y p.85

reveal.t: The answer is V-F-E NEXT MINUS 5. Callout: V-F-E NEXT MINUS 5 KNOTS.

correct_line.t: Correct. Speed for flap extension, V-F-E NEXT MINUS 5.

explain.t: Extend each stage at V-F-E next minus 5 knots, because the surfaces move slowly. Source: Q-R-H HYD G+B p.84 / G+Y p.85.

sop_intro.t: The discrete crew actions for a dual hydraulic failure — drilled. I give the cue, you give the action.

items.why: When in the landing configuration on final, decelerate to your calculated V-app.

reveal.t: The answer is DECELERATE TO V app. Callout: DECELERATE TO V-APP.

correct_line.t: Correct. Approach speed, DECELERATE TO V app.

explain.t: When in the landing configuration on final, decelerate to your calculated V-app. Source: Q-R-H HYD p.84-85.

sop_intro.t: Now the management: read the status, work the numbers, decide, and inform.

items.action: READ THE INOP SYSTEMS AND LIMITATIONS
items.why: The status page shows the inoperative systems and the landing limitations — Category 2 inoperative, the braking and steering restrictions, and the maximum speed.

brief_line.t: STATUS page. READ THE INOP SYSTEMS AND LIMITATIONS. The status page shows the inoperative systems and the landing limitations — Category 2 inoperative, the braking and steering restrictions, and the maximum speed.

sop_intro.t: Now the management: read the status, work the numbers, decide, and inform.

items.why: Compute your landing distance from the Q-R-H performance chapter or the performance application — the degraded braking and reverser increase the distance. Account for the fuel penalty too.
items.cite: QRH HYD p.83 / FCTM AOP-30 p.79

brief_line.t: Landing performance. RECOMPUTE FROM THE PERFORMANCE CHAPTER. Compute your landing distance from the Q-R-H performance chapter or the performance application — the degraded braking and reverser increase the distance. Account for the fuel penalty too.

sop_intro.t: Now the management: read the status, work the numbers, decide, and inform.

items.why: There is no land-as-soon-as-possible on a dual hydraulic failure — it is serious but managed. Assess the situation, decide on a suitable airport with adequate landing distance, and inform air traffic control, the cabin crew and the passengers.

brief_line.t: The decision. ASSESS, DECIDE, INFORM. There is no land-as-soon-as-possible on a dual hydraulic failure — it is serious but managed. Assess the situation, decide on a suitable airport with adequate landing distance, and inform air traffic control, the cabin crew and the passengers.

sop_intro.t: Brief the degraded approach and landing for your case, then call for the approach checklist.

items.why: Brief the landing flap for your case, the V-app, and that the gear goes down by gravity. Plan a stable, on-speed approach — the surfaces are slow and, in the worst case, manual trim is unusable.

brief_line.t: Brief the configuration. FLAP, V app, GEAR BY GRAVITY. Brief the landing flap for your case, the V-app, and that the gear goes down by gravity. Plan a stable, on-speed approach — the surfaces are slow and, in the worst case, manual trim is unusable.

sop_intro.t: Brief the degraded approach and landing for your case, then call for the approach checklist.

items.action: BRAKING MODE, NO NOSEWHEEL STEERING, REVERSER
items.why: Brief the braking — normal, alternate, or accumulator-only with max 1000 P-S-I depending on the case — that there is no nosewheel steering, and which reverser you have. Plan directional control on rudder and differential braking.

brief_line.t: Brief the landing roll. BRAKING MODE, NO NOSEWHEEL STEERING, REVERSER. Brief the braking — normal, alternate, or accumulator-only with max 1000 P-S-I depending on the case — that there is no nosewheel steering, and which reverser you have. Plan directional control on rudder and differential braking.

sop_intro.t: Brief the degraded approach and landing for your case, then call for the approach checklist.

items.action: MAX PITCH 15 IF APPLICABLE, NO GEAR RETRACTION
items.why: Brief the go-around: pitch limit where it applies, and no gear retraction. Then call for the approach checklist.
items.cite: QRH HYD p.83-85 / FCTM AOP-30 p.88

brief_line.t: Brief the go-around. MAX PITCH 15 IF APPLICABLE, NO GEAR RETRACTION. Brief the go-around: pitch limit where it applies, and no gear retraction. Then call for the approach checklist.

flows.name: ECAM Readout Primer (Reading the E/WD, SD & STATUS)

sop_intro.t: E-CAM Readout Primer. This is narrated — nothing to answer. We cover how to read the E-CAM: the colour code, the warning and caution levels and their sounds, the engine and warning display, the system and status display, the status page, and how the read-and-do sequence clears.

flows.source: FCOM Rev 2018-08-13 § DSC-31 Indicating/Recording Systems (ECAM Description, E/WD, SD, ECAM Sequence)

sop_intro.t: The E-CAM is two display units. Know what each one shows.

items.why: The upper display is the engine and warning display: engine parameters, fuel on board, flap and slat position, and in its lower part the warnings, cautions and memos. The lower display is the system and status display: the system synoptic pages and the status page.

brief_line.t: The E-CAM. TWO DISPLAYS — UPPER AND LOWER. The upper display is the engine and warning display: engine parameters, fuel on board, flap and slat position, and in its lower part the warnings, cautions and memos. The lower display is the system and status display: the system synoptic pages and the status page.

sop_intro.t: Six colours, each with a fixed meaning. Colour tells you the importance before you read a word.

items.action: RED, AMBER, GREEN, WHITE, BLUE, MAGENTA
items.why: Red: the configuration or failure requires immediate action. Amber: be aware, but no immediate action needed. Green: operating normally. White: titles and remarks that guide you through the procedures. Blue: actions to carry out, or limitations. Magenta: particular messages such as inhibition messages.

brief_line.t: Colour code. RED, AMBER, GREEN, WHITE, BLUE, MAGENTA. Red: the configuration or failure requires immediate action. Amber: be aware, but no immediate action needed. Green: operating normally. White: titles and remarks that guide you through the procedures. Blue: actions to carry out, or limitations. Magenta: particular messages such as inhibition messages.

sop_intro.t: Three levels of alert, plus advisory. The level sets the sound, the light, and whether the system page comes up.

items.why: A red warning means a dangerous configuration, or a failure affecting flight safety — for example engine fire or excess cabin altitude. You get a continuous repetitive chime, or a specific sound or synthetic voice, the master warning flashes red, a red message on the warning display, and the relevant system page appears automatically.

brief_line.t: Level 3 — red warning. IMMEDIATE ACTION. A red warning means a dangerous configuration, or a failure affecting flight safety — for example engine fire or excess cabin altitude. You get a continuous repetitive chime, or a specific sound or synthetic voice, the master warning flashes red, a red message on the warning display, and the relevant system page appears automatically.

sop_intro.t: Three levels of alert, plus advisory. The level sets the sound, the light, and whether the system page comes up.

items.why: An amber caution is a failure with no direct consequence on flight safety — for example green hydraulic low pressure. You get a single chime, the master caution steady amber, an amber message, and the system page appears automatically. Time and situation permitting, deal with it without delay to prevent further degradation.

brief_line.t: Level 2 — amber caution. BE AWARE, ACT WHEN ABLE. An amber caution is a failure with no direct consequence on flight safety — for example green hydraulic low pressure. You get a single chime, the master caution steady amber, an amber message, and the system page appears automatically. Time and situation permitting, deal with it without delay to prevent further degradation.

sop_intro.t: Three levels of alert, plus advisory. The level sets the sound, the light, and whether the system page comes up.

items.why: A level one caution requires only crew monitoring — for example a loss of redundancy. There is no aural and no master light, just an amber message, generally without a procedure.

brief_line.t: Level 1 — amber caution. MONITOR ONLY. A level one caution requires only crew monitoring — for example a loss of redundancy. There is no aural and no master light, just an amber message, generally without a procedure.

sop_intro.t: Three levels of alert, plus advisory. The level sets the sound, the light, and whether the system page comes up.

items.why: An advisory is system parameter monitoring: the relevant system page appears automatically and the affected parameter pulses green. No aural.

brief_line.t: Advisory. MONITOR A DRIFTING PARAMETER. An advisory is system parameter monitoring: the relevant system page appears automatically and the affected parameter pulses green. No aural.

sop_intro.t: Three levels of alert, plus advisory. The level sets the sound, the light, and whether the system page comes up.

items.why: A level three warning has priority over a level two caution, which has priority over a level one caution. The warning computer enforces this order.

brief_line.t: Priority. LEVEL 3 OVER LEVEL 2 OVER LEVEL 1. A level three warning has priority over a level two caution, which has priority over a level one caution. The warning computer enforces this order.

sop_intro.t: Learn the sounds — eyes-down, the aural tells you the level and the system.

items.why: The continuous repetitive chime — or a specific sound or synthetic voice — signals a red warning.

brief_line.t: Continuous repetitive chime. RED WARNING. The continuous repetitive chime — or a specific sound or synthetic voice — signals a red warning.

sop_intro.t: Learn the sounds — eyes-down, the aural tells you the level and the system.

items.why: A single chime signals an amber caution.

brief_line.t: Single chime. AMBER CAUTION. A single chime signals an amber caution.

sop_intro.t: Learn the sounds — eyes-down, the aural tells you the level and the system.

items.why: The cavalry charge signals autopilot disconnection. Press the master warning or the takeover pushbutton to cancel it.

brief_line.t: Cavalry charge. AUTOPILOT DISCONNECT. The cavalry charge signals autopilot disconnection. Press the master warning or the takeover pushbutton to cancel it.

sop_intro.t: Learn the sounds — eyes-down, the aural tells you the level and the system.

items.why: The C chord is the altitude alert. It cancels on a new altitude selection or by pressing the master warning.

brief_line.t: C chord. ALTITUDE ALERT. The C chord is the altitude alert. It cancels on a new altitude selection or by pressing the master warning.

sop_intro.t: Learn the sounds — eyes-down, the aural tells you the level and the system.

items.why: You can cancel any audio indicator with the emergency cancel pushbutton on the ECAM control panel, or the master warning pushbutton — except for overspeed or landing-gear-not-down warnings.

brief_line.t: To cancel an aural. EMERGENCY CANCEL OR MASTER WARNING. You can cancel any audio indicator with the emergency cancel pushbutton on the E-CAM control panel, or the master warning pushbutton — except for overspeed or landing-gear-not-down warnings.

sop_intro.t: The lower part of the upper display is where the failure and its actions live.

items.action: THE TITLE PLUS WHAT TO DO
items.why: When a failure is detected and there is no flight-phase inhibition, the warning display shows the title of the failure and the actions you must perform. An action line clears automatically once you have done it — though some lines do not disappear even when the action is complete.

brief_line.t: Failure title and action lines. THE TITLE PLUS WHAT TO DO. When a failure is detected and there is no flight-phase inhibition, the warning display shows the title of the failure and the actions you must perform. An action line clears automatically once you have done it — though some lines do not disappear even when the action is complete.

sop_intro.t: The lower part of the upper display is where the failure and its actions live.

items.why: A primary failure is shown as a boxed title on the bottom left.

brief_line.t: Primary failure. A BOXED TITLE. A primary failure is shown as a boxed title on the bottom left.

sop_intro.t: The lower part of the upper display is where the failure and its actions live.

items.action: A STAR IN FRONT OF THE TITLE
items.why: A secondary failure — a loss caused by the primary failure — is marked with a star in front of the affected system title, on the bottom right.

brief_line.t: Secondary failure. A STAR IN FRONT OF THE TITLE. A secondary failure — a loss caused by the primary failure — is marked with a star in front of the affected system title, on the bottom right.

sop_intro.t: The lower part of the upper display is where the failure and its actions live.

items.why: If there are too many messages for the space, a green arrow points down at the bottom. Scroll to the rest by pressing the clear pushbutton.

brief_line.t: Overflow. GREEN ARROW — SCROLL WITH CLEAR. If there are too many messages for the space, a green arrow points down at the bottom. Scroll to the rest by pressing the clear pushbutton.

sop_intro.t: The lower part of the upper display is where the failure and its actions live.

items.why: During takeoff and landing most warnings are inhibited so you are not distracted at high-workload moments; the display shows a magenta takeoff inhibit or landing inhibit memo.

brief_line.t: Takeoff and landing inhibit. MAGENTA TAKEOFF OR LANDING INHIBIT. During takeoff and landing most warnings are inhibited so you are not distracted at high-workload moments; the display shows a magenta takeoff inhibit or landing inhibit memo.

sop_intro.t: The lower display shows the system synoptics — twelve of them.

items.action: ENGINE, BLEED, CABIN PRESS, ELEC, HYD, FUEL, APU, COND, DOOR, WHEEL, FLIGHT CONTROLS, CRUISE
items.why: The lower display can show twelve system synoptic pages. They appear automatically on a failure or an advisory, or by flight phase, and you can call any of them manually on the ECAM control panel — except the cruise page.

brief_line.t: Twelve system pages. ENGINE, BLEED, CABIN PRESS, ELEC, HYD, FUEL, APU, COND, DOOR, WHEEL, FLIGHT CONTROLS, CRUISE. The lower display can show twelve system synoptic pages. They appear automatically on a failure or an advisory, or by flight phase, and you can call any of them manually on the E-CAM control panel — except the cruise page.

sop_intro.t: The lower display shows the system synoptics — twelve of them.

items.why: Amber crosses mean a parameter value is not available. Amber dashes over the last digits mean its accuracy is degraded.

brief_line.t: Amber crosses or dashes. PARAMETER NOT AVAILABLE OR DEGRADED. Amber crosses mean a parameter value is not available. Amber dashes over the last digits mean its accuracy is degraded.

sop_intro.t: The status page is your operational summary of the aircraft after the failure.

items.why: The status page summarises the state of the aircraft: limitations in blue, approach procedures, the procedures and corrections to apply for landing in blue, information in green, cancelled cautions in white, inoperative systems in amber, and maintenance status in white.

brief_line.t: Status page contents. OPERATIONAL SUMMARY OF THE AIRCRAFT. The status page summarises the state of the aircraft: limitations in blue, approach procedures, the procedures and corrections to apply for landing in blue, information in green, cancelled cautions in white, inoperative systems in amber, and maintenance status in white.

sop_intro.t: The status page is your operational summary of the aircraft after the failure.

items.why: The status page appears when you press the status pushbutton. In abnormal operations it also appears automatically when you clear the last alert on the warning display, or when you select configuration one for approach.

brief_line.t: Calling the status page. STATUS PUSHBUTTON. The status page appears when you press the status pushbutton. In abnormal operations it also appears automatically when you clear the last alert on the warning display, or when you select configuration one for approach.

items.subflow: The Read-and-Do Sequence — Clearing the ECAM

sop_intro.t: How the E-CAM walks you through a failure and how the clear pushbutton steps you back to normal.

items.action: MESSAGES, MASTER LIGHT, AURAL, SYSTEM PAGE, CLEAR LIT
items.why: On a failure, the warning display shows the messages, the master warning or master caution lights, an aural sounds, the system display shows the affected system page, and the clear pushbutton lights up.

brief_line.t: When a failure is detected. MESSAGES, MASTER LIGHT, AURAL, SYSTEM PAGE, CLEAR LIT. On a failure, the warning display shows the messages, the master warning or master caution lights, an aural sounds, the system display shows the affected system page, and the clear pushbutton lights up.

items.subflow: The Read-and-Do Sequence — Clearing the ECAM

sop_intro.t: How the E-CAM walks you through a failure and how the clear pushbutton steps you back to normal.

items.why: After completing the remedial actions, press the clear pushbutton repeatedly until the displays return to normal: memos on the warning display, the flight-phase system page on the system display, and the clear light off.

brief_line.t: After the actions are done. PRESS CLEAR REPEATEDLY UNTIL NORMAL. After completing the remedial actions, press the clear pushbutton repeatedly until the displays return to normal: memos on the warning display, the flight-phase system page on the system display, and the clear light off.

items.subflow: The Read-and-Do Sequence — Clearing the ECAM

sop_intro.t: How the E-CAM walks you through a failure and how the clear pushbutton steps you back to normal.

items.item: Worked example — a blue hydraulic overheat
items.action: DETECT, DO, THEN CLEAR STEP BY STEP
items.why: Single chime, master caution, the hydraulic page comes up with overheat in amber, and the instruction blue electric pump off. You switch the pump off. Clearing the warning display once brings up the flight-control secondary failure; clearing again brings up the status page with the procedures to finish the flight; clearing a third time leaves a status reminder and the flight-phase page.

brief_line.t: Worked example — a blue hydraulic overheat. DETECT, DO, THEN CLEAR STEP BY STEP. Single chime, master caution, the hydraulic page comes up with overheat in amber, and the instruction blue electric pump off. You switch the pump off. Clearing the warning display once brings up the flight-control secondary failure; clearing again brings up the status page with the procedures to finish the flight; clearing a third time leaves a status reminder and the flight-phase page.

flows.name: Engine Fire (ENG 1(2) FIRE)

sop_intro.t: Engine Fire. A red warning with the continuous repetitive chime. Fly the aircraft first, then secure the engine and fight the fire. The actions are on guarded controls, so confirm the correct engine before every action. In flight it is land as soon as possible; on the ground it ends in an evacuation. We will drill both action sequences.

flows.source: FCOM Rev 2018-08-13 § PRO-ABN-ENG — ENG 1(2) FIRE (In Flight) p.2346-2347, ENG 1(2) FIRE (On Ground) p.2348-2349, ENG 1(2) SHUT DOWN p.2382; guarded-control crosscheck FCTM § AOP-20

sop_intro.t: Recognise it and set the priorities before touching anything.

items.action: RED WARNING — IN FLIGHT, LAND ASAP
items.why: Engine fire is a level 3 red warning, with the continuous repetitive chime and the master warning. In flight it is land as soon as possible. It triggers when fire is detected by both loops, or by one loop with the other faulty, or when both loops rupture within 5 seconds.

brief_line.t: Engine fire warning. RED WARNING — IN FLIGHT, LAND ASAP. Engine fire is a level 3 red warning, with the continuous repetitive chime and the master warning. In flight it is land as soon as possible. It triggers when fire is detected by both loops, or by one loop with the other faulty, or when both loops rupture within 5 seconds.

sop_intro.t: Recognise it and set the priorities before touching anything.

items.why: The actions differ. In flight you fly the aircraft, secure the engine and fight the fire. On the ground you stop the aircraft first, then secure it and evacuate.

brief_line.t: Two contexts. IN FLIGHT, OR ON GROUND. The actions differ. In flight you fly the aircraft, secure the engine and fight the fire. On the ground you stop the aircraft first, then secure it and evacuate.

sop_intro.t: Recognise it and set the priorities before touching anything.

items.action: CONFIRM THE CORRECT ENGINE BEFORE EACH ACTION
items.why: The engine master, the engine fire pushbutton and the agents are critical guarded controls. The pilot monitoring points to the control, the pilot flying confirms it is the correct engine, then it is operated. Acting on the live engine is catastrophic.

brief_line.t: Guarded controls. CONFIRM THE CORRECT ENGINE BEFORE EACH ACTION. The engine master, the engine fire pushbutton and the agents are critical guarded controls. The pilot monitoring points to the control, the pilot flying confirms it is the correct engine, then it is operated. Acting on the live engine is catastrophic.

items.subflow: Engine Fire In Flight — ECAM Actions

sop_intro.t: In flight. Fly first, then this sequence — drilled. Confirm the affected engine on every guarded control.

items.why: Bring the affected thrust lever to idle.

reveal.t: The answer is idle. Callout: THRUST LEVER idle.

explain.t: Bring the affected thrust lever to idle. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2347.

items.subflow: Engine Fire In Flight — ECAM Actions

sop_intro.t: In flight. Fly first, then this sequence — drilled. Confirm the affected engine on every guarded control.

items.why: Set the affected engine master off — this closes the low and high pressure fuel valves. Confirm the engine first.

reveal.t: The answer is OFF. Callout: ENGINE MASTER OFF.

explain.t: Set the affected engine master off — this closes the low and high pressure fuel valves. Confirm the engine first. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2347.

items.subflow: Engine Fire In Flight — ECAM Actions

sop_intro.t: In flight. Fly first, then this sequence — drilled. Confirm the affected engine on every guarded control.

items.why: Push the affected engine fire pushbutton. The aural warning stops, the light stays on until the fire is out, and the FADEC is no longer supplied.

reveal.t: The answer is PUSH. Callout: ENGINE FIRE PUSHBUTTON PUSH.

correct_line.t: Correct. Affected engine fire pushbutton, PUSH.

explain.t: Push the affected engine fire pushbutton. The aural warning stops, the light stays on until the fire is out, and the FADEC is no longer supplied. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2347.

items.subflow: Engine Fire In Flight — ECAM Actions

sop_intro.t: In flight. Fly first, then this sequence — drilled. Confirm the affected engine on every guarded control.

items.why: Discharge agent 1 after 10 seconds. The delay lets N1 decrease, which reduces nacelle ventilation and makes the agent more effective. The ECAM counts the 10 seconds down automatically.

reveal.t: The answer is DISCHARGE AFTER 10 SECONDS. Callout: AGENT 1 DISCHARGE.

explain.t: Discharge agent 1 after 10 seconds. The delay lets N1 decrease, which reduces nacelle ventilation and makes the agent more effective. The E-CAM counts the 10 seconds down automatically. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2347.

items.subflow: Engine Fire In Flight — ECAM Actions

sop_intro.t: In flight. Fly first, then this sequence — drilled. Confirm the affected engine on every guarded control.

items.why: Notify air traffic control of the nature of the emergency and state your intentions.

reveal.t: The answer is NOTIFY. Callout: NOTIFY ATC.

explain.t: Notify air traffic control of the nature of the emergency and state your intentions. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2347.

items.subflow: Engine Fire In Flight — ECAM Actions

sop_intro.t: In flight. Fly first, then this sequence — drilled. Confirm the affected engine on every guarded control.

items.item: If the fire warning remains after 30 seconds
items.why: If the fire warning is still on 30 seconds after the first agent, discharge the second agent.

prompt.t: If the fire warning remains after 30 seconds

reveal.t: The answer is AGENT 2 DISCHARGE. Callout: AGENT 2 DISCHARGE.

correct_line.t: Correct. If the fire warning remains after 30 seconds, AGENT 2 DISCHARGE.

explain.t: If the fire warning is still on 30 seconds after the first agent, discharge the second agent. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2347.

sop_intro.t: On the ground. Stop the aircraft first, then secure and evacuate — drilled.

items.why: Bring the thrust levers to idle. Full reverse may be used to stop the aircraft.

reveal.t: The answer is idle. Callout: THRUST LEVERS idle.

explain.t: Bring the thrust levers to idle. Full reverse may be used to stop the aircraft. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2349.

sop_intro.t: On the ground. Stop the aircraft first, then secure and evacuate — drilled.

items.item: When the aircraft is stopped — parking brake
items.why: Once stopped, set the parking brake on.

prompt.t: When the aircraft is stopped — parking brake

reveal.t: The answer is ON. Callout: PARKING BRAKE ON.

correct_line.t: Correct. When the aircraft is stopped — parking brake, ON.

explain.t: Once stopped, set the parking brake on. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2349.

sop_intro.t: On the ground. Stop the aircraft first, then secure and evacuate — drilled.

items.why: Notify air traffic control on V-H-F 1 — only V-H-F 1 is available on batteries — and state your intentions.

reveal.t: The answer is NOTIFY. Callout: NOTIFY ATC ON V-H-F 1.

explain.t: Notify air traffic control on V-H-F 1 — only V-H-F 1 is available on batteries — and state your intentions. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2349.

sop_intro.t: On the ground. Stop the aircraft first, then secure and evacuate — drilled.

items.why: Alert the cabin crew on the P-A.

reveal.t: The answer is ALERT. Callout: ALERT THE CABIN CREW.

explain.t: Alert the cabin crew on the P-A. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2349.

sop_intro.t: On the ground. Stop the aircraft first, then secure and evacuate — drilled.

items.why: Set the affected engine master off — the low and high pressure valves close.

reveal.t: The answer is OFF. Callout: ENGINE MASTER OFF.

explain.t: Set the affected engine master off — the low and high pressure valves close. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2349.

sop_intro.t: On the ground. Stop the aircraft first, then secure and evacuate — drilled.

items.why: Push the affected engine fire pushbutton.

reveal.t: The answer is PUSH. Callout: ENGINE FIRE PUSHBUTTON PUSH.

correct_line.t: Correct. Affected engine fire pushbutton, PUSH.

explain.t: Push the affected engine fire pushbutton. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2349.

sop_intro.t: On the ground. Stop the aircraft first, then secure and evacuate — drilled.

items.why: On the ground, discharge both agents.

reveal.t: The answer is DISCHARGE. Callout: AGENT 1 AND 2 DISCHARGE.

explain.t: On the ground, discharge both agents. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2349.

sop_intro.t: On the ground. Stop the aircraft first, then secure and evacuate — drilled.

items.why: Apply the emergency evacuation procedure. This is the abn_emer_evac flow.
items.cite: FCOM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2349 / PRO-ABN-MISC EMER EVAC p.2594

reveal.t: The answer is APPLY. Callout: APPLY EMERGENCY EVACUATION.

explain.t: Apply the emergency evacuation procedure. This is the abn_emer_evac flow. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2349 / PRO-ABN-MISC emergency EVAC p.2594.

items.subflow: After the Fire — Shut Down, Status, Decide

sop_intro.t: With the fire out and the engine secured, manage the rest.

items.why: Apply the engine shutdown procedure and do not attempt to restart the affected engine. If high vibration continues after shutdown, reduce airspeed and descend to a safe altitude.
items.cite: FCOM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2347 / ENG SHUT DOWN p.2382

brief_line.t: Engine shutdown. DO NOT RESTART. Apply the engine shutdown procedure and do not attempt to restart the affected engine. If high vibration continues after shutdown, reduce airspeed and descend to a safe altitude.

items.subflow: After the Fire — Shut Down, Status, Decide

sop_intro.t: With the fire out and the engine secured, manage the rest.

items.why: Read the status page. You are now single-engine — bleed, electrics and hydraulics are reconfigured, and performance is reduced. Anticipate the workload for the approach.

brief_line.t: STATUS and single-engine state. REVIEW THE CONSEQUENCES. Read the status page. You are now single-engine — bleed, electrics and hydraulics are reconfigured, and performance is reduced. Anticipate the workload for the approach.

items.subflow: After the Fire — Shut Down, Status, Decide

sop_intro.t: With the fire out and the engine secured, manage the rest.

items.why: In flight an engine fire is land as soon as possible. Choose the nearest suitable airport, recompute the single-engine landing performance, and inform air traffic control, the cabin and the passengers.
items.cite: FCOM PRO-ABN-ENG p.2347 / FCTM AOP-30 p.79

brief_line.t: The decision. LAND ASAP — ASSESS, DECIDE, INFORM. In flight an engine fire is land as soon as possible. Choose the nearest suitable airport, recompute the single-engine landing performance, and inform air traffic control, the cabin and the passengers.

sop_intro.t: Brief the one-engine-inoperative approach, then call for the approach checklist.

items.why: Brief the single-engine approach: land flap 3, the V-app, and the managed thrust. For a straight-in, delay configuration full until established on final descent; if a level-off is expected, use configuration 3.
items.cite: QRH ENG One Engine Inop Straight-in p.73 / FCTM AOP-30 p.86-88

brief_line.t: Approach configuration. ONE ENGINE INOPERATIVE — FLAP 3, V app. Brief the single-engine approach: land flap 3, the V-app, and the managed thrust. For a straight-in, delay configuration full until established on final descent; if a level-off is expected, use configuration 3.

sop_intro.t: Brief the one-engine-inoperative approach, then call for the approach checklist.

items.why: Brief the one-engine-inoperative go-around — the thrust, the pitch and the lateral control with the asymmetry, and the missed approach. Then call for the approach checklist.
items.cite: FCTM AOP-30 p.88 / QRH ENG p.72-73

brief_line.t: Go-around. BRIEF THE SINGLE-ENGINE GO-AROUND. Brief the one-engine-inoperative go-around — the thrust, the pitch and the lateral control with the asymmetry, and the missed approach. Then call for the approach checklist.

flows.name: Abnormal Management (ECAM Handling & Decision Making)

sop_intro.t: Abnormal Management. Before any switch is touched, the priority is to fly the aircraft. We will walk the whole sequence: the Golden Rules, the first response, the E-CAM handling loop, the status review, the Q-R-H, and the decision that ends in an approach briefing. The Golden Rules and the philosophy are narrated. The E-CAM loop is drilled — say the call when I give you the cue.

flows.source: FCTM Rev 2018-08-13 (AOP-20 Tasksharing, AOP-30 Management of Abnormal Operations, AOP-40 Golden Rules) + FCOM 31 ECAM + QRH Abnormal & Emergency Procedures

sop_intro.t: The Golden Rules govern every abnormal and emergency. They come before any procedure.

items.why: In this order, and with appropriate tasksharing. Fly first: recover and hold a safe flight path. Navigate: know where you are, where you should be, where you should go, and where the weather, terrain and obstacles are. Communicate last: tell air traffic control and the cabin the situation and your intentions.

brief_line.t: Priorities. FLY, NAVIGATE, COMMUNICATE. In this order, and with appropriate tasksharing. Fly first: recover and hold a safe flight path. Navigate: know where you are, where you should be, where you should go, and where the weather, terrain and obstacles are. Communicate last: tell air traffic control and the cabin the situation and your intentions.

sop_intro.t: The Golden Rules govern every abnormal and emergency. They come before any procedure.

items.why: The first priority is to maintain a safe flight path before performing any read-and-do actions. The aircraft can be flown — do not let the failure pull you away from flying it.

brief_line.t: Fly the aircraft. FIRST PRIORITY. The first priority is to maintain a safe flight path before performing any read-and-do actions. The aircraft can be flown — do not let the failure pull you away from flying it.

sop_intro.t: The Golden Rules govern every abnormal and emergency. They come before any procedure.

items.action: USE THE APPROPRIATE LEVEL AT ALL TIMES
items.why: Use whatever level of automation gives you the best capacity to manage the situation. If the aircraft does not do what you expect, drop a level — hand-fly if you have to.

brief_line.t: Level of automation. USE THE APPROPRIATE LEVEL AT ALL TIMES. Use whatever level of automation gives you the best capacity to manage the situation. If the aircraft does not do what you expect, drop a level — hand-fly if you have to.

sop_intro.t: The Golden Rules govern every abnormal and emergency. They come before any procedure.

items.why: Always know what the automatics are doing. Cross-check the Flight Mode Annunciator against your intentions throughout the abnormal.

brief_line.t: Flight Mode Annunciator. UNDERSTAND AT ALL TIMES. Always know what the automatics are doing. Cross-check the Flight Mode Annunciator against your intentions throughout the abnormal.

sop_intro.t: The Golden Rules govern every abnormal and emergency. They come before any procedure.

items.item: If it does not go as expected
items.why: If things do not go as expected, do not wait and do not try to understand a misbehaving system first. Change the level of automation or intervene to secure the flight path.

brief_line.t: If it does not go as expected. TAKE ACTION. If things do not go as expected, do not wait and do not try to understand a misbehaving system first. Change the level of automation or intervene to secure the flight path.

sop_intro.t: An E-CAM caution is not an emergency to be rushed. Stabilise, gather the facts, then act in the right order.

items.why: Four hundred feet above the ground is the compromise between stabilising the aircraft and delaying the actions. Below four hundred feet you may start only if the flight path is safe.

brief_line.t: After takeoff or go-around. DELAY READ-AND-DO UNTIL 400 FEET. Four hundred feet above the ground is the compromise between stabilising the aircraft and delaying the actions. Below four hundred feet you may start only if the flight path is safe.

sop_intro.t: An E-CAM caution is not an emergency to be rushed. Stabilise, gather the facts, then act in the right order.

items.action: MEMORY ITEMS, THEN OEB, THEN ECAM, THEN QRH
items.why: Time-critical actions are done by memory first. Then any Operations Engineering Bulletin, then the ECAM read-and-do, then the quick reference handbook. Always apply judgement — this sequence may not fit every situation.

brief_line.t: Order of procedures. MEMORY ITEMS, THEN O-E-B, THEN E-CAM, THEN Q-R-H. Time-critical actions are done by memory first. Then any Operations Engineering Bulletin, then the E-CAM read-and-do, then the quick reference handbook. Always apply judgement — this sequence may not fit every situation.

sop_intro.t: An E-CAM caution is not an emergency to be rushed. Stabilise, gather the facts, then act in the right order.

items.why: Finish a procedure unless an action tells you to apply another one, or an unexpected new failure forces you to reassess.

brief_line.t: One procedure at a time. COMPLETE IT BEFORE STARTING ANOTHER. Finish a procedure unless an action tells you to apply another one, or an unexpected new failure forces you to reassess.

sop_intro.t: An E-CAM caution is not an emergency to be rushed. Stabilise, gather the facts, then act in the right order.

items.action: ONLY WHEN STABILISED AND ORDERED BY PF
items.why: ECAM actions begin only once the trajectory is stabilised and the pilot flying has called for ECAM actions.

brief_line.t: Start E-CAM actions. ONLY WHEN STABILISED AND ORDERED BY PF. E-CAM actions begin only once the trajectory is stabilised and the pilot flying has called for E-CAM actions.

sop_intro.t: This is the E-CAM tasksharing loop — drilled. The pilot flying flies and orders; the pilot monitoring reads and does. I give you the cue, you say the call.

items.item: A master warning or caution illuminates
items.callout: Press MASTER WARN or MASTER CAUT
items.why: The first pilot who notices presses the master warning or master caution to cancel the aural alert, so you can work the failure.

prompt.t: A master warning or caution illuminates

reveal.t: The answer is RESET. Callout: Press MASTER WARN or MASTER CAUT.

correct_line.t: Correct. A master warning or caution illuminates, RESET.

explain.t: The first pilot who notices presses the master warning or master caution to cancel the aural alert, so you can work the failure. Source: F-C-T-M AOP-30 p.76.

sop_intro.t: This is the E-CAM tasksharing loop — drilled. The pilot flying flies and orders; the pilot monitoring reads and does. I give you the cue, you say the call.

items.item: Failure identified and the aircraft is stabilised
items.why: Once the flight path is stable and you have considered any Operations Engineering Bulletin, you order ECAM actions. When several failures are displayed, you call ECAM actions for the first one only.

prompt.t: Failure identified and the aircraft is stabilised

reveal.t: The answer is E-CAM ACTIONS. Callout: E-CAM ACTIONS.

correct_line.t: Correct. Failure identified and the aircraft is stabilised, E-CAM ACTIONS.

explain.t: Once the flight path is stable and you have considered any Operations Engineering Bulletin, you order E-CAM actions. When several failures are displayed, you call E-CAM actions for the first one only. Source: F-C-T-M AOP-30 p.77.

sop_intro.t: This is the E-CAM tasksharing loop — drilled. The pilot flying flies and orders; the pilot monitoring reads and does. I give you the cue, you say the call.

items.item: PM points to an engine master, an IR selector, a guarded control or a circuit breaker
items.why: Before the pilot monitoring acts on any guarded or critical control, the pilot monitoring points to it and you verify the correct control and confirm. This crosscheck prevents action on the wrong system.

prompt.t: PM points to an engine master, an IR selector, a guarded control or a circuit breaker

reveal.t: The answer is CONFIRM. Callout: CONFIRM (the control).

correct_line.t: Correct. PM points to an engine master, an IR selector, a guarded control or a circuit breaker, CONFIRM.

explain.t: Before the pilot monitoring acts on any guarded or critical control, the pilot monitoring points to it and you verify the correct control and confirm. This crosscheck prevents action on the wrong system. Source: F-C-T-M AOP-20 p.73.

sop_intro.t: This is the E-CAM tasksharing loop — drilled. The pilot flying flies and orders; the pilot monitoring reads and does. I give you the cue, you say the call.

items.item: PM has performed the actions and asks to clear the system
items.why: You first check that all the actions are performed, then confirm clear with the system name. Only then does the pilot monitoring press the clear pushbutton.

prompt.t: PM has performed the actions and asks to clear the system

reveal.t: The answer is CLEAR. Callout: CLEAR (system name).

correct_line.t: Correct. PM has performed the actions and asks to clear the system, CLEAR.

explain.t: You first check that all the actions are performed, then confirm clear with the system name. Only then does the pilot monitoring press the clear pushbutton. Source: F-C-T-M AOP-30 p.77.

sop_intro.t: This is the E-CAM tasksharing loop — drilled. The pilot flying flies and orders; the pilot monitoring reads and does. I give you the cue, you say the call.

items.item: PM announces status, but a checklist, a config change, a baro set or an ATC call is pending
items.why: Before the status page you stop the ECAM to handle anything that needs both pilots — a normal checklist, a configuration change, a barometric setting, or a call to air traffic control.

prompt.t: PM announces status, but a checklist, a config change, a baro set or an ATC call is pending

reveal.t: The answer is STOP E-CAM. Callout: STOP E-CAM.

correct_line.t: Correct. PM announces status, but a checklist, a config change, a baro set or an ATC call is pending, STOP E-CAM.

explain.t: Before the status page you stop the E-CAM to handle anything that needs both pilots — a normal checklist, a configuration change, a barometric setting, or a call to air traffic control. Source: F-C-T-M AOP-30 p.77-78.

sop_intro.t: This is the E-CAM tasksharing loop — drilled. The pilot flying flies and orders; the pilot monitoring reads and does. I give you the cue, you say the call.

items.why: After the interruption is dealt with, order continue ECAM to resume the actions.

reveal.t: The answer is CONTINUE E-CAM. Callout: CONTINUE E-CAM.

correct_line.t: Correct. The pending action is handled, CONTINUE E-CAM.

explain.t: After the interruption is dealt with, order continue E-CAM to resume the actions. Source: F-C-T-M AOP-30 p.77.

sop_intro.t: This is the E-CAM tasksharing loop — drilled. The pilot flying flies and orders; the pilot monitoring reads and does. I give you the cue, you say the call.

items.item: PM has read the whole status page and you are ready
items.why: When the status review is complete you confirm remove status; the pilot monitoring presses the status pushbutton and then announces ECAM actions completed.

prompt.t: PM has read the whole status page and you are ready

reveal.t: The answer is REMOVE STATUS. Callout: REMOVE STATUS.

correct_line.t: Correct. PM has read the whole status page and you are ready, REMOVE STATUS.

explain.t: When the status review is complete you confirm remove status; the pilot monitoring presses the status pushbutton and then announces E-CAM actions completed. Source: F-C-T-M AOP-30 p.77.

sop_intro.t: The status page is your technical summary of the aircraft. Read all of it.

items.why: Status gives an overview of the technical state of the aircraft in all flight phases. Read the whole page so you assess the situation correctly before you decide.

brief_line.t: The whole status page. READ IT ALL. Status gives an overview of the technical state of the aircraft in all flight phases. Read the whole page so you assess the situation correctly before you decide.

sop_intro.t: The status page is your technical summary of the aircraft. Read all of it.

items.why: Read the procedures linked to the status page so you can anticipate and spread the workload across each flight phase.

brief_line.t: The associated procedures. READ TO ANTICIPATE WORKLOAD. Read the procedures linked to the status page so you can anticipate and spread the workload across each flight phase.

sop_intro.t: The status page is your technical summary of the aircraft. Read all of it.

items.action: FROM THE QRH, NEVER FROM MEMORY
items.why: If a reset is needed, never do it from memory — refer to the quick reference handbook. If the reset is successful, the status page disappears.

brief_line.t: A system reset. FROM THE Q-R-H, NEVER FROM MEMORY. If a reset is needed, never do it from memory — refer to the quick reference handbook. If the reset is successful, the status page disappears.

sop_intro.t: The quick reference handbook carries the paper procedures, the performance section, and the summaries for the big failures.

items.action: USE THE ABNORMAL AND EMERGENCY TABLE OF CONTENTS
items.why: The pilot monitoring uses the abnormal and emergency procedures table of contents to search and select the applicable procedure.

brief_line.t: To find a Q-R-H procedure. USE THE ABNORMAL AND EMERGENCY TABLE OF CONTENTS. The pilot monitoring uses the abnormal and emergency procedures table of contents to search and select the applicable procedure.

sop_intro.t: The quick reference handbook carries the paper procedures, the performance section, and the summaries for the big failures.

items.item: When an ECAM procedure requests a QRH procedure
items.action: KEEP ECAM DISPLAYED, APPLY THE QRH
items.why: Leave the procedure displayed on the ECAM and apply the requested handbook procedure, so lower-priority ECAM alerts do not disturb you.

brief_line.t: When an E-CAM procedure requests a Q-R-H procedure. KEEP E-CAM DISPLAYED, APPLY THE Q-R-H. Leave the procedure displayed on the E-CAM and apply the requested handbook procedure, so lower-priority E-CAM alerts do not disturb you.

sop_intro.t: The quick reference handbook carries the paper procedures, the performance section, and the summaries for the big failures.

items.item: If the conditions no longer apply
items.why: You can stop any abnormal handbook procedure if the conditions for applying it disappear.

brief_line.t: If the conditions no longer apply. YOU MAY STOP THE PROCEDURE. You can stop any abnormal handbook procedure if the conditions for applying it disappear.

sop_intro.t: The quick reference handbook carries the paper procedures, the performance section, and the summaries for the big failures.

items.item: An electrical emergency configuration or a dual hydraulic failure
items.why: These summaries split the actions into cruise, approach, landing and go-around. Apply the ECAM first — the procedure and the status page — then work the matching summary section, which links to the performance pages for fuel and landing distance.

brief_line.t: An electrical emergency configuration or a dual hydraulic failure. USE THE Q-R-H SUMMARIES. These summaries split the actions into cruise, approach, landing and go-around. Apply the E-CAM first — the procedure and the status page — then work the matching summary section, which links to the performance pages for fuel and landing distance.

items.subflow: After the Procedure — Decide and Brief

sop_intro.t: Once the E-CAM, bulletin and handbook actions are done, the management becomes a structured decision that ends in an approach briefing.

items.why: With the actions complete, go back to the normal operations tasksharing rules.

brief_line.t: Tasksharing. RESUME NORMAL OPERATIONS TASKSHARING. With the actions complete, go back to the normal operations tasksharing rules.

items.subflow: After the Procedure — Decide and Brief

sop_intro.t: Once the E-CAM, bulletin and handbook actions are done, the management becomes a structured decision that ends in an approach briefing.

items.why: Assess the situation, make the decision, then inform air traffic control, the cabin crew, the passengers and operations as required. There is no acronym — it is assess, decide, inform.

brief_line.t: The decision. ASSESS, DECIDE, INFORM. Assess the situation, make the decision, then inform air traffic control, the cabin crew, the passengers and operations as required. There is no acronym — it is assess, decide, inform.

items.subflow: After the Procedure — Decide and Brief

sop_intro.t: Once the E-CAM, bulletin and handbook actions are done, the management becomes a structured decision that ends in an approach briefing.

items.action: LAND AS SOON AS POSSIBLE — RED OR AMBER
items.why: A red land-as-soon-as-possible means land as soon as possible at the nearest airport where a safe landing can be made. An amber means consider landing at the nearest suitable airport.

brief_line.t: Where to land. LAND AS SOON AS POSSIBLE — RED OR AMBER. A red land-as-soon-as-possible means land as soon as possible at the nearest airport where a safe landing can be made. An amber means consider landing at the nearest suitable airport.

items.subflow: After the Procedure — Decide and Brief

sop_intro.t: Once the E-CAM, bulletin and handbook actions are done, the management becomes a structured decision that ends in an approach briefing.

items.why: From the status page, check any fuel penalty and the fuel remaining at destination or diversion, and check any landing-distance penalty and compute the landing distance at the airport you choose.

brief_line.t: Performance and fuel. RECOMPUTE FROM STATUS. From the status page, check any fuel penalty and the fuel remaining at destination or diversion, and check any landing-distance penalty and compute the landing distance at the airport you choose.

items.subflow: After the Procedure — Decide and Brief

sop_intro.t: Once the E-CAM, bulletin and handbook actions are done, the management becomes a structured decision that ends in an approach briefing.

items.why: Build the approach and landing briefing around the failure. For the big failures use the approach, landing and go-around sections of the handbook summary, crosschecked against the flight management pages and the status page.

brief_line.t: Approach and landing. BRIEF FOR THE FAILED CONFIGURATION. Build the approach and landing briefing around the failure. For the big failures use the approach, landing and go-around sections of the handbook summary, crosschecked against the flight management pages and the status page.

sop_intro.t: This is the E-CAM tasksharing loop — drilled. The pilot flying flies and orders; the pilot monitoring reads and does. I give you the cue, you say the call.

items.item: The master warning has been silenced
items.why: The pilot monitoring reads the title of the failure on the warning display and announces it.

prompt.t: The master warning has been silenced

reveal.t: The answer is ANNOUNCE TITLE. Callout: (title of the failure).

correct_line.t: Correct. The master warning has been silenced, ANNOUNCE TITLE.

explain.t: The pilot monitoring reads the title of the failure on the warning display and announces it. Source: F-C-T-M AOP-30 p.76.

sop_intro.t: This is the E-CAM tasksharing loop — drilled. The pilot flying flies and orders; the pilot monitoring reads and does. I give you the cue, you say the call.

items.item: Before taking any action on the failure
items.why: The pilot monitoring checks the overhead panel and the system display to confirm the failure is real before taking any action.

prompt.t: Before taking any action on the failure

correct_line.t: Correct. Before taking any action on the failure, CONFIRM.

explain.t: The pilot monitoring checks the overhead panel and the system display to confirm the failure is real before taking any action. Source: F-C-T-M AOP-30 p.77.

sop_intro.t: This is the E-CAM tasksharing loop — drilled. The pilot flying flies and orders; the pilot monitoring reads and does. I give you the cue, you say the call.

items.item: The PF has ordered ECAM actions
items.why: The pilot monitoring reads and does the action lines on the warning display, out loud.

prompt.t: The PF has ordered E-CAM actions

correct_line.t: Correct. The PF has ordered E-CAM actions, PERFORM.

explain.t: The pilot monitoring reads and does the action lines on the warning display, out loud. Source: F-C-T-M AOP-30 p.76-77.

sop_intro.t: This is the E-CAM tasksharing loop — drilled. The pilot flying flies and orders; the pilot monitoring reads and does. I give you the cue, you say the call.

items.why: On the overhead the pilot monitoring calls the system, then the control, then the action — for example, air, crossbleed, close — then checks the system display to verify it happened.

reveal.t: The answer is ANNOUNCE SYSTEM, CONTROL, ACTION. Callout: for example, AIR, X-BLEED, CLOSE.

correct_line.t: Correct. Acting on the overhead panel, ANNOUNCE SYSTEM, CONTROL, ACTION.

explain.t: On the overhead the pilot monitoring calls the system, then the control, then the action — for example, air, crossbleed, close — then checks the system display to verify it happened. Source: F-C-T-M AOP-20 p.74.

sop_intro.t: This is the E-CAM tasksharing loop — drilled. The pilot flying flies and orders; the pilot monitoring reads and does. I give you the cue, you say the call.

items.item: All actions on this system are done
items.why: The pilot monitoring requests clear with the system name; after the pilot flying confirms, the pilot monitoring presses the clear pushbutton.

prompt.t: All actions on this system are done

reveal.t: The answer is REQUEST CLEAR. Callout: CLEAR (system name)?.

correct_line.t: Correct. All actions on this system are done, REQUEST CLEAR.

explain.t: The pilot monitoring requests clear with the system name; after the pilot flying confirms, the pilot monitoring presses the clear pushbutton. Source: F-C-T-M AOP-30 p.77.

sop_intro.t: This is the E-CAM tasksharing loop — drilled. The pilot flying flies and orders; the pilot monitoring reads and does. I give you the cue, you say the call.

items.item: Each system display page in turn
items.why: The pilot monitoring analyses each system display page for the operational impact before it is cleared.

prompt.t: Each system display page in turn

correct_line.t: Correct. Each system display page in turn, ANALYZE.

explain.t: The pilot monitoring analyses each system display page for the operational impact before it is cleared. Source: F-C-T-M AOP-30 p.77.

sop_intro.t: This is the E-CAM tasksharing loop — drilled. The pilot flying flies and orders; the pilot monitoring reads and does. I give you the cue, you say the call.

items.item: Ready to move to the status page
items.why: The pilot monitoring announces status; the pilot flying orders stop ECAM if anything is pending, then continue ECAM.

prompt.t: Ready to move to the status page

reveal.t: The answer is ANNOUNCE STATUS. Callout: STATUS.

correct_line.t: Correct. Ready to move to the status page, ANNOUNCE STATUS.

explain.t: The pilot monitoring announces status; the pilot flying orders stop E-CAM if anything is pending, then continue E-CAM. Source: F-C-T-M AOP-30 p.77.

sop_intro.t: This is the E-CAM tasksharing loop — drilled. The pilot flying flies and orders; the pilot monitoring reads and does. I give you the cue, you say the call.

items.why: The pilot monitoring reads the whole status page, including the associated procedures, to assess the workload for each flight phase.

correct_line.t: Correct. The status page is displayed, READ STATUS.

explain.t: The pilot monitoring reads the whole status page, including the associated procedures, to assess the workload for each flight phase. Source: F-C-T-M AOP-30 p.76-78.

sop_intro.t: This is the E-CAM tasksharing loop — drilled. The pilot flying flies and orders; the pilot monitoring reads and does. I give you the cue, you say the call.

items.why: The pilot monitoring requests remove status; after the pilot flying confirms, presses the status pushbutton, then announces ECAM actions completed.

reveal.t: The answer is REQUEST REMOVE STATUS. Callout: REMOVE STATUS?.

correct_line.t: Correct. The status review is complete, REQUEST REMOVE STATUS.

explain.t: The pilot monitoring requests remove status; after the pilot flying confirms, presses the status pushbutton, then announces E-CAM actions completed. Source: F-C-T-M AOP-30 p.77.

sop_intro.t: The After Landing flow cleans up the aircraft once it is clear of the high-energy landing/rollout regime: disarm ground spoilers, reconfigure exterior lights for taxi, switch the weather radar and predictive-windshear (and both DISPLAY mode selectors) OFF — the F-COM's stated reason being to avoid radiating persons at the gate area — return engine mode to normal, retract flaps, set TCAS to STBY and ATC/transponder per airport requirements, start the APU, set anti-ice as required, and check brake temperature. The F-COM lists these as discrete idented items under PRO-NOR-S-O-P-21 (F-COM Rev 2018-08-13 § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-21, p. 2883-2885), with the After Landing Checklist completed at the end once the aircraft has cleared the runway. The deck shows the flow seat-split: PF disarms ground spoilers and sets the exterior lights then signals the PM, who works the remaining overhead/pedestal items (deck p286-287).

sop_intro.t: F-COM S-O-P-18-B Aircraft Configuration Management is a decelerated-approach schedule: the PF orders each configuration step and the PM selects it, with deceleration toward the next reference speed checked before each selection. AT green dot SPEED the PF orders FLAPS 1 ("FLAPS 1 should be selected more than 3 NM before the Final Descent Point") checking deceleration toward "S" speed; AT 2 000 feet AGL MINIMUM FLAPS 2 checking deceleration toward "F" speed; WHEN FLAPS ARE AT 2 the landing gear is ordered DOWN with AUTO BRK CONFIRM / ground spoilers ARM / EXTERIOR LIGHTS set; WHEN LANDING GEAR IS DOWN FLAPS 3 (E-CAM WHEEL SD page CHECK) then FLAPS FULL checking deceleration towards V app, then auto-thrust CHECK IN SPEED MODE OR OFF. The F-COM stabilization criteria require the aircraft to be "in the desired landing configuration" and "at target speed for approach" before or at the stabilization height. For decelerated approaches, "the aircraft must reach or be established on the final descent with FLAPS 1 and 'S' speed at or above 2 000 feet AGL" (F-COM Rev 2018-08-13 § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-18-B, p. 3/6).

correct_line.t: Correct. config 1 (FLAPS 1) - at green dot speed, FLAPS 1.

sop_intro.t: F-COM S-O-P-18-B Aircraft Configuration Management is a decelerated-approach schedule: the PF orders each configuration step and the PM selects it, with deceleration toward the next reference speed checked before each selection. AT green dot SPEED the PF orders FLAPS 1 ("FLAPS 1 should be selected more than 3 NM before the Final Descent Point") checking deceleration toward "S" speed; AT 2 000 feet AGL MINIMUM FLAPS 2 checking deceleration toward "F" speed; WHEN FLAPS ARE AT 2 the landing gear is ordered DOWN with AUTO BRK CONFIRM / ground spoilers ARM / EXTERIOR LIGHTS set; WHEN LANDING GEAR IS DOWN FLAPS 3 (E-CAM WHEEL SD page CHECK) then FLAPS FULL checking deceleration towards V app, then auto-thrust CHECK IN SPEED MODE OR OFF. The F-COM stabilization criteria require the aircraft to be "in the desired landing configuration" and "at target speed for approach" before or at the stabilization height. For decelerated approaches, "the aircraft must reach or be established on the final descent with FLAPS 1 and 'S' speed at or above 2 000 feet AGL" (F-COM Rev 2018-08-13 § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-18-B, p. 3/6).

sop_intro.t: The LANDING checklist is the final normal checklist before touchdown. It confirms the missed-approach (go-around) altitude is set in the FCU, the cabin crew has been advised, and the aircraft is in landing configuration — the E/WD LDG MEMO showing no blue (gear down, signs on, cabin ready, ground spoilers armed, flaps set) — before crossing the stabilization height.

AT green dot SPEED the PF orders FLAPS 1 ("FLAPS 1 should be selected more than 3 NM before the Final Descent Point") checking deceleration toward "S" speed; AT 2 000 feet AGL MINIMUM FLAPS 2 checking deceleration toward "F" speed; WHEN FLAPS ARE AT 2 the landing gear is ordered DOWN with AUTO BRK CONFIRM / ground spoilers ARM / EXTERIOR LIGHTS set; WHEN LANDING GEAR IS DOWN FLAPS 3 (E-CAM WHEEL SD page CHECK) then FLAPS FULL checking deceleration towards V app, then auto-thrust CHECK IN SPEED MODE OR OFF. The F-COM stabilization criteria require the aircraft to be "in the desired landing configuration" and "at target speed for approach" before or at the stabilization height. For decelerated approaches, "the aircraft must reach or be established on the final descent with FLAPS 1 and 'S' speed at or above 2 000 feet AGL" (F-COM Rev 2018-08-13 § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-18-B, p. 3/6).

For decelerated approaches, "the aircraft must reach or be established on the final descent with FLAPS 1 and 'S' speed at or above 2 000 feet AGL" (F-COM Rev 2018-08-13 § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-18-B, p. 3/6).

sop_intro.t: The LANDING checklist is the final normal checklist before touchdown. It confirms the missed-approach (go-around) altitude is set in the FCU, the cabin crew has been advised, and the aircraft is in landing configuration — the E/WD LDG MEMO showing no blue (gear down, signs on, cabin ready, ground spoilers armed, flaps set) — before crossing the stabilization height.
