flows.name: Approach Types & Modes (2D / 3D)
flows.source: A320 Tutorials with UPRT - Flow Patterns - Cockpit Panels (January 2024)

sop_intro.t: Approach types, and how to brief them. Every instrument approach is either a 2D or a 3D approach. A 2D approach gives lateral guidance only - you fly the vertical path yourself with a selected flight path angle. A 3D approach gives both lateral and vertical guidance. In the arrival briefing, brief the approach type, the lateral and vertical modes you will use and the FMA you expect, the minima - decision altitude or height for a 3D, minimum descent altitude or height for a 2D - the final descent point, the go-around altitude and trajectory, and the stabilization gates.

items.action: ONE THOUSAND FEET IMC, FIVE HUNDRED FEET VMC
items.why: If you are not stabilized - in the landing configuration, at target speed, on the lateral and vertical path - by one thousand feet in IMC, or five hundred feet in VMC, go around.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck, p.239 (final approach gates)

brief_line.t: Stabilization gates. ONE THOUSAND FEET I-M-C, FIVE HUNDRED FEET V-M-C. If you are not stabilized - in the landing configuration, at target speed, on the lateral and vertical path - by one thousand feet in I-M-C, or five hundred feet in V-M-C, go around.

sop_intro.t: The 2D, or non-precision, approach. The navaid or the FMS gives lateral guidance only; there is no vertical beam, so you fly the descent path with a selected flight path angle. Localizer-only, V-O-R, and N-D-B approaches flown without vertical guidance are 2D. Aim to be stabilized at the final descent point, at approach speed, in the landing configuration - entering the approach speed as a speed constraint at the final descent point on the flight-plan page helps achieve that.

items.action: NAV, LOC, or selected TRACK or HEADING
items.why: Lateral guidance comes from managed NAV following the flight plan, from LOC for a localizer approach, or from selected track or heading. About one mile before the final descent point set the lateral mode for the approach; for a LOC approach press the LOC pushbutton and expect the call TRACK, LOC BLUE.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck, p.228, p.235

brief_line.t: 2D lateral mode. NAV, LOC, or selected TRACK or HEADING. Lateral guidance comes from managed NAV following the flight plan, from LOC for a localizer approach, or from selected track or heading. About one mile before the final descent point set the lateral mode for the approach; for a LOC approach press the LOC pushbutton and expect the call TRACK, LOC BLUE.

sop_intro.t: The 2D, or non-precision, approach. The navaid or the FMS gives lateral guidance only; there is no vertical beam, so you fly the descent path with a selected flight path angle. Localizer-only, V-O-R, and N-D-B approaches flown without vertical guidance are 2D. Aim to be stabilized at the final descent point, at approach speed, in the landing configuration - entering the approach speed as a speed constraint at the final descent point on the flight-plan page helps achieve that.

items.action: FPA - preset, then PULL at the final descent point
items.why: There is no vertical guidance, so preset the flight path angle for the final segment and pull it at the final descent point. A typical call is FPA minus three point one degrees. Then monitor position and flight path against the published profile.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck, p.236, p.239

brief_line.t: 2D vertical mode. FPA - preset, then PULL at the final descent point. There is no vertical guidance, so preset the flight path angle for the final segment and pull it at the final descent point. A typical call is FPA minus three point one degrees. Then monitor position and flight path against the published profile.

sop_intro.t: The 2D, or non-precision, approach. The navaid or the FMS gives lateral guidance only; there is no vertical beam, so you fly the descent path with a selected flight path angle. Localizer-only, V-O-R, and N-D-B approaches flown without vertical guidance are 2D. Aim to be stabilized at the final descent point, at approach speed, in the landing configuration - entering the approach speed as a speed constraint at the final descent point on the flight-plan page helps achieve that.

items.action: NAV sequences automatically; TRACK or HEADING is manual
items.why: In NAV mode the flight plan sequences automatically - the TO waypoint shows on the upper right of the ND. In track or heading the sequencing is not automatic, so clear the remaining flight-plan lines manually, or insert a DIR TO with the radial-in opposite the final course at the final descent point. After a go-around, NAV mode is only available if the flight plan was sequenced.

brief_line.t: Flight-plan sequencing. NAV sequences automatically; TRACK or HEADING is manual. In NAV mode the flight plan sequences automatically - the TO waypoint shows on the upper right of the ND. In track or heading the sequencing is not automatic, so clear the remaining flight-plan lines manually, or insert a DIR TO with the radial-in opposite the final course at the final descent point. After a go-around, NAV mode is only available if the flight plan was sequenced.

sop_intro.t: The 2D, or non-precision, approach. The navaid or the FMS gives lateral guidance only; there is no vertical beam, so you fly the descent path with a selected flight path angle. Localizer-only, V-O-R, and N-D-B approaches flown without vertical guidance are 2D. Aim to be stabilized at the final descent point, at approach speed, in the landing configuration - entering the approach speed as a speed constraint at the final descent point on the flight-plan page helps achieve that.

items.action: automatic at the DECEL point in managed modes; manual on the MCDU in selected modes
items.why: Do not confuse arming the approach with activating the approach phase. In managed modes - NAV, LOC, LOC star - the approach phase activates automatically when overflying the magenta DECEL point. In selected modes - track, heading - the DECEL point is inoperative and shown white, so activate the approach phase manually from the MCDU.

brief_line.t: Approach phase activation. automatic at the DECEL point in managed modes; manual on the MCDU in selected modes. Do not confuse arming the approach with activating the approach phase. In managed modes - NAV, LOC, LOC star - the approach phase activates automatically when overflying the magenta DECEL point. In selected modes - track, heading - the DECEL point is inoperative and shown white, so activate the approach phase manually from the MCDU.

sop_intro.t: The 3D approach gives both lateral and vertical guidance, and is armed with the APPR pushbutton on the FCU. Which guidance you get depends on the approach type in the FMS database. An I-L-S gives a glide slope and a localizer. Every other approach flown with FMS vertical guidance - R-nav with GNSS, V-O-R, N-D-B, and R-nav with required navigation performance authorization required - gives FINAL APP.

items.action: arm APPR - FMA shows GLIDE SLOPE and LOC
items.why: When cleared, on the intercept trajectory with localizer deviation available, press the APPR pushbutton and engage the second autopilot. Expect GLIDE SLOPE BLUE, LOC BLUE, CAT 3 DUAL, AP 1 plus 2. Watch the capture sequence - LOC star then LOC, glide slope star then glide slope - and crosscheck raw data to guard against capturing a false beam. On the A320 the second autopilot and CAT 3 require two thousand feet above ground level as a minimum.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck, p.245 (summary table), p.254

brief_line.t: I-L-S approach. arm APPR - FMA shows GLIDE SLOPE and LOC. When cleared, on the intercept trajectory with localizer deviation available, press the APPR pushbutton and engage the second autopilot. Expect GLIDE SLOPE BLUE, LOC BLUE, CAT 3 DUAL, A-P one plus 2. Watch the capture sequence - LOC star then LOC, glide slope star then glide slope - and crosscheck raw data to guard against capturing a false beam. On the A320 the second autopilot and CAT 3 require two thousand feet above ground level as a minimum.

sop_intro.t: The 3D approach gives both lateral and vertical guidance, and is armed with the APPR pushbutton on the FCU. Which guidance you get depends on the approach type in the FMS database. An I-L-S gives a glide slope and a localizer. Every other approach flown with FMS vertical guidance - R-nav with GNSS, V-O-R, N-D-B, and R-nav with required navigation performance authorization required - gives FINAL APP.

items.action: arm APPR - FMA shows FINAL APP
items.why: Press the APPR pushbutton; check APP NAV armed or engaged and FINAL armed - expect the call FINAL BLUE, APP NAV. These approaches have no autoland: by the minimum, autopilot off, flight directors off, bird on, and check or set the runway track. If the autopilot is still engaged at the minimum minus fifty feet, the message DISCONNECT AP FOR LDG pulses on the FMA.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck, p.245 (summary table), p.258, p.262

brief_line.t: R-nav, V-O-R, or N-D-B approach. arm APPR - FMA shows FINAL APP. Press the APPR pushbutton; check APP NAV armed or engaged and FINAL armed - expect the call FINAL BLUE, APP NAV. These approaches have no autoland: by the minimum, autopilot off, flight directors off, bird on, and check or set the runway track. If the autopilot is still engaged at the minimum minus fifty feet, the message DISCONNECT AP FOR LDG pulses on the FMA.

flows.name: Before Pushback/Start - Engine Start - After Start
flows.source: A320 Tutorials with UPRT - Flow Patterns - Cockpit Panels (January 2024)

sop_intro.t: Final loadsheet, weights and fuel verified; seats and MCDU pages set; external power dropped.

items.why: Both pilots check the final Load and Trim Sheet for gross errors and confirm the data is correct, because the loadsheet drives weight, CG and performance.

explain.t: Both pilots check the final Load and Trim Sheet for gross errors and confirm the data is correct, because the loadsheet drives weight, CG and performance. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-07, p. 1/6.

sop_intro.t: Final loadsheet, weights and fuel verified; seats and MCDU pages set; external power dropped.

items.why: PF compares the final ZFW and ZFWCG against the previously entered data and adjusts if needed, and the PM crosschecks the FMS entry against LTS limits, so the takeoff CG stays inside operational limits.

prompt.t: Zero Fuel Weight and Zero Fuel Weight Center of Gravity

correct_line.t: Correct. Zero Fuel Weight and Zero Fuel Weight Center of Gravity, CHECK.

explain.t: PF compares the final zero fuel weight and zero fuel weight C-G against the previously entered data and adjusts if needed, and the PM crosschecks the FMS entry against LTS limits, so the takeoff CG stays inside operational limits. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-07, p. 1/6.

sop_intro.t: Final loadsheet, weights and fuel verified; seats and MCDU pages set; external power dropped.

items.why: Confirm ECAM fuel matches the flight plan and imbalance is within limits, and crosscheck last-flight fuel plus uplift against current FOB, because an abnormal discrepancy means a maintenance action is due.

explain.t: Confirm E-CAM fuel matches the flight plan and imbalance is within limits, and crosscheck last-flight fuel plus uplift against current FOB, because an abnormal discrepancy means a maintenance action is due. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-07, p. 1/6.

sop_intro.t: Final loadsheet, weights and fuel verified; seats and MCDU pages set; external power dropped.

condition.t: Condition: If takeoff conditions changed

items.why: PF and PM independently recompute the takeoff performance whenever takeoff conditions change, such as a different final loadsheet or a change affecting the computation like runway condition, intersection or runway change.
items.cite: FCOM § PRO-NOR-SOP-07, p. 1/6; FCTM § PR-NP-SOP-70, p. 1/2

correct_line.t: Correct. Final Takeoff Performance Data, RECOMPUTE.

explain.t: PF and PM independently recompute the takeoff performance whenever takeoff conditions change, such as a different final loadsheet or a change affecting the computation like runway condition, intersection or runway change. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-07, p. 1/6; F-C-T-M § PR-NP-S-O-P-70, p. 1/2.

sop_intro.t: Final loadsheet, weights and fuel verified; seats and MCDU pages set; external power dropped.

condition.t: Condition: If takeoff conditions changed

items.why: After the new double computation the PF enters the revised takeoff data in the FMS, paying particular attention to any slat/flap configuration change if the optimum configuration option was used.

explain.t: After the new double computation the PF enters the revised takeoff data in the FMS, paying particular attention to any slat/flap configuration change if the optimum configuration option was used. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-07, p. 2/6.

sop_intro.t: Final loadsheet, weights and fuel verified; seats and MCDU pages set; external power dropped.

items.why: Adjust the seat until your eyes align with the red and white balls, because sitting too low increases the cockpit cut-off angle and reduces the visual segment, which matters most for acquiring visual reference during low visibility approaches.
items.cite: FCOM § PRO-NOR-SOP-07, p. 2/6; FCTM § PR-NP-SOP-70, p. 1/2

explain.t: Adjust the seat until your eyes align with the red and white balls, because sitting too low increases the cockpit cut-off angle and reduces the visual segment, which matters most for acquiring visual reference during low visibility approaches. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-07, p. 2/6; F-C-T-M § PR-NP-S-O-P-70, p. 1/2.

sop_intro.t: Final loadsheet, weights and fuel verified; seats and MCDU pages set; external power dropped.

items.why: Display the PERF TO page on the PF side, as recommended, so the PF has the takeoff performance data in view.

correct_line.t: Correct. F-M-S Performance Takeoff page, SELECT.

explain.t: Display the performance TO page on the PF side, as recommended, so the PF has the takeoff performance data in view. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-07, p. 2/6.

items.item: Open the call to ground — ready to drop external power

prompt.t: Open the call to ground — ready to drop external power

reveal.t: The answer is GROUND FROM COCKPIT.

correct_line.t: Correct. Open the call to ground — ready to drop external power, GROUND FROM COCKPIT.

items.item: Ground mechanic: COCKPIT FROM GROUND, GO AHEAD

narration.t: Ground mechanic: COCKPIT FROM GROUND, GO AHEAD

reveal.t: The answer is REMOVE EXTERNAL POWER.

correct_line.t: Correct. Request the GPU removal, REMOVE EXTERNAL POWER.

items.why: Once on own power the crew requests pushback and start clearance; it must be obtained before the aircraft moves.

correct_line.t: Correct. Pushback and Start Clearance, REQUEST.

explain.t: Once on own power the crew requests pushback and start clearance; it must be obtained before the aircraft moves. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-07, p. 3/6.

items.why: Configuration item — no specific rationale in the book.

explain.t: It's a standard configuration setting.

items.why: You confirm the sliding window is locked with the red indicator visible, all aircraft doors show closed on the ECAM lower display, and (where required by local airworthiness authorities) the cockpit door is closed and locked before any movement.

correct_line.t: Correct. Windows and Doors, CHECK CLOSED.

explain.t: You confirm the sliding window is locked with the red indicator visible, all aircraft doors show closed on the E-CAM lower display, and (where required by local airworthiness authorities) the cockpit door is closed and locked before any movement. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-07, p. 4/6.

items.why: When the slide arming lever is ARMED, opening the door automatically inflates and deploys the slide, so confirming ARMED configures the doors for emergency evacuation.

explain.t: When the slide arming lever is ARMED, opening the door automatically inflates and deploys the slide, so confirming ARMED configures the doors for emergency evacuation. Source: F-COM § DSC-52-10-20, p. 1865.

items.why: Engines start regardless of lever position, so levers not at idle would let thrust rapidly increase to the lever position and create a hazardous situation.

explain.t: Engines start regardless of lever position, so levers not at idle would let thrust rapidly increase to the lever position and create a hazardous situation. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-07, p. 4/6.

items.why: The accumulator pressure must read in the green band so the brake accumulator is charged; if it is not, you recharge it using the yellow electric pump.

explain.t: The accumulator pressure must read in the green band so the brake accumulator is charged; if it is not, you recharge it using the yellow electric pump. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-07, p. 4/6.

items.item: You have push clearance — call ground
items.action: GROUND FROM COCKPIT, CLEARED FOR PUSH

prompt.t: You have push clearance — call ground

reveal.t: The answer is GROUND FROM COCKPIT, CLEARED FOR PUSH.

correct_line.t: Correct. You have push clearance — call ground, GROUND FROM COCKPIT, CLEARED FOR PUSH.

items.item: Ground mechanic: COCKPIT FROM GROUND, RELEASE BRAKES

narration.t: Ground mechanic: COCKPIT FROM GROUND, RELEASE BRAKES

items.why: For pushback the nosewheel steering bypass pin must be in tow position, and this ECAM memo confirms it; without it you must not start engines during pushback to avoid nose landing gear damage on yellow hydraulic pressurization.

correct_line.t: Correct. Nosewheel Steering Disconnect memo, CHECK DISPLAYED.

explain.t: For pushback the nosewheel steering bypass pin must be in takeoff weight position, and this E-CAM memo confirms it; without it you must not start engines during pushback to avoid nose landing gear damage on yellow hydraulic pressurization. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-07, p. 5/6.

reveal.t: The answer is BRAKES RELEASED, READY TO PUSH.

correct_line.t: Correct. Brakes released — tell ground, BRAKES RELEASED, READY TO PUSH.

items.why: Release the park brake so the aircraft can be pushed; do not use the brakes during pushback unless required due to an emergency.

explain.t: Release the park brake so the aircraft can be pushed; do not use the brakes during pushback unless required due to an emergency. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-07, p. 5/6.

sop_intro.t: Ground mechanic: Set brakes.

items.why: When pushback is complete, set the park brake ON, check normal brake pressure, and ask the ground crew for towbar disconnection.

explain.t: When pushback is complete, set the park brake ON, check normal brake pressure, and ask the ground crew for towbar disconnection. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-07, p. 5/6.

sop_intro.t: Ground mechanic: Set brakes.

items.why: You confirm normal brake pressure indications before relying on the brakes.

explain.t: You confirm normal brake pressure indications before relying on the brakes. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-07, p. 5/6.

sop_intro.t: Ground mechanic: Set brakes.

correct_line.t: Correct. Brakes set — tell ground, BRAKES SET.

items.why: You put the ENG MODE selector back to NORM once the engines are running to permit normal pack operation.

explain.t: You put the engine mode selector back to normal once the engines are running to permit normal pack operation. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-09, p. 3/4.

items.why: Turning APU bleed off closes the APU bleed valve and opens the engine bleed valves, and it avoids ingestion of engine exhaust gases.

explain.t: Turning APU bleed off closes the APU bleed valve and opens the engine bleed valves, and it avoids ingestion of engine exhaust gases. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-09, p. 1/4.

items.why: Engine anti-ice must be ON for all ground operations when icing conditions exist or are anticipated, and in icing you turn it on without waiting to see ice build up.

explain.t: Engine anti-ice must be ON for all ground operations when icing conditions exist or are anticipated, and in icing you turn it on without waiting to see ice build up. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-09, p. 1/4.

condition.t: Condition: If the APU is not required

items.why: Once the engines are running and the APU is no longer required, you shut it down with the master switch.

correct_line.t: Correct. A-P-U Master switch pushbutton, OFF.

explain.t: Once the engines are running and the APU is no longer required, you shut it down with the master switch. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-09, p. 2/4.

items.why: You check the STATUS reminder is not displayed; if STS shows, press the STS pb and review the ECAM Status page so you know the aircraft's current status before taxi.

explain.t: You check the STATUS reminder is not displayed; if STS shows, press the STS pushbutton and review the E-CAM Status page so you know the aircraft's current status before taxi. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-09, p. 3/4.

explain.t: If the STS reminder is displayed you press the STS pushbutton to call up and review the E-CAM Status page. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-09, p. 3/4.

items.why: The NW STRG DISC memo means the nose-wheel steering selector is in the towing (disconnect) position, so steering is inoperative; it must be back in normal before taxi.

reveal.t: The answer is CHECK NOT DISPLAYED.

correct_line.t: Correct. Nosewheel Steering Disconnect memo, CHECK NOT DISPLAYED.

explain.t: The NW STRG DISC memo means the nose-wheel steering selector is in the towing (disconnect) position, so steering is inoperative; it must be back in normal before taxi. Source: F-COM § DSC-32-20-20, p. 1362.

explain.t: PF and PM independently recompute the takeoff performance whenever takeoff conditions change, such as a different final loadsheet or a change affecting the computation like runway condition, intersection or runway change. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-07, p. 1/6; F-C-T-M § PR-NP-S-O-P-70, p. 1/2.

correct_line.t: Correct. F-M-S Revised Takeoff Performance Data, CROSSCHECK.

explain.t: You set takeoff CG on the pitch trim handwheel so the stabiliser is trimmed for the takeoff CG. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-09, p. 3/4.

explain.t: If the STS reminder is displayed you press the STS pushbutton to call up and review the E-CAM Status page. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-09, p. 3/4.

sop_intro.t: This is the ten-thousand-foot flow, run passing ten thousand feet above airfield level. Per the JAN24 deck the PF's only item is E-F-I-S OPTION as required; the PM runs the landing-light selector to OFF, seat belts as required, E-F-I-S option as required, reviews the E-CAM memo, then clears the navaids, sets the SEC F-plan as required, and checks OPT/MAX altitude.

flows.name: Cockpit Preparation

sop_intro.t: Cockpit Preparation is the systematic panel scan that brings the aircraft to a known, safe configuration before flight. It runs in two parts: the Preliminary Cockpit Prep first verifies the required checks before electrical power is applied — so no system operates inadvertently — and includes the APU start and the establishment of electrical and pneumatic power. Then the main Cockpit Prep follows the defined panel scan sequence, with PF and PM working the overhead panel first, then the center instrument panel, then the pedestal, checking, setting, and testing each item per the section flow. Work the scan in that order and we'll set up the jet exactly as the book lays it out.

flows.source: A320 Tutorials with UPRT - Flow Patterns - Cockpit Panels (January 2024)

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: We press and hold the CVR test to confirm the cockpit voice recorder is working — the test passes when you hear the audio test signal through the loudspeakers while holding the button.

explain.t: We press and hold the CVR test to confirm the cockpit voice recorder is working — the test passes when you hear the audio test signal through the loudspeakers while holding the button. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 1/20.

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: On the ground the CVR and flight data recorders only run automatically for the first five minutes after power-up or with an engine running, so selecting GND CTL to ON energizes them manually to capture the whole cockpit preparation.

explain.t: On the ground the CVR and flight data recorders only run automatically for the first five minutes after power-up or with an engine running, so selecting GND CTL to ON energizes them manually to capture the whole cockpit preparation. Source: F-COM § DSC-23-10-40, p. 885.

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: This EVAC-panel switch decides who can trigger the evacuation alert: CAPT & PURS lets it be activated from either cockpit or cabin, while CAPT restricts activation to the cockpit only, so it is set per company policy.

correct_line.t: Correct. Captain and Purser, Captain, AS required.

explain.t: This EVAC-panel switch decides who can trigger the evacuation alert: CAPT & PURS lets it be activated from either cockpit or cabin, while CAPT restricts activation to the cockpit only, so it is set per company policy. Source: F-COM § DSC-23-40-10, p. 909.

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: In AUTO the strobe lights come on automatically when the main landing gear is no longer compressed, so setting AUTO arms them to illuminate by themselves once the aircraft is airborne.

explain.t: In AUTO the strobe lights come on automatically when the main landing gear is no longer compressed, so setting AUTO arms them to illuminate by themselves once the aircraft is airborne. Source: F-COM § DSC-33-20-20, p. 1401.

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: Configuration item — no specific rationale in the book.

explain.t: It's a standard configuration setting.

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: Configuration item — no specific rationale in the book.

correct_line.t: Correct. Remaining Exterior Lights, AS required.

explain.t: It's a standard configuration setting.

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: Set as required; note that leaving the EXIT, NO SMOKING or NO PORTABLE/ELEC DEVICE selector ON prevents the emergency batteries from charging.

reveal.t: The answer is ON or AUTO.

explain.t: Set as required; note that leaving the EXIT, NO SMOKING or NO PORTABLE/ELEC DEVICE selector ON prevents the emergency batteries from charging. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 3/20.

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: Set to ARM; note that leaving the EXIT, NO SMOKING or NO PORTABLE/ELEC DEVICE selector ON prevents the emergency batteries from charging.

explain.t: Set to ARM; note that leaving the EXIT, NO SMOKING or NO PORTABLE/ELEC DEVICE selector ON prevents the emergency batteries from charging. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 3/20.

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: In AUTO the pressurization controller uses the FMGS landing elevation to build an optimized cabin-pressure schedule; if you leave it manual it ignores FMGS data and pressurizes to a fixed selected elevation instead.

explain.t: In AUTO the pressurization controller uses the F-M-G-S landing elevation to build an optimized cabin-pressure schedule; if you leave it manual it ignores F-M-G-S data and pressurizes to a fixed selected elevation instead. Source: F-COM § DSC-21-20-40, p. 175.

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: Leaving it auto means the Probe Heat Computers heat the pitot, static, AOA and TAT probes automatically once an engine is running or in flight, so you can't forget it and risk unreliable air data from blocked probes.

correct_line.t: Correct. Probe and Window Heat, AUTO.

explain.t: Leaving it auto means the Probe Heat Computers heat the pitot, static, AOA and TAT probes automatically once an engine is running or in flight, so you can't forget it and risk unreliable air data from blocked probes. Source: F-COM § DSC-30-50-10, p. 1173.

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: Pack flow is set to the cabin demand — LO below 141 occupants, HI for hot and humid conditions, NORM otherwise — and the controllers force HI anyway when the APU is supplying bleed.

explain.t: Pack flow is set to the cabin demand — LO below 141 occupants, HI for hot and humid conditions, normal otherwise — and the controllers force HI anyway when the APU is supplying bleed. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 4/20.

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: We scan the panel for any amber lights except GEN FAULT to confirm the electrical system is healthy.

explain.t: We scan the panel for any amber lights except generator FAULT to confirm the electrical system is healthy. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 4/20.

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: Cycling the batteries OFF then ON starts a charging cycle, and ten seconds later you verify each battery charge is below 60 A and decreasing to confirm the batteries are good.

explain.t: Cycling the batteries OFF then ON starts a charging cycle, and ten seconds later you verify each battery charge is below 60 A and decreasing to confirm the batteries are good. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 4/20.

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: Holding the ENG FIRE test pressed confirms all engine fire detection and extinguishing systems are operative — CRC, master warns, ECAM fire alerts, red pb-sw, SQUIB and DISCH lights all come on.

reveal.t: The answer is CHECK or TEST.

correct_line.t: Correct. Engine Fire, CHECK or TEST.

explain.t: Holding the engine fire test pressed confirms all engine fire detection and extinguishing systems are operative — C-R-C, master warns, E-CAM fire alerts, red , SQUIB and DISCH lights all come on. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 5/20.

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: NORM has each crew member on his own dedicated audio equipment; you only move it off NORM to route through the third ACP if ACP1 or ACP2 fails.

explain.t: normal has each crew member on his own dedicated audio equipment; you only move it off normal to route through the third ACP if ACP1 or ACP2 fails. Source: F-COM § DSC-23-10-50, p. 890.

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

explain.t: It's a standard configuration setting.

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: Confirms the avionics ventilation is healthy: it's the fully-automatic system that cools the avionics and flight-deck instrument and circuit-breaker panels with two electric fans, so a fault here threatens the electronics.

explain.t: Confirms the avionics ventilation is healthy: it's the fully-automatic system that cools the avionics and flight-deck instrument and circuit-breaker panels with two electric fans, so a fault here threatens the electronics. Source: F-COM § DSC-21-30-20, p. 1/8 (PDF p. 183).

condition.t: Condition: If secured stop or flight crew change: perform ALL actions. Otherwise (transit stop): perform only actions marked by * (per Q-R-H).

items.why: Set to RECEPT so cabin attendant announcements get recorded on the CVR, and the volume should be at or above mid-range for proper recording.

explain.t: Set to RECEPT so cabin attendant announcements get recorded on the CVR, and the volume should be at or above mid-range for proper recording. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 5/20.

items.why: Verifies the Integrated Standby Instrument System is operative, since it's the independent backup that displays attitude, airspeed/mach, altitude and barometric setting if the main PFDs are lost.

explain.t: Verifies the Integrated Standby Instrument System is operative, since it's the independent backup that displays attitude, airspeed/mach, altitude and barometric setting if the main PFDs are lost. Source: F-COM § DSC-34-NAV-20, p. 1/10 (PDF p. 1425).

items.why: We set the clock so the time is right for ATC datalink — to meet the plus or minus one second UTC precision requirement, the clock must be in GPS mode, or in internal mode synchronized with GPS at least once a day.

reveal.t: The answer is CHECK or SET.

explain.t: We set the clock so the time is right for ATC datalink — to meet the plus or minus one second U-T-C precision requirement, the clock must be in G-P-S mode, or in internal mode synchronized with G-P-S at least once a day. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 6/20.

items.why: With it ON, antiskid and nose wheel steering are available from green hydraulics (yellow takes over if green is lost); OFF deactivates antiskid and loses nose wheel steering, leaving only differential braking through the pedals.

correct_line.t: Correct. Anti-skid and Nosewheel Steering switch, ON.

explain.t: With it ON, antiskid and nose wheel steering are available from green hydraulics (yellow takes over if green is lost); OFF deactivates antiskid and loses nose wheel steering, leaving only differential braking through the pedals. Source: F-COM § DSC-32-30-20, p. 1369.

items.why: You set and check the ACP so the interphone volume is turned up enough to stay in contact with the ground crew during the prep and pushback.

reveal.t: The answer is CHECK ON or SET.

correct_line.t: Correct. Audio Control Panel, CHECK ON or SET.

explain.t: You set and check the ACP so the interphone volume is turned up enough to stay in contact with the ground crew during the prep and pushback. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 7/20.

items.why: NORM keeps all latches locked but still allows the cabin crew an emergency access request; it is the secure default, unlike LOCK which inhibits emergency entry or UNLOCK which opens the door.

explain.t: normal keeps all latches locked but still allows the cabin crew an emergency access request; it is the secure default, unlike LOCK which inhibits emergency entry or UNLOCK which opens the door. Source: F-COM Rev 2018-08-13 § DSC-52-40-20, p. 1891.

items.why: NORM gives the standard display source split — DMC 1 drives the Captain's PFD/ND and the ECAM, DMC 2 drives the First Officer's PFD/ND — so DMC 3 stays in reserve to cover a failed DMC.

explain.t: normal gives the standard display source split — DMC 1 drives the Captain's P-F-D/ND and the E-CAM, DMC 2 drives the First Officer's P-F-D/ND — so DMC 3 stays in reserve to cover a failed DMC. Source: F-COM Rev 2018-08-13 § DSC-31-30, p. 1250.

items.why: Thrust lever position is transmitted to the FADEC, which computes the thrust rating limit and N1 for that lever angle; IDLE is the no-thrust baseline before engine start.

explain.t: Thrust lever position is transmitted to the FADEC, which computes the thrust rating limit and N1 for that lever angle; idle is the no-thrust baseline before engine start. Source: F-COM § DSC-70-35-30, p. 1935.

items.why: Masters OFF is the required initial configuration before an automatic engine start, keeping the engine fuel shutoff valves closed so no fuel reaches the engine until the master is set ON during the start.
items.why_full: Setting the ENG MASTERS to OFF and the ENG MODE selector to NORM during aircraft setup ensures the required checks are completed before applying electrical power, to avoid inadvertent operation of systems and danger to the aircraft and personnel.

explain.t: Setting the engine masters to OFF and the engine mode selector to normal during aircraft setup ensures the required checks are completed before applying electrical power, to avoid inadvertent operation of systems and danger to the aircraft and personnel. Source: F-COM § DSC-70-80-40, p. 4/6.

items.why: NORM is the required initial configuration before start; selecting IGN/START is the deliberate first step that brings up the ENG page, makes all engine parameters available and auto-closes the pack valves, so it is left in NORM until the start is commanded.
items.why_full: ENG MODE selector NORM with the ENG MASTERS OFF is the engine portion of the aircraft setup performed before applying electrical power, preventing inadvertent system operation.

explain.t: engine mode selector normal with the engine masters OFF is the engine portion of the aircraft setup performed before applying electrical power, preventing inadvertent system operation. Source: F-COM § DSC-70-80-40, p. 4/6.

items.why: It is normally checked ON, but if the brakes are hot and chocks are in place you release it to OFF, because that increases brake cooling.

correct_line.t: Correct. Parking Brake handle, AS required.

explain.t: It is normally checked ON, but if the brakes are hot and chocks are in place you release it to OFF, because that increases brake cooling. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 8/20.

items.why: The gravity crank must be stowed because turning it isolates the landing gear from the green hydraulic system; if left out, normal hydraulic gear extension and retraction is disabled.

correct_line.t: Correct. Gravity Gear Extension, CHECK STOWED.

explain.t: The gravity crank must be stowed because turning it isolates the landing gear from the green hydraulic system; if left out, normal hydraulic gear extension and retraction is disabled. Source: F-COM § DSC-32-10-10, p. 4/6.

items.why: ATC and TCAS are left on standby to prevent possible interference to radar surveillance systems; TCAS should not be selected before the holding point or lineup.

explain.t: ATC and TCAS are left on standby to prevent possible interference to radar surveillance systems; TCAS should not be selected before the holding point or lineup. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 8/20.

items.why: Check the ACCU PRESS is in the green band; if required, recharge the brake accumulator using the electric pump on the yellow hydraulic system, and confirm normal brake pressure.

correct_line.t: Correct. Accumulator and Brake Pressure, CHECK.

explain.t: Check the ACCU PRESS is in the green band; if required, recharge the brake accumulator using the electric pump on the yellow hydraulic system, and confirm normal brake pressure. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 8/20.

items.why: On the M-C-D-U, run DIFRIP: STATUS page checked, then fill INIT A, F-PLN A, secondary F-PLN, RAD NAV, INIT B and PERF pages. That sequences navigation data then performance data so the F-M-S produces correct predictions, with the box-prompted fields filled in the most practical order.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24, Cockpit Preparation — FMGS Preparation, p. 143; FCTM § PR-NP-SOP-60, p. 2/6-3/6; FCOM § PRO-NOR-SOP-06, p. 9/20

explain.t: On the M-C-D-U, run DIFRIP: STATUS page checked, then fill INIT A, F-plan A, secondary F-plan, RAD NAV, INIT B and performance pages. That sequences navigation data then performance data so the F-M-S produces correct predictions, with the box-prompted fields filled in the most practical order. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24, Cockpit Preparation — F-M-G-S Preparation, p. 143; F-C-T-M § PR-NP-S-O-P-60, p. 2/6-3/6; F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 9/20.

items.subflow: Cockpit Preparation - FMGS Preparation Crosscheck (PM reference)

sop_intro.t: Pilot Monitoring F-M-G-S preparation crosscheck — spoken reference, performed independently of the flow.

items.item: F-PLN A page: check the flight plan using the N-D in PLAN mode against the computerized flight plan.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24, Cockpit Preparation — FMGS Preparation, p. 144

narration.t: F-plan A page: check the flight plan using the N-D in PLAN mode against the computerized flight plan.

items.subflow: Cockpit Preparation - FMGS Preparation Crosscheck (PM reference)

sop_intro.t: Pilot Monitoring F-M-G-S preparation crosscheck — spoken reference, performed independently of the flow.

items.item: Check distance to destination and compare it with the total distance computed for the flight with the computerized flight plan; any discrepancy may affect fuel management.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24, Cockpit Preparation — FMGS Preparation, p. 144

narration.t: Check distance to destination and compare it with the total distance computed for the flight with the computerized flight plan; any discrepancy may affect fuel management.

items.subflow: Cockpit Preparation - FMGS Preparation Crosscheck (PM reference)

sop_intro.t: Pilot Monitoring F-M-G-S preparation crosscheck — spoken reference, performed independently of the flow.

items.item: Should the flight management hold erroneous information, including the constraints, the aircraft follows an unwanted trajectory; crosschecking against the charts — SIDs and STARs — prevents it.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24, Cockpit Preparation — FMGS Preparation, p. 144

narration.t: Should the flight management hold erroneous information, including the constraints, the aircraft follows an unwanted trajectory; crosschecking against the charts — SIDs and STARs — prevents it.

items.subflow: Cockpit Preparation - FMGS Preparation Crosscheck (PM reference)

sop_intro.t: Pilot Monitoring F-M-G-S preparation crosscheck — spoken reference, performed independently of the flow.

items.item: FMGS preparation crosscheck: before the departure briefing, the Pilot Monitoring carefully crosschecks, against the computerized flight plan — waypoints and constraints of the expected departure route versus the departure chart; the flight-plan waypoints; initial cruise altitude; total track miles; performance data on the Pilot Monitoring's side with the takeoff page of the FMGS active perf page, including the thrust reduction, acceleration and engine-out acceleration altitude settings if applicable; and setup of the secondary flight plan.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24, Cockpit Preparation — FMGS Preparation, p. 145

narration.t: F-M-G-S preparation crosscheck: before the departure briefing, the Pilot Monitoring carefully crosschecks, against the computerized flight plan — waypoints and constraints of the expected departure route versus the departure chart; the flight-plan waypoints; initial cruise altitude; total track miles; performance data on the Pilot Monitoring's side with the takeoff page of the F-M-G-S active performance page, including the thrust reduction, acceleration and engine-out acceleration altitude settings if applicable; and setup of the secondary flight plan.

items.subflow: Cockpit Preparation - FMGS Preparation Crosscheck (PM reference)

sop_intro.t: Pilot Monitoring F-M-G-S preparation crosscheck — spoken reference, performed independently of the flow.

items.item: The Pilot Monitoring should hold the same mental image of the intended departure — procedure, trajectory and constraints — as the Pilot Flying, and check with the Pilot Flying if anything is not clear.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24, Cockpit Preparation — FMGS Preparation, p. 145

narration.t: The Pilot Monitoring should hold the same mental image of the intended departure — procedure, trajectory and constraints — as the Pilot Flying, and check with the Pilot Flying if anything is not clear.

condition.t: Condition: When both crewmembers are seated.

items.why: You set QNH on both EFIS panels and the standby altimeter, then cross-check that all altitude indications agree within the published tolerances, so every altimeter in the cockpit is referenced the same before flight.

explain.t: You set Q-N-H on both E-F-I-S panels and the standby altimeter, then cross-check that all altitude indications agree within the published tolerances, so every altimeter in the cockpit is referenced the same before flight. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 14/20.

condition.t: Condition: When both crewmembers are seated.

items.why: FD on so it provides attitude and flight-path orders that let the pilot flying accurately hand-fly the aircraft, with the FMA showing the appropriate guidance modes for the intended path.

explain.t: FD on so it provides attitude and flight-path orders that let the pilot flying accurately hand-fly the aircraft, with the FMA showing the appropriate guidance modes for the intended path. Source: F-C-T-M § AS-FG-10-1, p. 1/4.

condition.t: Condition: When both crewmembers are seated.

items.why: The LS pushbutton brings up the ILS/GLS/MLS course plus the LOC and glideslope deviation scales on the PFD, so you arm it as required for the planned approach; if APPR is armed and LS isn't selected, an amber LS message flashes to warn you the deviation guidance is missing.

explain.t: The L-S pushbutton brings up the I-L-S/GLS/MLS course plus the LOC and glideslope deviation scales on the P-F-D, so you arm it as required for the planned approach; if APPR is armed and L-S isn't selected, an amber L-S message flashes to warn you the deviation guidance is missing. Source: F-COM Rev 2018-08-13 § DSC-31-40, p. 1274.

condition.t: Condition: When both crewmembers are seated.

items.why: Set the ND mode and range as required for the phase — the EFIS control panel selects the navigation picture and distance range shown on the ND.

correct_line.t: Correct. N-D mode and range, AS required.

explain.t: Set the ND mode and range as required for the phase — the E-F-I-S control panel selects the navigation picture and distance range shown on the ND. Source: F-COM § DSC-22-10-10, p. 214.

condition.t: Condition: When both crewmembers are seated.

items.why: Not covered in the 2018 FCOM cockpit-prep SOP (the tutorial deck is newer); per the deck, set EFIS options to display constraints.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials Jan 2024, Cockpit Preparation, p. 147

explain.t: Not covered in the 2018 F-COM cockpit-prep S-O-P (the tutorial deck is newer); per the deck, set E-F-I-S options to display constraints. Source: A320 Tutorials Jan 2024, Cockpit Preparation, p. 147.

condition.t: Condition: When both crewmembers are seated.

items.why: You set the ADF/VOR selectors so the ND shows the VOR and ADF pointers you actually need for the departure.

correct_line.t: Correct. A-D-F, V-O-R selector, AS required.

explain.t: You set the ADF/V-O-R selectors so the ND shows the V-O-R and ADF pointers you actually need for the departure. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 15/20.

condition.t: Condition: When both crewmembers are seated.

items.why: You dash the speed window, select HDG V/S, and set the initial expected clearance altitude in the ALT window; note you do not engage the autothrust on the ground because it may generate the AUTO FLT A/THR OFF warning at engine start.

explain.t: You dash the speed window, select heading vertical speed, and set the initial expected clearance altitude in the altitude window; note you do not engage the autothrust on the ground because it may generate the AUTO FLT auto-thrust OFF warning at engine start. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06, p. 15/20.

Final Cockpit Preparation - Lateral Consoles / Instrument Panels

correct_line.t: Correct. Landing Elevation on the E-CAM, CHECK AUTO.

Final Cockpit Preparation - Lateral Consoles / Instrument Panels

reveal.t: The answer is COCKPIT PREPARATION CHECKLIST.

correct_line.t: Correct. Call for the checklist, COCKPIT PREPARATION CHECKLIST.

items.why: Once the PF finishes the FMGS preparation the PM independently checks every entry by going through the same FMGS pages in the same order, including comparing the FMS PERF T.O page against the PM's own EFB takeoff data, to catch any error before it is used.
items.cite: FCOM § PRO-NOR-SOP-06 (CHECK OF FMGS PREPARATION), p. 14/20 (and FCTM § PR-NP-SOP-60 FMGS DATA CROSSCHECK, p. 4/6)

explain.t: Once the PF finishes the F-M-G-S preparation the PM independently checks every entry by going through the same F-M-G-S pages in the same order, including comparing the FMS performance T.O page against the PM's own EFB takeoff data, to catch any error before it is used. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06 (CHECK OF F-M-G-S PREPARATION), p. 14/20 (and F-C-T-M § PR-NP-S-O-P-60 F-M-G-S DATA CROSSCHECK, p. 4/6).

items.why: The PM compares the Green Dot speed the FMGS computed with the one the EFB TAKEOFF application computed because a discrepancy reveals that a different takeoff weight was used in the two systems, flagging a weight-entry error.
items.cite: FCOM § PRO-NOR-SOP-06 (CHECK OF FMGS PREPARATION), p. 14/20

correct_line.t: Correct. E-F-B and M-C-D-U green dot, COMPARE.

explain.t: The PM compares the green dot speed the F-M-G-S computed with the one the EFB TAKEOFF application computed because a discrepancy reveals that a different takeoff weight was used in the two systems, flagging a weight-entry error. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-06 (CHECK OF F-M-G-S PREPARATION), p. 14/20.

flows.name: Descent Preparation
flows.source: A320 Tutorials with UPRT - Flow Patterns - Cockpit Panels (January 2024)

sop_intro.t: Descent Preparation, run about fifteen minutes before top of descent. The PM obtains the weather and landing information and checks the STATUS page; both pilots confirm the landing conditions and, if they have changed, compute and cross-check the landing performance. The PF prepares the FMS - the PM cross-checks it - then sets landing elevation, autobrake and radar, and performs the arrival briefing.

condition.t: Condition: Approximately 15 min before top of descent

items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck, p.212 (Descent Preparation)

sop_intro.t: Descent Preparation, run about fifteen minutes before top of descent. The PM obtains the weather and landing information and checks the STATUS page; both pilots confirm the landing conditions and, if they have changed, compute and cross-check the landing performance. The PF prepares the FMS - the PM cross-checks it - then sets landing elevation, autobrake and radar, and performs the arrival briefing.

condition.t: Condition: Approximately 15 min before top of descent

items.why: Check whether the landing conditions changed compared with the landing distance computed at dispatch, or with a previous computation - e.g. runway change, weather change, in-flight failure affecting performance, or diversion.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck, p.213 (Descent Preparation - Landing Conditions)

Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck, p.213 (Descent Preparation - Landing Conditions).

condition.t: Condition: Approximately 15 min before top of descent

items.why: Insert the lateral and vertical revisions for the arrival; select or deselect navaids and check the idents on the PFDs and NDs; monitor NAV ACCURACY in the descent; complete the DES, APPR and GO AROUND pages. On the PERF APPR page an erroneous OAT or QNH entry can cause an undue emergency descent (wrong cabin-pressure-control segment computation). Check extra fuel for holding, and copy/revise the SEC F-PLN per circumstances (alternative approach, circling).
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck, p.214 (Descent Preparation - FMS Prepare)

explain.t: Insert the lateral and vertical revisions for the arrival; select or deselect navaids and check the idents on the PFDs and NDs; monitor NAV ACCURACY in the descent; complete the descent, APPR and GO AROUND pages. On the performance APPR page an erroneous outside air temperature or Q-N-H entry can cause an undue emergency descent (wrong cabin-pressure-control segment computation). Check extra fuel for holding, and copy/revise the SEC F-plan per circumstances (alternative approach, circling). Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck, p.214 (Descent Preparation - FMS Prepare).

condition.t: Condition: Approximately 15 min before top of descent

condition.t: Condition: Approximately 15 min before top of descent

items.why: A structured, threat-focused briefing. PLAN: approach guidance, landing flap setting, stop-margin, use of reverse and autobrake, planned runway exit, taxi-in hotspots, and special or non-standard operations. Then the identified THREATS and their MITIGATIONS, and MISCELLANEOUS items. The PM briefs the main PLAN items so both pilots share the same mental picture; the PF briefs what she or he considers for landing. Keep it conversational - not a re-read of the FMS.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck, p.216-217 (Descent Preparation - Arrival Briefing)

Then the identified THREATS and their MITIGATIONS, and MISCELLANEOUS items. The PM briefs the main PLAN items so both pilots share the same mental picture; the PF briefs what she or he considers for landing. Keep it conversational - not a re-read of the FMS. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck, p.216-217 (Descent Preparation - Arrival Briefing).

sop_intro.t: Go-around clean-up scan (deck p273/274). PM, in deck order: FLAP 0, then ground spoilers disarm, then check landing gear up, then exterior lights as required (NOSE/runway TURN OFF off).
Go-around clean-up scan (deck p273/274). PM, in deck order: FLAP 0, then ground spoilers disarm, then check landing gear up, then exterior lights as required (NOSE/runway TURN OFF off).
