Abnormal Slats or Flaps is a single-action memory item: for any slats or flaps problem, pull the SPEED knob to selected speed. At takeoff that selected speed stops the acceleration and keeps you from exceeding V-F-E; during the approach it stops any further deceleration. It is run before completing the E-CAM.
The reflex: pull the SPEED knob to freeze speed in the safe band for the surfaces you have.

condition.t: Condition: For any slats / flaps problem

items.why: Pull the SPEED knob to selected speed. At takeoff the selected speed stops the acceleration and avoids exceeding VFE; during approach it avoids further deceleration. Then complete the ECAM.
items.cite: A320 memory-items card p.3; FCTM PR-AEP-F_CTL

explain.t: Pull the SPEED knob to selected speed. At takeoff the selected speed stops the acceleration and avoids exceeding V-F-E; during approach it avoids further deceleration. Then complete the E-CAM. Source: A320 memory-items card p.3; F-C-T-M PR-AEP-F_CTL.

sop_intro.t: G-P-W-S/E-G-P-W-S Cautions are conditional. Terrain/obstacle cautions at night or in I-M-C are flown like a warning (full pull-up); in daylight V-M-C with terrain in sight, just adjust the flight path. Rate and glideslope cautions hinge on the 1000 feet I-M-C / 500 feet V-M-C gate.

items.item: TERRAIN / TOO LOW TERRAIN / TERRAIN AHEAD / OBSTACLE AHEAD
items.why: Night or IMC: full pull-up reaction (AP off, full back stick, TOGA, wings level, no config change). Daylight + VMC with terrain in sight: adjust pitch/bank/thrust to silence.

prompt.t: TERRAIN / TOO LOW TERRAIN / TERRAIN AHEAD / OBSTACLE AHEAD

reveal.t: The answer is PULL UP (night or I-M-C) or ADJUST (daylight V-M-C).

correct_line.t: Correct. TERRAIN / TOO LOW TERRAIN / TERRAIN AHEAD / OBSTACLE AHEAD, PULL UP (night or I-M-C) or ADJUST (daylight V-M-C).

explain.t: Night or I-M-C: full pull-up reaction (AP off, full back stick, toga, wings level, no config change). Daylight + V-M-C with terrain in sight: adjust pitch/bank/thrust to silence. Source: F-COM p.2699.

items.why: Above 1000 ft AAL IMC / 500 ft VMC: adjust flight path. Below: consider go-around.

reveal.t: The answer is ADJUST or GO-AROUND CONSIDER.

correct_line.t: Correct. SINK RATE, ADJUST or GO-AROUND CONSIDER.

explain.t: Above 1000 feet AAL I-M-C / 500 feet V-M-C: adjust flight path. Below: consider go-around. Source: F-COM p.2699.

items.why: Adjust pitch and thrust to silence the alert.

reveal.t: The answer is FLIGHT PATH ADJUST.

correct_line.t: Correct. DON'T SINK, FLIGHT PATH ADJUST.

explain.t: Adjust pitch and thrust to silence the alert. Source: F-COM p.2699.

items.item: TOO LOW GEAR / TOO LOW FLAPS
items.why: Perform a go-around.

prompt.t: TOO LOW GEAR / TOO LOW FLAPS

correct_line.t: Correct. TOO LOW GEAR / TOO LOW FLAPS, GO-AROUND PERFORM.

explain.t: Perform a go-around. Source: F-COM p.2700.

items.action: ADJUST / G/S MODE OFF / GO-AROUND CONSIDER
items.why: Above 1000 ft IMC / 500 ft VMC: adjust to reduce deviation. Deliberate approach below G/S: G/S MODE OFF. Below the gate: consider go-around.

reveal.t: The answer is ADJUST or G or S MODE OFF or GO-AROUND CONSIDER.

correct_line.t: Correct. GLIDESLOPE, ADJUST or G or S MODE OFF or GO-AROUND CONSIDER.

explain.t: Above 1000 feet I-M-C / 500 feet V-M-C: adjust to reduce deviation. Deliberate approach below G/S: G/S MODE OFF. Below the gate: consider go-around. Source: F-COM p.2700.

sop_intro.t: G-P-W-S/E-G-P-W-S Warning. A red PULL UP is an unconditional, immediate maneuver — pull up regardless of day or night. All actions are simultaneous. Do not change configuration until clear of the obstacle.

items.why: Pull to full back stick and maintain — simultaneously with the other actions.

reveal.t: The answer is PULL UP. Callout: PULL UP toga.

explain.t: Pull to full back stick and maintain — simultaneously with the other actions. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-SURV p.2701.

items.why: Disconnect AP to hand-fly the escape.

explain.t: Disconnect AP to hand-fly the escape. Source: F-COM p.2701.

items.why: TOGA thrust for max climb performance.

explain.t: toga thrust for max climb performance. Source: F-COM p.2701.

items.why: Confirm speedbrakes retracted.

correct_line.t: Correct. SPEED BRAKES lever, CHECK RETRACTED.

explain.t: Confirm speedbrakes retracted. Source: F-COM p.2701.

items.why: Wings level gives best climb. Turn only if the crew concludes turning is the safest action — pull up first, then turn.

reveal.t: The answer is WINGS LEVEL or ADJUST.

correct_line.t: Correct. BANK, WINGS LEVEL or ADJUST.

explain.t: Wings level gives best climb. Turn only if the crew concludes turning is the safest action — pull up first, then turn. Source: F-COM p.2701.

items.why: Do not change slats/flaps/gear until clear of the obstacle.

reveal.t: The answer is DO NOT CHANGE UNTIL CLEAR.

correct_line.t: Correct. CONFIGURATION, DO NOT CHANGE UNTIL CLEAR.

explain.t: Do not change slats/flaps/gear until clear of the obstacle. Source: F-COM p.2701.

sop_intro.t: Emergency Descent is a memory item with two steps. First step: the memory items, done from memory to get the aircraft descending and the crew on oxygen. Second step: the read-and-do procedure to level-off at flight level one zero zero or M-E-A-M-O-R-A. F-C-T-M frames it as two steps but does not itself partition which exact items are step one versus step two. During step one the PM monitors the FMA to confirm the PF has established the descent; during step two the PF refines the settings.
Step one — the reflex actions. Get the masks on, establish the descent, idle and full speedbrake.

items.why: Both pilots don masks immediately — protect the crew.

explain.t: Both pilots don masks immediately — protect the crew. Source: Q-R-H 25.02A p.92.

sop_intro.t: Step one — the reflex actions. Get the masks on, establish the descent, idle and full speedbrake.

items.callout: THRUST IDLE, OPEN DESCENT, HEADING, ALT BLUE
items.why: AP and autothrust use is highly recommended (FCTM p.401). Technique to initiate: ALT knob turn then pull, HDG knob turn off the airway then pull, SPD knob pull — this is instructor/SOP technique; only 'AP/ATHR highly recommended' is FCOM/FCTM text.

reveal.t: The answer is INITIATE. Callout: THRUST idle, OPEN DESCENT, HEADING, altitude BLUE.

explain.t: AP and autothrust use is highly recommended (F-C-T-M p.401). Technique to initiate: altitude knob turn then pull, heading knob turn off the airway then pull, speed knob pull — this is instructor/S-O-P technique; only 'AP/ATHR highly recommended' is F-COM/F-C-T-M text. Source: Q-R-H 25.02A p.92; F-C-T-M p.401.

sop_intro.t: Step one — the reflex actions. Get the masks on, establish the descent, idle and full speedbrake.

condition.t: Condition: If auto-thrust not active

items.why: Only if autothrust is not active — idle thrust to accelerate the descent.

explain.t: Only if autothrust is not active — idle thrust to accelerate the descent. Source: Q-R-H 25.02A p.92.

sop_intro.t: Step one — the reflex actions. Get the masks on, establish the descent, idle and full speedbrake.

items.why: Full speedbrake for max rate; at high FL watch VLS so alpha-prot doesn't retract them and drop the AP.

explain.t: Full speedbrake for max rate; at high FL watch V-L-S so alpha-prot doesn't retract them and drop the AP. Source: Q-R-H 25.02A p.92; F-C-T-M p.399.

sop_intro.t: Step two — the read-and-do, refined. Continue to level-off at flight level one zero zero or M-E-A-M-O-R-A.

condition.t: Condition: When descent established

items.why: Once established, fly MAX or appropriate speed.

reveal.t: The answer is MAX or APPROPRIATE.

explain.t: Once established, fly MAX or appropriate speed. Source: Q-R-H 25.02A p.92.

sop_intro.t: Step two — the read-and-do, refined. Continue to level-off at flight level one zero zero or M-E-A-M-O-R-A.

condition.t: Condition: If structural damage suspected

items.why: If a loud bang / high cabin V/S suggests structural damage, maneuver with care — FCTM: set SPD/MACH pb to SPEED and use speedbrakes carefully.

reveal.t: The answer is MANEUVER WITH CARE.

correct_line.t: Correct. STRUCTURAL DAMAGE, MANEUVER WITH CARE.

explain.t: If a loud bang / high cabin vertical speed suggests structural damage, maneuver with care — F-C-T-M: set speed/MACH pushbutton to SPEED and use speedbrakes carefully. Source: Q-R-H 25.02A p.92; F-C-T-M p.399.

sop_intro.t: Step two — the read-and-do, refined. Continue to level-off at flight level one zero zero or M-E-A-M-O-R-A.

items.why: Consider gear extension to increase drag/rate (respect VLO/VLE).

explain.t: Consider gear extension to increase drag/rate (respect V-L-O/V-L-E). Source: Q-R-H 25.02A p.92.

sop_intro.t: Step two — the read-and-do, refined. Continue to level-off at flight level one zero zero or M-E-A-M-O-R-A.

items.why: Level-off target: FL100, or MEA/MORA if terrain higher (highest MORA within 40 NM).

explain.t: Level-off target: flight level 100, or M-E-A/M-O-R-A if terrain higher (highest M-O-R-A within 40 NM). Source: Q-R-H 25.02A p.92; F-C-T-M p.399.

sop_intro.t: Step one — the reflex actions. Get the masks on, establish the descent, idle and full speedbrake.

items.why: Both pilots don masks immediately.

explain.t: Both pilots don masks immediately. Source: Q-R-H 25.02A p.92.

sop_intro.t: Step one — the reflex actions. Get the masks on, establish the descent, idle and full speedbrake.

items.why: Seatbelt/no-smoking signs on for the cabin.

explain.t: Seatbelt/no-smoking signs on for the cabin. Source: Q-R-H 25.02A p.92; F-C-T-M p.401.

sop_intro.t: Step one — the reflex actions. Get the masks on, establish the descent, idle and full speedbrake.

items.why: PM monitors the FMA to confirm the PF has correctly established the aircraft in the descent. ('CHECKED' is a crew-standard readback, not printed on the card.)

reveal.t: The answer is CHECK. Callout: CHECKED.

explain.t: PM monitors the FMA to confirm the PF has correctly established the aircraft in the descent. ('CHECKED' is a crew-standard readback, not printed on the card.) Source: F-C-T-M p.401.

sop_intro.t: Step two — the read-and-do, refined. Continue to level-off at flight level one zero zero or M-E-A-M-O-R-A.

items.why: Set ENG MODE SEL to IGN per the procedure. (Rationale commonly given — relight protection in the rapid descent — is crew-standard, not stated on the QRH/FCOM card.)

explain.t: Set engine mode SEL to ignition per the procedure. (Rationale commonly given — relight protection in the rapid descent — is crew-standard, not stated on the Q-R-H/F-COM card.) Source: Q-R-H 25.02A p.92.

sop_intro.t: Step two — the read-and-do, refined. Continue to level-off at flight level one zero zero or M-E-A-M-O-R-A.

items.why: Tell ATC — you are leaving your level.

explain.t: Tell ATC — you are leaving your level. Source: Q-R-H 25.02A p.92.

sop_intro.t: Step two — the read-and-do, refined. Continue to level-off at flight level one zero zero or M-E-A-M-O-R-A.

items.why: PM makes the passenger PA announcing the emergency descent.

reveal.t: The answer is ANNOUNCE. Callout: EMERGENCY DESCENT.

explain.t: PM makes the passenger PA announcing the emergency descent. Source: Q-R-H 25.02A p.92.

sop_intro.t: Step two — the read-and-do, refined. Continue to level-off at flight level one zero zero or M-E-A-M-O-R-A.

items.why: Consider squawking 7700 unless otherwise specified by ATC. Canonical wording is CONSIDER, not a hard set.

explain.t: Consider squawking 7700 unless otherwise specified by ATC. Canonical wording is CONSIDER, not a hard set. Source: Q-R-H 25.02A p.92; F-COM p.2186.

sop_intro.t: Step two — the read-and-do, refined. Continue to level-off at flight level one zero zero or M-E-A-M-O-R-A.

items.why: Set the diluter to the N/NORM position to save oxygen.

correct_line.t: Correct. CREW OXY MASKS DILUTION, normal.

explain.t: Set the diluter to the N/normal position to save oxygen. Source: Q-R-H 25.02A p.92.

sop_intro.t: Step two — the read-and-do, refined. Continue to level-off at flight level one zero zero or M-E-A-M-O-R-A.

condition.t: Condition: If CAB altitude above 14 000 feet

items.why: If cabin altitude exceeds 14 000 ft, manually deploy the passenger masks.

correct_line.t: Correct. OXYGEN passengers MASK MAN ON, PRESS.

explain.t: If cabin altitude exceeds 14 000 feet, manually deploy the passenger masks. Source: Q-R-H 25.02A p.92.

flows.name: Engine Failure during Climb / Cruise (Memory Item)

sop_intro.t: Engine Failure in the climb or cruise. The reflex is to secure thrust and heading, then determine the engine-out strategy: set the thrust levers to maximum continuous, disconnect autothrust if you are above the engine-out ceiling, take an appropriate heading, recall your engine-out recovery altitude, and pick a descent strategy — standard, obstacle, or fixed speed — before flying the driftdown.
Secure thrust and flight path first.

items.why: Set the live engine to maximum continuous thrust — the max thrust permitted continuously with one engine out.
items.cite: A320 memory-items card p.7; FCTM PR-AEP-ENG

explain.t: Set the live engine to maximum continuous thrust — the max thrust permitted continuously with one engine out. Source: A320 memory-items card p.7; F-C-T-M PR-AEP-ENG.

sop_intro.t: Secure thrust and flight path first.

condition.t: Condition: If above engine-out ceiling

items.why: Disconnect autothrust only if you are above the engine-out ceiling, so thrust stays at MCT for the driftdown instead of being pulled back.
items.cite: A320 memory-items card p.7; FCTM PR-AEP-ENG

explain.t: Disconnect autothrust only if you are above the engine-out ceiling, so thrust stays at M-C-T for the driftdown instead of being pulled back. Source: A320 memory-items card p.7; F-C-T-M PR-AEP-ENG.

sop_intro.t: Secure thrust and flight path first.

items.why: Take an appropriate heading — terrain, the engine-out routing, or ATC.
items.cite: A320 memory-items card p.7; FCTM PR-AEP-ENG

explain.t: Take an appropriate heading — terrain, the engine-out routing, or ATC. Source: A320 memory-items card p.7; F-C-T-M PR-AEP-ENG.

sop_intro.t: Determine the strategy, then fly the descent.

items.why: Recall the engine-out recovery (driftdown) altitude — determined in cruise ahead of time for the terrain on track.
items.cite: A320 memory-items card p.7; FCTM PR-AEP-ENG

correct_line.t: Correct. ENGINE OUT RECOVERY ALTITUDE, DETERMINE.

explain.t: Recall the engine-out recovery (driftdown) altitude — determined in cruise ahead of time for the terrain on track. Source: A320 memory-items card p.7; F-C-T-M PR-AEP-ENG.

sop_intro.t: Determine the strategy, then fly the descent.

items.why: Choose the driftdown strategy: standard, obstacle (terrain-limited), or fixed speed.
items.cite: A320 memory-items card p.7; FCTM PR-AEP-ENG

reveal.t: The answer is DETERMINE — STANDARD or OBSTACLE or FIXED SPEED.

correct_line.t: Correct. DESCENT STRATEGY, DETERMINE — STANDARD or OBSTACLE or FIXED SPEED.

explain.t: Choose the driftdown strategy: standard, obstacle (terrain-limited), or fixed speed. Source: A320 memory-items card p.7; F-C-T-M PR-AEP-ENG.

sop_intro.t: Determine the strategy, then fly the descent.

items.action: PULL SPEED, SET EO RECOVERY ALT, PULL FOR OPEN DES
items.why: To start down: pull the SPEED knob for selected speed, set the engine-out recovery altitude on the FCU, and pull for OPEN DESCENT.
items.cite: A320 memory-items card p.7; FCTM PR-AEP-ENG

reveal.t: The answer is PULL SPEED, SET EO RECOVERY altitude, PULL FOR OPEN descent.

correct_line.t: Correct. FOR DESCENT, PULL SPEED, SET EO RECOVERY altitude, PULL FOR OPEN descent.

explain.t: To start down: pull the SPEED knob for selected speed, set the engine-out recovery altitude on the FCU, and pull for OPEN DESCENT. Source: A320 memory-items card p.7; F-C-T-M PR-AEP-ENG.

sop_intro.t: Determine the strategy, then fly the descent.

items.why: Once the flight path is secured, complete the ECAM and any applicable OEB actions.
items.cite: A320 memory-items card p.7; FCTM PR-AEP-ENG

explain.t: Once the flight path is secured, complete the E-CAM and any applicable O-E-B actions. Source: A320 memory-items card p.7; F-C-T-M PR-AEP-ENG.

sop_intro.t: Loss of Braking is a memory item run as one continuous escalating flow. It is sequencing-critical: the PF releases the pedals, orders anti-skid off, the PM selects it off, and only then does the PF press the pedals again — this avoids a brutal brake application when control transfers from the BSCU to the ABCU.
First tier — when there is no braking at all.

condition.t: Condition: If no braking

items.why: Select max reverse immediately — primary deceleration when wheel braking is lost.

reveal.t: The answer is MAX. Callout: LOSS OF BRAKING.

explain.t: Select max reverse immediately — primary deceleration when wheel braking is lost. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-BRAKES p.2163.

sop_intro.t: First tier — when there is no braking at all.

condition.t: Condition: If no braking

items.why: Release pedals BEFORE A/SKID off — control transfers BSCU to ABCU; releasing first avoids a brutal application.

explain.t: Release pedals BEFORE anti-skid off — control transfers BSCU to ABCU; releasing first avoids a brutal application. Source: F-COM p.2163; F-C-T-M p.357.

sop_intro.t: First tier — when there is no braking at all.

condition.t: Condition: If no braking

items.why: PF orders the PM to set the A/SKID & N/W STRG switch off — activates alternate braking.

reveal.t: The answer is ORDER. Callout: anti-skid OFF.

explain.t: PF orders the PM to set the anti-skid & nose wheel steering switch off — activates alternate braking. Source: F-COM p.2163.

sop_intro.t: First tier — when there is no braking at all.

condition.t: Condition: If no braking

items.why: Press pedals again only after the PM has set A/SKID off.

explain.t: Press pedals again only after the PM has set anti-skid off. Source: F-COM p.2163; F-C-T-M p.357.

sop_intro.t: First tier — when there is no braking at all.

condition.t: Condition: If no braking

items.why: In alternate braking the ABCU limits pressure to 1000 PSI — monitor on the BRAKES PRESS indicator.

explain.t: In alternate braking the ABCU limits pressure to 1000 PSI — monitor on the BRAKES PRESS indicator. Source: F-COM p.2163.

sop_intro.t: Second tier — if there is still no braking.

condition.t: Condition: If still no braking

items.why: Use SHORT successive parking-brake applications to stop — brake-onset asymmetry may be felt; delay to low speed if possible to reduce tyre-burst risk. Max reverse may be kept to a full stop.

explain.t: Use SHORT successive parking-brake applications to stop — brake-onset asymmetry may be felt; delay to low speed if possible to reduce tyre-burst risk. Max reverse may be kept to a full stop. Source: F-COM p.2163; F-C-T-M p.357.

sop_intro.t: First tier — when there is no braking at all.

condition.t: Condition: If no braking

items.why: PM selects A/SKID & N/W STRG off on the PF's order — transfers to alternate braking (no anti-skid).

correct_line.t: Correct. anti-skid & nose wheel steering switch, OFF.

explain.t: PM selects anti-skid & nose wheel steering off on the PF's order — transfers to alternate braking (no anti-skid). Source: F-COM p.2163; F-C-T-M p.357.

sop_intro.t: Low Energy. The aural 'SPEED SPEED SPEED' warns the energy level is dropping below the threshold where thrust is needed to regain a positive flight path through pitch control. There is no E-CAM. It fires before alpha-floor in a normal deceleration. Recovery is to increase thrust and/or adjust the pitch, depending on the circumstances, until the aural stops — the F-C-T-M sets no fixed order between them. Note: Low Energy is a systems alert plus F-C-T-M technique, not on the official Airbus memory-item list; it is drilled here as a reflex.

items.why: Increase thrust to regain a positive flight path angle through pitch control. FCTM: thrust and/or pitch, depending on circumstances — there is no mandated thrust-first order.

reveal.t: The answer is INCREASE. Callout: SPEED SPEED SPEED.

explain.t: Increase thrust to regain a positive flight path angle through pitch control. F-C-T-M: thrust and/or pitch, depending on circumstances — there is no mandated thrust-first order. Source: F-COM DSC-22_40-30 p.847; F-C-T-M p.408.

items.why: Adjust pitch as required, until the aural alert stops. Thrust and pitch are applied together as needed (and/or), not in a fixed sequence.

reveal.t: The answer is ADJUST AS required.

correct_line.t: Correct. PITCH ATTITUDE, ADJUST AS required.

explain.t: Adjust pitch as required, until the aural alert stops. Thrust and pitch are applied together as needed (and/or), not in a fixed sequence. Source: F-C-T-M p.408.

sop_intro.t: Predictive Windshear scans ahead with the weather radar. Three levels: advisory (icon only), caution 'MONITOR RADAR DISPLAY', and warning. Response depends on phase.

condition.t: Condition: Warning at takeoff

items.why: Before the roll: delay takeoff or select the most favorable runway.

reveal.t: The answer is DELAY or SELECT FAVORABLE runway. Callout: WINDSHEAR AHEAD, WINDSHEAR AHEAD.

correct_line.t: Correct. TAKEOFF (before roll), DELAY or SELECT FAVORABLE runway.

explain.t: Before the roll: delay takeoff or select the most favorable runway. Source: F-COM p.2706.

condition.t: Condition: Warning at takeoff

items.why: During the takeoff roll up to 100 kt, the Captain rejects (warning inhibited above 100 kt until 50 ft).

explain.t: During the takeoff roll up to 100 knots, the Captain rejects (warning inhibited above 100 knots until 50 feet). Source: F-COM p.2706; F-C-T-M p.327.

condition.t: Condition: Warning when airborne

items.why: Set TOGA. Config may be changed only if not entering the windshear.

explain.t: Set toga. Config may be changed only if not entering the windshear. Source: F-COM p.2706.

condition.t: Condition: Warning when airborne

items.why: Keep AP on.

explain.t: Keep AP on. Source: F-COM p.2706.

condition.t: Condition: Warning when airborne

items.why: Follow SRS; full aft stick if needed.

explain.t: Follow SRS; full aft stick if needed. Source: F-COM p.2706.

condition.t: Condition: Warning in approach

items.why: Perform the go-around.

reveal.t: The answer is PERFORM. Callout: GO AROUND, WINDSHEAR AHEAD.

explain.t: Perform the go-around. Source: F-COM p.2707.

condition.t: Condition: Warning in approach

items.why: Keep AP on.

explain.t: Keep AP on. Source: F-COM p.2707.

sop_intro.t: Caution path — precautionary measures per F-C-T-M.

condition.t: Condition: Amber caution

items.why: Apply precautionary measures. Approach: delay or divert; if continuing — CONF 3 landing, managed speed, VAPP up to VLS+15 kt.

reveal.t: The answer is APPLY. Callout: MONITOR RADAR DISPLAY.

explain.t: Apply precautionary measures. Approach: delay or divert; if continuing — config 3 landing, managed speed, V app up to V-L-S+15 knots. Source: F-COM p.2707; F-C-T-M p.327-328.

sop_intro.t: Reactive Windshear is a memory item. The response depends on phase. Do not change configuration until out of the shear, closely monitor flight path and speed, and recover smoothly to normal climb.

condition.t: Condition: Before V one

items.why: Reject only if significant airspeed variations occur below V1 AND the Captain judges sufficient runway remains to stop.

reveal.t: The answer is REJECT. Callout: STOP.

explain.t: Reject only if significant airspeed variations occur below V one AND the Captain judges sufficient runway remains to stop. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-SURV p.2705.

condition.t: Condition: After V one

items.why: Set TOGA — escape the shear.

reveal.t: The answer is toga. Callout: WINDSHEAR toga.

explain.t: Set toga — escape the shear. Source: F-COM p.2705.

condition.t: Condition: After V one

items.why: Rotate at VR. If no FD bars, target initial pitch 17.5 deg.

explain.t: Rotate at V R. If no FD bars, target initial pitch 17.5 degrees. Source: F-COM p.2705.

condition.t: Condition: After V one

items.why: Follow SRS pitch orders rapidly and smoothly, not aggressively. Pull full aft stick if needed.

explain.t: Follow SRS pitch orders rapidly and smoothly, not aggressively. Pull full aft stick if needed. Source: F-COM p.2705; F-C-T-M p.327.

condition.t: Condition: Airborne, initial climb or landing

items.why: Set or confirm TOGA.

reveal.t: The answer is SET OR CONFIRM. Callout: WINDSHEAR toga.

correct_line.t: Correct. THR LEVERS AT toga, SET OR CONFIRM.

explain.t: Set or confirm toga. Source: F-COM p.2705.

condition.t: Condition: Airborne, initial climb or landing

items.why: Keep the AP on (it disengages if AOA exceeds alpha-prot).

explain.t: Keep the AP on (it disengages if AOA exceeds alpha-prot). Source: F-COM p.2705.

condition.t: Condition: Airborne, initial climb or landing

items.why: Follow SRS; full aft stick if needed. Do not change config until out of shear.

explain.t: Follow SRS; full aft stick if needed. Do not change config until out of shear. Source: F-COM p.2705.

condition.t: Condition: After V one

items.why: PM calls wind variations from the ND and V/S; reports to ATC when out of the shear.

explain.t: PM calls wind variations from the ND and vertical speed; reports to ATC when out of the shear. Source: F-C-T-M p.327.

condition.t: Condition: Airborne, initial climb or landing

items.why: PM calls wind variations from ND and V/S; reports to ATC out of the shear.

explain.t: PM calls wind variations from ND and vertical speed; reports to ATC out of the shear. Source: F-C-T-M p.328.

sop_intro.t: Stall Warning at Lift-off. A stall warning just after rotation in NORMAL law may be spurious from a damaged AOA probe. Fly the takeoff thrust and pitch, ensure a safe path; if it continues, consider it spurious.

items.why: Set TOGA thrust.

reveal.t: The answer is toga. Callout: STALL toga 15.

explain.t: Set toga thrust. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-MISC p.2583.

condition.t: Condition: At the same time

items.why: Target 15 degrees pitch (FCOM: 15 °) — at the same time as TOGA.

explain.t: Target 15 degrees pitch (F-COM: 15 °) — at the same time as toga. Source: F-COM p.2583.

items.why: Keep wings level for a safe climb. If the warning continues once safe path/speed achieved, consider it spurious.

explain.t: Keep wings level for a safe climb. If the warning continues once safe path/speed achieved, consider it spurious. Source: F-COM p.2583.

sop_intro.t: Stall Recovery. The key action is to reduce angle of attack — nose down. Recover energy only once stall indications have stopped. This is an immediate maneuver, not a read-and-do.
First and most important: reduce angle of attack.

items.why: Apply nose-down pitch on the sidestick to reduce AOA immediately. In case of lack of pitch-down authority, reducing thrust may be necessary.

reveal.t: The answer is APPLY. Callout: STALL, I HAVE CONTROL.

correct_line.t: Correct. NOSE DOWN PITCH CONTROL, APPLY.

explain.t: Apply nose-down pitch on the sidestick to reduce AOA immediately. In case of lack of pitch-down authority, reducing thrust may be necessary. Source: F-COM PRO-ABN-MISC p.2582.

sop_intro.t: First and most important: reduce angle of attack.

items.why: Roll wings level to recover.

explain.t: Roll wings level to recover. Source: F-COM p.2582.

sop_intro.t: Once stall indications have stopped, recover energy and flight path smoothly.

condition.t: Condition: When out of stall (no stall indications)

items.why: Add thrust smoothly — increase it too early or too aggressively and, on under-wing-mounted engines, the thrust-induced pitch-up can work against the required AOA reduction. Single-engine: progressively compensate the asymmetry with rudder.

reveal.t: The answer is INCREASE SMOOTHLY AS NEEDED.

correct_line.t: Correct. THRUST, INCREASE SMOOTHLY AS NEEDED.

explain.t: Add thrust smoothly — increase it too early or too aggressively and, on under-wing-mounted engines, the thrust-induced pitch-up can work against the required AOA reduction. Single-engine: progressively compensate the asymmetry with rudder. Source: F-COM p.2582; F-C-T-M p.420.

sop_intro.t: Once stall indications have stopped, recover energy and flight path smoothly.

condition.t: Condition: When out of stall (no stall indications)

items.why: Confirm speedbrakes retracted.

explain.t: Confirm speedbrakes retracted. Source: F-COM p.2582.

sop_intro.t: Once stall indications have stopped, recover energy and flight path smoothly.

condition.t: Condition: When out of stall (no stall indications)

items.why: Recover the flight path smoothly; if risk of ground contact, establish a positive climb gradient once clearly out of stall.

explain.t: Recover the flight path smoothly; if risk of ground contact, establish a positive climb gradient once clearly out of stall. Source: F-COM p.2582.

sop_intro.t: Once stall indications have stopped, recover energy and flight path smoothly.

condition.t: Condition: If clean configuration and below 20 000 feet

items.why: If clean and below 20 000 ft (FCTM: FL200), select FLAP 1 to increase the margin to AOA-stall.

reveal.t: The answer is SELECT. Callout: FLAPS 1.

explain.t: If clean and below 20 000 feet (F-C-T-M: flight level 200), select FLAP 1 to increase the margin to AOA-stall. Source: F-COM p.2582.

sop_intro.t: Once stall indications have stopped, recover energy and flight path smoothly.

condition.t: Condition: If clean and below 20 000 feet (on PF command)

items.why: PM selects FLAP 1 on the PF's command. (The PF-commands/PM-selects split is SOP crew tasksharing, not explicit FCOM/FCTM text; the FLAP 1 action itself is FCTM p.421.)

explain.t: PM selects FLAP 1 on the PF's command. (The PF-commands/PM-selects split is S-O-P crew tasksharing, not explicit F-COM/F-C-T-M text; the FLAP 1 action itself is F-C-T-M p.421.) Source: F-C-T-M PR-AEP-MISC p.421.

sop_intro.t: TCAS. A TA is advisory — do not maneuver on a TA alone. An RA is flown immediately: with AP/FD TCAS, follow the orders; without it, AP off and fly the green band, both FDs off. Notify ATC, and never maneuver opposite the RA.

condition.t: Condition: TRAFFIC TRAFFIC

items.why: PF announces the AP/FD TCAS mode armed (blue on FMA); PM checks.
items.cite: FCOM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.4/10 (PDF p.2702); FCTM AS-TCAS (PDF p.141)

reveal.t: The answer is ANNOUNCE. Callout: TCAS BLUE.

explain.t: PF announces the AP/FD TCAS mode armed (blue on FMA); PM checks. Source: F-COM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.4/10 (PDF p.2702); F-C-T-M AS-TCAS (PDF p.141).

condition.t: Condition: TRAFFIC TRAFFIC

items.why: Confirm the TCAS flight-guidance mode arms. If not, be prepared to manually follow the RA.

explain.t: Confirm the TCAS flight-guidance mode arms. If not, be prepared to manually follow the RA. Source: F-COM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.4/10 (PDF p.2702).

condition.t: Condition: Callout only if AP/FD TCAS fails to arm

items.why: Do not initiate an evasive maneuver on a TA alone. The 'TCAS, I have control' call is made ONLY if the AP/FD TCAS mode fails to arm — then the PF takes over and prepares to fly the RA manually.
items.cite: FCOM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.4/10 (PDF p.2702); FCTM AS-TCAS (PDF p.141)

reveal.t: The answer is DO NOT (on TA alone). Callout: TCAS, I HAVE CONTROL.

correct_line.t: Correct. MANEUVER, DO NOT (on TA alone).

explain.t: Do not initiate an evasive maneuver on a TA alone. The 'TCAS, I have control' call is made ONLY if the AP/FD TCAS mode fails to arm — then the PF takes over and prepares to fly the RA manually. Source: F-COM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.4/10 (PDF p.2702); F-C-T-M AS-TCAS (PDF p.141).

condition.t: Condition: RA, TCAS mode engages

items.why: If AP engaged, keep it on (it flies the RA). If AP off, smoothly and firmly follow the FD pitch bar — AP may be engaged.
items.cite: FCOM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.4/10 (PDF p.2702); FCTM AS-TCAS (PDF p.141)

explain.t: If AP engaged, keep it on (it flies the RA). If AP off, smoothly and firmly follow the FD pitch bar — AP may be engaged. Source: F-COM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.4/10 (PDF p.2702); F-C-T-M AS-TCAS (PDF p.141).

condition.t: Condition: RA, TCAS mode engages

items.why: Monitor V/S — fly the green area, keep out of the red. If V/S can't reach green, disconnect AP and override FD; use the full speed range Valpha-max to Vmax if needed.
items.cite: FCOM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.4/10 (PDF p.2702); FCTM AS-TCAS (PDF p.141)

explain.t: Monitor vertical speed — fly the green area, keep out of the red. If vertical speed can't reach green, disconnect AP and override FD; use the full speed range Valpha-max to Vmax if needed. Source: F-COM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.4/10 (PDF p.2702); F-C-T-M AS-TCAS (PDF p.141).

condition.t: Condition: If a CLIMB RA on final approach

items.why: A CLIMB RA on final: apply the procedure, then consider a go-around when clear.

explain.t: A CLIMB RA on final: apply the procedure, then consider a go-around when clear. Source: F-COM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.4/10 (PDF p.2702).

items.subflow: Resolution Advisory — AP/FD TCAS NOT available

condition.t: Condition: RA, TCAS mode does not engage

items.why: Disconnect AP promptly and smoothly.

explain.t: Disconnect AP promptly and smoothly. Source: F-COM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.5/10 (PDF p.2703).

items.subflow: Resolution Advisory — AP/FD TCAS NOT available

condition.t: Condition: RA, TCAS mode does not engage

items.why: Adjust/maintain pitch to reach the green and avoid the red — fly the green within 5 s. The PF orders both FDs off (forces A/THR speed mode, avoids FD/TCAS confusion); 'SET FDs OFF' is the PF's verbal order, not a book-printed callout.
items.cite: FCOM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.5/10 (PDF p.2703); FCTM AS-TCAS (PDF p.143)

reveal.t: The answer is ADJUST or MAINTAIN. Callout: SET FDs OFF.

correct_line.t: Correct. VERTICAL SPEED, ADJUST or MAINTAIN.

explain.t: Adjust/maintain pitch to reach the green and avoid the red — fly the green within 5 s. The PF orders both FDs off (forces auto-thrust speed mode, avoids FD/TCAS confusion); 'SET FDs OFF' is the PF's verbal order, not a book-printed callout. Source: F-COM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.5/10 (PDF p.2703); F-C-T-M AS-TCAS (PDF p.143).

items.subflow: Resolution Advisory — AP/FD TCAS NOT available

condition.t: Condition: If a CLIMB RA on final approach

items.why: A CLIMB RA on final approach: perform a go-around.

explain.t: A CLIMB RA on final approach: perform a go-around. Source: F-COM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.5/10 (PDF p.2703).

sop_intro.t: Resume the assigned clearance.

condition.t: Condition: CLEAR OF CONFLICT

items.why: Re-engage FDs/AP as required, adjust lateral/vertical guidance and speed to the latest ATC clearance. (AP/FD re-engagement is 'AS RQRD' — no fixed 'FDs ON' callout is printed.)

explain.t: Re-engage FDs/AP as required, adjust lateral/vertical guidance and speed to the latest ATC clearance. (AP/FD re-engagement is 'AS required' — no fixed 'FDs ON' callout is printed.) Source: F-COM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.5-6/10 (PDF pp.2703-2704).

condition.t: Condition: TRAFFIC TRAFFIC

items.why: PM confirms the PF's TCAS-blue call.

reveal.t: The answer is CHECK. Callout: CHECKED.

explain.t: PM confirms the PF's TCAS-blue call. Source: ATO S-O-P (crew readback).

condition.t: Condition: TRAFFIC TRAFFIC

items.why: If A/THR is off, set it on to avoid the AUTO FLT A/THR LIMITED alert in case of an RA.

explain.t: If auto-thrust is off, set it on to avoid the AUTO FLT auto-thrust LIMITED alert in case of an RA. Source: F-COM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.4/10 (PDF p.2702).

condition.t: Condition: RA, TCAS mode engages

items.why: PM notifies ATC of the RA.

reveal.t: The answer is NOTIFY. Callout: AIRBUS xxx TCAS RA.

explain.t: PM notifies ATC of the RA. Source: F-COM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.5/10 (PDF p.2703).

items.subflow: Resolution Advisory — AP/FD TCAS NOT available

condition.t: Condition: RA, TCAS mode does not engage

items.why: PM switches both FDs off on the PF's order.
items.cite: FCOM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.5/10 (PDF p.2703); FCTM AS-TCAS (PDF p.143)

explain.t: PM switches both FDs off on the PF's order. Source: F-COM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.5/10 (PDF p.2703); F-C-T-M AS-TCAS (PDF p.143).

items.subflow: Resolution Advisory — AP/FD TCAS NOT available

condition.t: Condition: RA, TCAS mode does not engage

items.why: PM notifies ATC.

reveal.t: The answer is NOTIFY. Callout: AIRBUS xxx TCAS RA.

explain.t: PM notifies ATC. Source: F-COM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.5/10 (PDF p.2703).

sop_intro.t: Resume the assigned clearance.

condition.t: Condition: CLEAR OF CONFLICT

items.callout: AIRBUS xxx CLEAR OF CONFLICT RETURNING TO ...
items.why: PM tells ATC clear of conflict, returning to assigned clearance.

reveal.t: The answer is NOTIFY. Callout: AIRBUS xxx CLEAR OF CONFLICT RETURNING TO

explain.t: PM tells ATC clear of conflict, returning to assigned clearance. Source: F-COM § PRO-ABN-SURV p.5/10 (PDF p.2703).

sop_intro.t: Unreliable Speed. Two objectives: fly the aircraft, then identify and isolate the affected ADR. Apply the memory items only if the safe conduct of the flight is impacted; otherwise go straight to the read-and-do. Do not fly the memory pitch/thrust values for long — apply the Q-R-H without delay.
Reflex actions: kill the automatics, fly a known pitch/thrust, clean up drag, then level off to troubleshoot.

items.why: Disconnect AP — the FMGS may be using corrupted air data.

reveal.t: The answer is OFF. Callout: UNRELIABLE SPEED.

explain.t: Disconnect AP — the F-M-G-S may be using corrupted air data. Source: Q-R-H 26.02A p.102.

sop_intro.t: Reflex actions: kill the automatics, fly a known pitch/thrust, clean up drag, then level off to troubleshoot.

items.why: Disconnect autothrust for the same reason.

explain.t: Disconnect autothrust for the same reason. Source: Q-R-H 26.02A p.102.

sop_intro.t: Reflex actions: kill the automatics, fly a known pitch/thrust, clean up drag, then level off to troubleshoot.

items.why: Flight directors off — fly raw pitch/thrust, not corrupted guidance. The PF challenge is 'SET FDs OFF'; the PM responds by setting both FDs off.

reveal.t: The answer is OFF. Callout: SET FDs OFF.

explain.t: Flight directors off — fly raw pitch/thrust, not corrupted guidance. The PF challenge is 'SET FDs OFF'; the PM responds by setting both FDs off. Source: Q-R-H 26.02A p.102; ATO S-O-P p.558/560 (callout).

sop_intro.t: Reflex actions: kill the automatics, fly a known pitch/thrust, clean up drag, then level off to troubleshoot.

items.why: Below THR RED ALT: 15 deg / TOGA. Above THR RED ALT and below FL100: 10 deg / CLB. Above FL100: 5 deg / CLB.

reveal.t: The answer is 15 or toga - 10 or climb - 5 or climb.

correct_line.t: Correct. PITCH/THRUST, 15 or toga - 10 or climb - 5 or climb.

explain.t: Below THR RED altitude: 15 degrees / toga. Above THR RED altitude and below flight level 100: 10 degrees / climb. Above flight level 100: 5 degrees / climb. Source: Q-R-H 26.02A p.102.

sop_intro.t: Reflex actions: kill the automatics, fly a known pitch/thrust, clean up drag, then level off to troubleshoot.

items.why: If CONF 0/1/2/3 maintain current conf; if CONF FULL select CONF 3 and maintain.

reveal.t: The answer is MAINTAIN or config 3.

explain.t: If config 0/1/2/3 maintain current config; if config FULL select config 3 and maintain. Source: Q-R-H 26.02A p.102.

sop_intro.t: Reflex actions: kill the automatics, fly a known pitch/thrust, clean up drag, then level off to troubleshoot.

items.why: Confirm speedbrakes retracted — clean the wing.

explain.t: Confirm speedbrakes retracted — clean the wing. Source: Q-R-H 26.02A p.102.

sop_intro.t: Reflex actions: kill the automatics, fly a known pitch/thrust, clean up drag, then level off to troubleshoot.

items.why: Gear up to reduce drag.

explain.t: Gear up to reduce drag. Source: Q-R-H 26.02A p.102.

sop_intro.t: Reflex actions: kill the automatics, fly a known pitch/thrust, clean up drag, then level off to troubleshoot.

items.why: When at or above MSA or circuit altitude, level off to troubleshoot — gate into the read-and-do.

reveal.t: The answer is AT or ABOVE M-S-A OR CIRCUIT altitude.

correct_line.t: Correct. LEVEL OFF, AT or ABOVE M-S-A OR CIRCUIT altitude.

explain.t: When at or above M-S-A or circuit altitude, level off to troubleshoot — gate into the read-and-do. Source: Q-R-H 26.02A p.102.

sop_intro.t: Read-and-do: stabilize on BUSS or pitch/thrust tables, then identify and isolate the faulty ADR.

condition.t: Condition: Below flight level 250

items.why: Below FL250 use the Back-Up Speed Scale (BUSS) via the BKUP SPD/ALT pb — this action is reversible. (Note: 'BIRD ON' / TRK-FPA selection is a separate PM action, not the BUSS activation.)

explain.t: Below flight level 250 use the Back-Up Speed Scale (BUSS) via the BKUP speed/altitude pushbutton — this action is reversible. (Note: 'BIRD ON' / track F-P-A selection is a separate PM action, not the BUSS activation.) Source: Q-R-H 26.02A/B p.102-103.

sop_intro.t: Read-and-do: stabilize on BUSS or pitch/thrust tables, then identify and isolate the faulty ADR.

condition.t: Condition: Below flight level 250 (BUSS active)

items.why: Fly the green band of the BUSS scale. If BUSS doesn't react to stick input, disregard it and use pitch/thrust tables. Do not use speedbrakes with BUSS.

reveal.t: The answer is FLY THE GREEN.

explain.t: Fly the green band of the BUSS scale. If BUSS doesn't react to stick input, disregard it and use pitch/thrust tables. Do not use speedbrakes with BUSS. Source: Q-R-H 26.02B p.103; F-C-T-M p.434.

sop_intro.t: Read-and-do: stabilize on BUSS or pitch/thrust tables, then identify and isolate the faulty ADR.

condition.t: Condition: Above flight level 250

items.why: Above FL250 fly the QRH pitch/thrust tables (50/60/70 t per phase). RESPECT STALL WARNING.

explain.t: Above flight level 250 fly the Q-R-H pitch/thrust tables (50/60/70 t per phase). RESPECT STALL WARNING. Source: Q-R-H 26.02C-D p.104-105.

sop_intro.t: Reflex actions: kill the automatics, fly a known pitch/thrust, clean up drag, then level off to troubleshoot.

items.why: PM confirms the discrepancy — standby vs own PFD, and own PFD vs PF's PFD.

reveal.t: The answer is CONFIRM. Callout: UNRELIABLE SPEED.

explain.t: PM confirms the discrepancy — standby vs own P-F-D, and own P-F-D vs PF's P-F-D. Source: F-C-T-M PR-AEP-NAV p.431.

sop_intro.t: Reflex actions: kill the automatics, fly a known pitch/thrust, clean up drag, then level off to troubleshoot.

items.why: Cross-confirm speedbrakes retracted.

explain.t: Cross-confirm speedbrakes retracted. Source: Q-R-H 26.02A p.102.

sop_intro.t: Read-and-do: stabilize on BUSS or pitch/thrust tables, then identify and isolate the faulty ADR.

items.why: Ensure probe heat on — a blocked/iced pitot is a common cause.

correct_line.t: Correct. Probe and Window Heat, ON.

explain.t: Ensure probe heat on — a blocked/iced pitot is a common cause. Source: Q-R-H 26.02B p.103.

sop_intro.t: Read-and-do: stabilize on BUSS or pitch/thrust tables, then identify and isolate the faulty ADR.

items.why: Cross-check all speeds. Note ADR3 and STBY use the same probe data.

explain.t: Cross-check all speeds. Note ADR3 and STBY use the same probe data. Source: Q-R-H 26.02B p.103.

sop_intro.t: Read-and-do: stabilize on BUSS or pitch/thrust tables, then identify and isolate the faulty ADR.

condition.t: Condition: If at least one ADR confirmed reliable

items.why: If a reliable ADR is identified, use it and switch the unreliable ADR(s) off.

explain.t: If a reliable ADR is identified, use it and switch the unreliable ADR(s) off. Source: Q-R-H 26.02B p.103.

sop_intro.t: Read-and-do: stabilize on BUSS or pitch/thrust tables, then identify and isolate the faulty ADR.

condition.t: Condition: If affected ADR(s) cannot be identified

items.why: If none can be identified, keep one ADR on and switch two off — prevents the laws using two coherent-but-wrong ADRs. Do NOT instinctively reject an outlier; all three can read the same wrong value.

explain.t: If none can be identified, keep one ADR on and switch two off — prevents the laws using two coherent-but-wrong ADRs. Do NOT instinctively reject an outlier; all three can read the same wrong value. Source: Q-R-H 26.02B p.103; F-C-T-M p.434.

sop_intro.t: The PF orders the PM with five verbs: SET means rotate the knob to a value without changing mode; PULL engages a selected mode or target; MANAGE or PUSH engages a managed mode or target; ARM pushes the named pushbutton; and the simple ON or OFF works the autopilot, flight directors, autothrust and the bird. Every action is cross-checked on the P-F-D and N-D. I name the intent, you give the standard call.

items.why: PULL = engage a selected mode/target — pull and turn the HDG-TRK knob.

reveal.t: The answer is PULL HEADING 270. Callout: PULL HEADING 270.

correct_line.t: Correct. Engage a selected heading of 270, PULL HEADING 270.

explain.t: PULL = engage a selected mode/target — pull and turn the heading-TRK knob. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-90-A, p. 2 (idx 2895).

Every action is cross-checked on the P-F-D and N-D. I name the intent, you give the standard call.

explain.t: MANAGE NAV = push the heading-TRK knob for managed lateral. Active mode announced without colour. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-90-A, p. 2 (idx 2895).

flows.name: Takeoff
flows.source: A320 Tutorials with UPRT - Flow Patterns - Cockpit Panels (January 2024)

sop_intro.t: Before the takeoff roll, the golden-rules and FMA brief - review only, no readback. The four Airbus Golden Rules: one, fly, navigate, communicate, in this order and with appropriate tasksharing. Two, use the appropriate level of automation at all times. Three, understand the FMA at all times. Four, take action if things do not go as expected.

items.item: Expected takeoff FMA: MAN FLEX or MAN TOGA, SRS, RUNWAY, autothrust blue, with CLIMB and NAV armed. Announce it, cross-check it; if it is not what you briefed, do not roll.
items.why: Brief-only (operator): the golden rules and expected takeoff FMA are reviewed aloud, not drilled. Rule three - understand the FMA - is the takeoff one: if the FMA at thrust set does not match the brief, do not begin the roll.
items.cite: FCTM Rev 2018-08-13 § AOP-40 (Golden Rules for Pilots), p. 1/4-4/4; expected FMA per FCOM § PRO-NOR-SOP-12, p. 2/6 (golden-rules wording committee-verified byte-exact 2026-06-29, runs/20260629T034912Z)

narration.t: Expected takeoff FMA: MAN flex or MAN toga, SRS, RUNWAY, autothrust blue, with CLIMB and NAV armed. Announce it, cross-check it; if it is not what you briefed, do not roll.

sop_intro.t: Takeoff-roll and rotation technique. I'll talk you through each case - the condition, the technique, and why.

items.item: Roll sidestick - crosswind twenty knots or less, no tailwind
items.callout: HALF FORWARD to 80 kt, then neutral by 100 kt
items.why: Counters the nose-up from setting takeoff thrust. Standard case (crosswind at or below 20 kt, no tailwind): apply half forward sidestick until 80 kt, then release gradually to neutral by 100 kt.
items.cite: FCOM § PRO-NOR-SOP-12, p. 1/6; FCTM § PR-NP-SOP-120, p. 1/8

brief_line.t: Roll sidestick - crosswind twenty knots or less, no tailwind. HALF FORWARD. Counters the nose-up from setting takeoff thrust. Standard case (crosswind at or below 20 knots, no tailwind): apply half forward sidestick until 80 knots, then release gradually to neutral by 100 knots.

sop_intro.t: Takeoff-roll and rotation technique. I'll talk you through each case - the condition, the technique, and why.

items.item: Roll sidestick - tailwind, or crosswind above twenty knots
items.callout: FULL FORWARD to 80 kt, neutral by 100 kt; thrust to takeoff by 40 kt groundspeed
items.why: With a tailwind or crosswind greater than 20 kt, apply full forward sidestick (held to 80 kt, neutral by 100 kt) and increase thrust progressively to reach takeoff thrust by 40 kt groundspeed. FCOM gives the 20 kt threshold and groups tailwind with it; FCTM states only 'full forward in cross wind case' - no number, no tailwind.
items.cite: FCOM § PRO-NOR-SOP-12, p. 1/6; FCTM § PR-NP-SOP-120, p. 1/8

brief_line.t: Roll sidestick - tailwind, or crosswind above twenty knots. FULL FORWARD. With a tailwind or crosswind greater than 20 knots, apply full forward sidestick (held to 80 knots, neutral by 100 knots) and increase thrust progressively to reach takeoff thrust by 40 knots groundspeed. F-COM gives the 20 knots threshold and groups tailwind with it; F-C-T-M states only 'full forward in cross wind case' - no number, no tailwind.

sop_intro.t: Takeoff-roll and rotation technique. I'll talk you through each case - the condition, the technique, and why.

items.item: Into-wind aileron on a crosswind takeoff
items.callout: Not routine; small inputs only, wings level; never more than one-third stick (spoilers)
items.why: Into-wind aileron is NOT routinely used. In strong crosswind use only small lateral input to keep wings level; avoid more than a third of sidestick deflection, which deploys spoilers, increases drag, and worsens the turn-into-wind tendency.

brief_line.t: Into-wind aileron on a crosswind takeoff. SMALL INPUTS, WINGS LEVEL. Into-wind aileron is NOT routinely used. In strong crosswind use only small lateral input to keep wings level; avoid more than a third of sidestick deflection, which deploys spoilers, increases drag, and worsens the turn-into-wind tendency.

sop_intro.t: Takeoff-roll and rotation technique. I'll talk you through each case - the condition, the technique, and why.

items.callout: Rudder pedals; nosewheel steering gone by 130 kt; no tiller
items.why: Keep straight with rudder pedals. Nosewheel steering authority decays with groundspeed and is no longer effective at 130 kt wheel speed, where the steering-to-pedal connection is removed - so more rudder is needed in strong crosswind from that point. The tiller is not used on the roll (too sensitive).
items.cite: FCOM § PRO-NOR-SOP-12, p. 2/6; FCTM § PR-NP-SOP-120, p. 1/8

brief_line.t: Directional control during the roll. RUDDER. Keep straight with rudder pedals. Nosewheel steering authority decays with groundspeed and is no longer effective at 130 knots wheel speed, where the steering-to-pedal connection is removed - so more rudder is needed in strong crosswind from that point. The tiller is not used on the roll (too sensitive).

sop_intro.t: Takeoff-roll and rotation technique. I'll talk you through each case - the condition, the technique, and why.

items.action: THREE DEGREES PER SECOND TO FIFTEEN
items.callout: At VR, about 3 deg/s to 15 deg (12.5 deg engine-out), then follow SRS
items.why: At VR, a positive aft stick input for a continuous rotation of about 3 deg/s toward 15 deg pitch (12.5 deg if one engine is failed); follow the SRS pitch bar after liftoff (liftoff around 10 deg, 4-5 s after initiation). Minimize lateral inputs through the rotation to avoid spoiler extension.
items.cite: FCOM § PRO-NOR-SOP-12, p. 3/6; FCTM § PR-NP-SOP-120, p. 2/8

brief_line.t: Rotation - rate and pitch target. THREE DEGREES PER SECOND TO FIFTEEN. At V R, a positive aft stick input for a continuous rotation of about 3 degrees/s toward 15 degrees pitch (12.5 degrees if one engine is failed); follow the SRS pitch bar after liftoff (liftoff around 10 degrees, 4-5 s after initiation). Minimize lateral inputs through the rotation to avoid spoiler extension.

condition.t: Condition: Standard: crosswind at or below 20 knots and no tailwind

items.why: PF's standard call that opens the takeoff roll; a procedural callout, not a system action.

reveal.t: The answer is ANNOUNCE. Callout: TAKEOFF.

explain.t: PF's standard call that opens the takeoff roll; a procedural callout, not a system action. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 1/6.

condition.t: Condition: Standard: crosswind at or below 20 knots and no tailwind

items.why: Set this stabilized intermediate thrust before going to takeoff power. The FADEC has a keep-out zone (60-74% N1) to avoid steady-state fan flutter, which can make EPR increase discontinuously during thrust setting.
items.cite: FCOM § PRO-NOR-SOP-12, p. 1/6 and p. 2/6 (rationale § DSC-70-20)

reveal.t: The answer is 50% N1 or 1.05 EPR.

correct_line.t: Correct. Thrust Levers, 50% N1 or 1.05 EPR.

explain.t: Set this stabilized intermediate thrust before going to takeoff power. The FADEC has a keep-out zone (60-74% N1) to avoid steady-state fan flutter, which can make EPR increase discontinuously during thrust setting. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 1/6 and p. 2/6 (rationale § DSC-70-20).

condition.t: Condition: Standard: crosswind at or below 20 knots and no tailwind

items.why: Release brakes after setting intermediate thrust to begin the ground run before advancing to takeoff thrust; a procedural sequencing step.

explain.t: Release brakes after setting intermediate thrust to begin the ground run before advancing to takeoff thrust; a procedural sequencing step. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 1/6.

condition.t: Condition: Standard: crosswind at or below 20 knots and no tailwind

items.why: Advance the levers to the FLX or TOGA detent to command computed takeoff thrust; once set, the Captain keeps a hand on the levers until V1 so a reject can be initiated without delay.

reveal.t: The answer is FLX or toga.

correct_line.t: Correct. Thrust Levers, FLX or toga.

explain.t: Advance the levers to the FLX or toga detent to command computed takeoff thrust; once set, the Captain keeps a hand on the levers until V one so a reject can be initiated without delay. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 1/6.

condition.t: Condition: Standard: crosswind at or below 20 knots and no tailwind

items.why: Track the centerline with rudder. At 130 kt wheel speed the nosewheel-steering-to-rudder-pedal connection is removed, so in strong crosswinds more rudder input is required from that point to stop the aircraft turning into the wind.

explain.t: Track the centerline with rudder. At 130 knots wheel speed the nosewheel-steering-to-rudder-pedal connection is removed, so in strong crosswinds more rudder input is required from that point to stop the aircraft turning into the wind. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 2/6.

condition.t: Condition: Standard: crosswind at or below 20 knots and no tailwind

items.callout: MAN FLEX 45, SRS, RUNWAY, AUTO THRUST BLUE
items.why: PF announces the displayed FMA modes (MAN TOGA or MAN FLX xx / SRS / RWY or blank / A/THR in blue) so both pilots share the same picture of armed/active modes.

reveal.t: The answer is ANNOUNCE. Callout: MAN flex 45, SRS, RUNWAY, AUTO THRUST BLUE.

explain.t: PF announces the displayed FMA modes (MAN toga or MAN FLX xx / SRS / runway or blank / auto-thrust in blue) so both pilots share the same picture of armed/active modes. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 2/6.

condition.t: Condition: Standard: crosswind at or below 20 knots and no tailwind

items.why: Start the chronometer at the takeoff roll; a procedural action with no system rationale given in the SOP.

explain.t: Start the chronometer at the takeoff roll; a procedural action with no system rationale given in the S-O-P. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 2/6.

condition.t: Condition: Standard: crosswind at or below 20 knots and no tailwind

items.why: Crosscheck the FMA shows the correct takeoff modes (MAN TOGA or MAN FLX xx / SRS / RWY or blank / A/THR in blue) and that the FMS position on the ND has the aircraft on the runway centerline.

reveal.t: The answer is MONITOR. Callout: CHECKED.

correct_line.t: Correct. P-F-D, F-M-A, and N-D, MONITOR.

explain.t: Crosscheck the FMA shows the correct takeoff modes (MAN toga or MAN FLX xx / SRS / runway or blank / auto-thrust in blue) and that the FMS position on the ND has the aircraft on the runway centerline. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 2/6.

condition.t: Condition: Before reaching 80 knots

items.why: Before 80 kt confirm each engine's actual EPR has reached the EPR rating limit and EGT is in band, so both engines are verified making planned thrust during the BELOW 80 KT check.

reveal.t: The answer is CHECK. Callout: THRUST SET.

correct_line.t: Correct. TAKEOFF N1 or EPR, CHECK.

explain.t: Before 80 knots confirm each engine's actual EPR has reached the EPR rating limit and EGT is in band, so both engines are verified making planned thrust during the BELOW 80 knots check. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 2/6.

items.why: Scan airspeed, EPR and EGT throughout the takeoff so any engine or speed anomaly is caught during the roll.

correct_line.t: Correct. P-F-D and Engine indications, MONITOR.

explain.t: Scan airspeed, EPR and EGT throughout the takeoff so any engine or speed anomaly is caught during the roll. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 2/6.

condition.t: Condition: At 100 knots

items.why: PM calls 100 kt; the PF crosschecks the PFD speed. 100 kt marks the shift in reject philosophy (below: Captain may abort; above: more serious).

reveal.t: The answer is ANNOUNCE. Callout: ONE HUNDRED KNOTS.

explain.t: PM calls 100 knots; the PF crosschecks the P-F-D speed. 100 knots marks the shift in reject philosophy (below: Captain may abort; above: more serious). Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 2/6.

condition.t: Condition: At V one

items.why: V1 is the gate up to which the Captain keeps a hand on the thrust levers; the callout marks reaching it.

reveal.t: The answer is ANNOUNCE. Callout: V one.

explain.t: V one is the gate up to which the Captain keeps a hand on the thrust levers; the callout marks reaching it. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 1/6 (rationale) and p. 3/6 (callout).

condition.t: Condition: At V R

items.why: At VR the PM orders the rotation ('ROTATE'); the PF performs it.

reveal.t: The answer is ORDER. Callout: ROTATE.

explain.t: At V R the PM orders the rotation ('ROTATE'); the PF performs it. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 3/6.

condition.t: Condition: When Vertical Speed positive and Radio Altitude increases

items.why: PM calls POSITIVE CLIMB once there is a positive rate of climb and the radio height has increased; this suggests to the PF that the landing gear can be retracted, gating the L/G UP order.

reveal.t: The answer is ANNOUNCE. Callout: POSITIVE CLIMB.

explain.t: PM calls POSITIVE CLIMB once there is a positive rate of climb and the radio height has increased; this suggests to the PF that the landing gear can be retracted, gating the landing gear UP order. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 3/6 (definition F-C-T-M § PR-AEP-ENG).

condition.t: Condition: When Vertical Speed positive and Radio Altitude increases

items.why: On the PF's L/G UP order (given when positive climb is confirmed), the PM selects the gear up.

reveal.t: The answer is UP. Callout: GEAR UP.

explain.t: On the PF's landing gear UP order (given when positive climb is confirmed), the PM selects the gear up. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 3/6.

condition.t: Condition: If takeoff was performed with packs off

items.why: Select PACK 1 on after CLB thrust reduction, and PACK 2 at least 10 s later, for passenger comfort. Packs on before reducing takeoff thrust would raise EGT; leaving them off after the takeoff phase triggers an ECAM caution.

reveal.t: The answer is ON. Callout: CHECKED.

correct_line.t: Correct. Packs One and Two, ON.

explain.t: Select PACK 1 on after climb thrust reduction, and PACK 2 at least 10 s later, for passenger comfort. Packs on before reducing takeoff thrust would raise EGT; leaving them off after the takeoff phase triggers an E-CAM caution. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 4/6.

condition.t: Condition: At F speed with positive speed trend

items.why: On the PF's order and after confirming positive speed trend ('SPEED CHECKED'), the PM selects FLAPS 1.

reveal.t: The answer is SELECT. Callout: SPEED CHECKED / FLAPS 1.

explain.t: On the PF's order and after confirming positive speed trend ('SPEED CHECKED'), the PM selects FLAPS 1. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 4/6.

condition.t: Condition: At S speed with positive speed trend

items.why: On the PF's order and after confirming positive speed trend, the PM selects FLAPS 0 (clean).

reveal.t: The answer is SELECT. Callout: SPEED CHECKED / FLAPS 0.

explain.t: On the PF's order and after confirming positive speed trend, the PM selects FLAPS 0 (clean). Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 4/6.

condition.t: Condition: At acceleration altitude

items.why: Scheduled SOP clean-up action: at S speed the procedure calls to disarm the ground spoilers. The SOP states the action; it gives no further rationale.

explain.t: Scheduled S-O-P clean-up action: at S speed the procedure calls to disarm the ground spoilers. The S-O-P states the action; it gives no further rationale. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 4/6.

condition.t: Condition: At acceleration altitude

items.why: Crosscheck only: the gear was ordered up earlier at positive climb, so by the acceleration phase this confirms the landing gear is up.

explain.t: Crosscheck only: the gear was ordered up earlier at positive climb, so by the acceleration phase this confirms the landing gear is up. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 3/6.

condition.t: Condition: At acceleration altitude

items.why: At S speed the NOSE and RWY TURN OFF switches are set OFF and other exterior lights AS RQRD; LAND lights may stay ON per airline policy or regulatory recommendation.

explain.t: At S speed the NOSE and runway TURN OFF switches are set OFF and other exterior lights AS required; LAND lights may stay ON per airline policy or regulatory recommendation. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-12, p. 4/6.

condition.t: Condition: If APU was used for takeoff

items.why: If the APU supplied bleed air during takeoff, set its bleed OFF now that the engine bleed/packs carry the load.

explain.t: If the APU supplied bleed air during takeoff, set its bleed OFF now that the engine bleed/packs carry the load. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-13, p. 1/2.

condition.t: Condition: If APU was used for takeoff

items.why: After APU BLEED OFF, with the APU no longer needed it is shut down (APU MASTER SW OFF).

explain.t: After APU bleed OFF, with the APU no longer needed it is shut down (APU master switch OFF). Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-13, p. 1/2.

items.why: Weather-driven selection: select IGN if severe turbulence or heavy rain is encountered; otherwise leave NORM.

correct_line.t: Correct. Engine Mode selector, AS required.

explain.t: Weather-driven selection: select ignition if severe turbulence or heavy rain is encountered; otherwise leave normal. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-13, p. 1/2.

condition.t: Condition: If the takeoff was performed with the TA ONLY mode

items.why: Restore full TA/RA if the takeoff was performed with TA ONLY, so the system can again command resolution advisories in the climb.

explain.t: Restore full T-A R-A if the takeoff was performed with TA ONLY, so the system can again command resolution advisories in the climb. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-13, p. 1/2.

items.why: Engine anti-ice ON in icing conditions (existing or anticipated), except in climb/cruise with SAT below -40 C. With ENG ANTI ICE ON the FADEC auto-selects continuous ignition and the IGNITION memo appears.

explain.t: Engine anti-ice ON in icing conditions (existing or anticipated), except in climb/cruise with SAT below -40 C. With ENG ANTI ICE ON the FADEC auto-selects continuous ignition and the IGNITION memo appears. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-13, p. 1/2.

condition.t: Condition: At transition altitude

items.why: PM also sets STD and cross-checks; the standard datum is set on both sides at transition altitude so both altimeters read flight levels against one reference.

reveal.t: The answer is SET STANDARD. Callout: STANDARD CROSSCHECKED.

explain.t: PM also sets standard and cross-checks; the standard datum is set on both sides at transition altitude so both altimeters read flight levels against one reference. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-14, p. 2/4.

condition.t: Condition: At transition altitude

items.why: Engine anti-ice ON when icing conditions exist or are anticipated, the exception being climb and cruise with SAT below -40 C where ice does not form; 'as required' avoids the bleed/thrust penalty when ice cannot accrete.

explain.t: Engine anti-ice ON when icing conditions exist or are anticipated, the exception being climb and cruise with SAT below -40 C where ice does not form; 'as required' avoids the bleed/thrust penalty when ice cannot accrete. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-14, p. 2/4.

flows.name: Taxi - Before Takeoff - Line-Up
flows.source: A320 Tutorials with UPRT - Flow Patterns - Cockpit Panels (January 2024)

sop_intro.t: Taxi Initiation runs in two phases. Stationary, before you start moving: the PF and PM perform the FLIGHT CONTROL CHECK on the flight controls page (placed here, before taxi clearance, as our S-O-P — F-C-T-M allows it before or during taxi, only before arming the autobrake), and the PM obtains taxi clearance. Then, once cleared and rolling: PF calls CLEAR LEFT/RIGHT with PM cross-calling the other side, selects the taxi and runway-turnoff lights, releases the park brake, checks brake pressure at zero and does the BRAKE CHECK by gently pressing the pedals. STROBE goes on when crossing a runway. Let's drill it.

items.why: Before arming the autobrake, PF applies full sidestick while PM calls full travel on the F/CTL page; rudder checked separately via PEDAL DISC pb.

reveal.t: The answer is CHECK. Callout: FLIGHT CONTROL CHECK.

explain.t: Before arming the autobrake, PF applies full sidestick while PM calls full travel on the flight controls page; rudder checked separately via PEDAL DISC pushbutton. Source: F-C-T-M § PR-NP-S-O-P-100, p. 2/10.

sop_intro.t: Taxi Initiation runs in two phases. Stationary, before you start moving: the PF and PM perform the FLIGHT CONTROL CHECK on the flight controls page (placed here, before taxi clearance, as our S-O-P — F-C-T-M allows it before or during taxi, only before arming the autobrake), and the PM obtains taxi clearance. Then, once cleared and rolling: PF calls CLEAR LEFT/RIGHT with PM cross-calling the other side, selects the taxi and runway-turnoff lights, releases the park brake, checks brake pressure at zero and does the BRAKE CHECK by gently pressing the pedals. STROBE goes on when crossing a runway. Let's drill it.

condition.t: Condition: When taxi clearance obtained

items.why: TAXI turns on only the taxi light; both nose-gear lights go off automatically when the gear is retracted.

explain.t: TAXI turns on only the taxi light; both nose-gear lights go off automatically when the gear is retracted. Source: F-COM § DSC-33-20-20, p. 4/6.

sop_intro.t: Taxi Initiation runs in two phases. Stationary, before you start moving: the PF and PM perform the FLIGHT CONTROL CHECK on the flight controls page (placed here, before taxi clearance, as our S-O-P — F-C-T-M allows it before or during taxi, only before arming the autobrake), and the PM obtains taxi clearance. Then, once cleared and rolling: PF calls CLEAR LEFT/RIGHT with PM cross-calling the other side, selects the taxi and runway-turnoff lights, releases the park brake, checks brake pressure at zero and does the BRAKE CHECK by gently pressing the pedals. STROBE goes on when crossing a runway. Let's drill it.

items.why: Runway turn-off lights; go off automatically on gear retraction.

explain.t: Runway turn-off lights; go off automatically on gear retraction. Source: F-COM § DSC-33-20-20, p. 4/6.

sop_intro.t: Taxi Initiation runs in two phases. Stationary, before you start moving: the PF and PM perform the FLIGHT CONTROL CHECK on the flight controls page (placed here, before taxi clearance, as our S-O-P — F-C-T-M allows it before or during taxi, only before arming the autobrake), and the PM obtains taxi clearance. Then, once cleared and rolling: PF calls CLEAR LEFT/RIGHT with PM cross-calling the other side, selects the taxi and runway-turnoff lights, releases the park brake, checks brake pressure at zero and does the BRAKE CHECK by gently pressing the pedals. STROBE goes on when crossing a runway. Let's drill it.

items.why: Release the parking brake so the aircraft can roll for taxi.

explain.t: Release the parking brake so the aircraft can roll for taxi. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-10, p. 1/4.

sop_intro.t: Taxi Initiation runs in two phases. Stationary, before you start moving: the PF and PM perform the FLIGHT CONTROL CHECK on the flight controls page (placed here, before taxi clearance, as our S-O-P — F-C-T-M allows it before or during taxi, only before arming the autobrake), and the PM obtains taxi clearance. Then, once cleared and rolling: PF calls CLEAR LEFT/RIGHT with PM cross-calling the other side, selects the taxi and runway-turnoff lights, releases the park brake, checks brake pressure at zero and does the BRAKE CHECK by gently pressing the pedals. STROBE goes on when crossing a runway. Let's drill it.

items.why: Confirms the brakes are released (triple indicator at zero); slight residual pressure may show briefly.

reveal.t: The answer is CHECK AT ZERO.

correct_line.t: Correct. Brake Pressure, CHECK AT ZERO.

explain.t: Confirms the brakes are released (triple indicator at zero); slight residual pressure may show briefly. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-10, p. 1/4.

sop_intro.t: Taxi Initiation runs in two phases. Stationary, before you start moving: the PF and PM perform the FLIGHT CONTROL CHECK on the flight controls page (placed here, before taxi clearance, as our S-O-P — F-C-T-M allows it before or during taxi, only before arming the autobrake), and the PM obtains taxi clearance. Then, once cleared and rolling: PF calls CLEAR LEFT/RIGHT with PM cross-calling the other side, selects the taxi and runway-turnoff lights, releases the park brake, checks brake pressure at zero and does the BRAKE CHECK by gently pressing the pedals. STROBE goes on when crossing a runway. Let's drill it.

items.why: As the aircraft moves the PF gently presses the pedals to check normal braking; first low-speed application is less effective if parked wet a long time.

reveal.t: The answer is PRESS. Callout: BRAKE CHECK.

explain.t: As the aircraft moves the PF gently presses the pedals to check normal braking; first low-speed application is less effective if parked wet a long time. Source: F-C-T-M § PR-NP-S-O-P-100, p. 1/10.

sop_intro.t: Taxi Initiation runs in two phases. Stationary, before you start moving: the PF and PM perform the FLIGHT CONTROL CHECK on the flight controls page (placed here, before taxi clearance, as our S-O-P — F-C-T-M allows it before or during taxi, only before arming the autobrake), and the PM obtains taxi clearance. Then, once cleared and rolling: PF calls CLEAR LEFT/RIGHT with PM cross-calling the other side, selects the taxi and runway-turnoff lights, releases the park brake, checks brake pressure at zero and does the BRAKE CHECK by gently pressing the pedals. STROBE goes on when crossing a runway. Let's drill it.

condition.t: Condition: When crossing a runway

items.why: Three synchronized strobes; set ON when crossing a runway / entering the runway.

reveal.t: The answer is ON or AS required.

correct_line.t: Correct. Strobe switch, ON or AS required.

explain.t: Three synchronized strobes; set ON when crossing a runway / entering the runway. Source: F-COM § DSC-33-20-20, p. 4/6.

sop_intro.t: Taxi is the during-taxi set-up and the pre-takeoff configuration scan, merged. As PM you confirm the ATC clearance, check the FMS flight plan and speeds, set the FCU altitude (and heading only for a radar vector), check both FDs on and the P-F-D/ND, then run the numbered scan: AUTO BRK MAX on, TERR ON ND as required, ATC code/mode set for takeoff, engine mode selector as required, weather radar/P-W-S on with the radar displays set, press T.O CONFIG to TEST and confirm T.O CONFIG NORMAL, then check the T.O MEMO shows no blue. As PF you check the P-F-D/ND, confirm the departure briefing (only changes since the bay briefing), receive the cabin report, and call for the Taxi Checklist. Let's drill it.

items.why: Last scan of PFD/ND before takeoff; valid data, no flags.

explain.t: Last scan of P-F-D/ND before takeoff; valid data, no flags. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-10, p. 3/4.

sop_intro.t: Taxi is the during-taxi set-up and the pre-takeoff configuration scan, merged. As PM you confirm the ATC clearance, check the FMS flight plan and speeds, set the FCU altitude (and heading only for a radar vector), check both FDs on and the P-F-D/ND, then run the numbered scan: AUTO BRK MAX on, TERR ON ND as required, ATC code/mode set for takeoff, engine mode selector as required, weather radar/P-W-S on with the radar displays set, press T.O CONFIG to TEST and confirm T.O CONFIG NORMAL, then check the T.O MEMO shows no blue. As PF you check the P-F-D/ND, confirm the departure briefing (only changes since the bay briefing), receive the cabin report, and call for the Taxi Checklist. Let's drill it.

items.why: Review only changes since the full TAKEOFF BRIEFING at the bay (e.g. SID/runway condition change).

explain.t: Review only changes since the full TAKEOFF BRIEFING at the bay (e.g. sid/runway condition change). Source: F-C-T-M § PR-NP-S-O-P-100, p. 9/10.

sop_intro.t: Taxi is the during-taxi set-up and the pre-takeoff configuration scan, merged. As PM you confirm the ATC clearance, check the FMS flight plan and speeds, set the FCU altitude (and heading only for a radar vector), check both FDs on and the P-F-D/ND, then run the numbered scan: AUTO BRK MAX on, TERR ON ND as required, ATC code/mode set for takeoff, engine mode selector as required, weather radar/P-W-S on with the radar displays set, press T.O CONFIG to TEST and confirm T.O CONFIG NORMAL, then check the T.O MEMO shows no blue. As PF you check the P-F-D/ND, confirm the departure briefing (only changes since the bay briefing), receive the cabin report, and call for the Taxi Checklist. Let's drill it.

items.why: Obtain purser report, minimum 'CABIN SECURED FOR TAKEOFF'.

explain.t: Obtain purser report, minimum 'CABIN SECURED FOR TAKEOFF'. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-10, p. 4/4.

sop_intro.t: Taxi is the during-taxi set-up and the pre-takeoff configuration scan, merged. As PM you confirm the ATC clearance, check the FMS flight plan and speeds, set the FCU altitude (and heading only for a radar vector), check both FDs on and the P-F-D/ND, then run the numbered scan: AUTO BRK MAX on, TERR ON ND as required, ATC code/mode set for takeoff, engine mode selector as required, weather radar/P-W-S on with the radar displays set, press T.O CONFIG to TEST and confirm T.O CONFIG NORMAL, then check the T.O MEMO shows no blue. As PF you check the P-F-D/ND, confirm the departure briefing (only changes since the bay briefing), receive the cabin report, and call for the Taxi Checklist. Let's drill it.

items.why: PF calls for the Taxi Checklist at this point — you make the call, then the challenge-response runs.

sop_intro.t: Taxi Initiation runs in two phases. Stationary, before you start moving: the PF and PM perform the FLIGHT CONTROL CHECK on the flight controls page (placed here, before taxi clearance, as our S-O-P — F-C-T-M allows it before or during taxi, only before arming the autobrake), and the PM obtains taxi clearance. Then, once cleared and rolling: PF calls CLEAR LEFT/RIGHT with PM cross-calling the other side, selects the taxi and runway-turnoff lights, releases the park brake, checks brake pressure at zero and does the BRAKE CHECK by gently pressing the pedals. STROBE goes on when crossing a runway. Let's drill it.

items.why: PM calls out full travel of elevators/ailerons and spoiler deflection on the F/CTL page.

explain.t: PM calls out full travel of elevators/ailerons and spoiler deflection on the flight controls page. Source: F-C-T-M § PR-NP-S-O-P-100, p. 2/10.

sop_intro.t: Taxi Initiation runs in two phases. Stationary, before you start moving: the PF and PM perform the FLIGHT CONTROL CHECK on the flight controls page (placed here, before taxi clearance, as our S-O-P — F-C-T-M allows it before or during taxi, only before arming the autobrake), and the PM obtains taxi clearance. Then, once cleared and rolling: PF calls CLEAR LEFT/RIGHT with PM cross-calling the other side, selects the taxi and runway-turnoff lights, releases the park brake, checks brake pressure at zero and does the BRAKE CHECK by gently pressing the pedals. STROBE goes on when crossing a runway. Let's drill it.

items.why: Coordination item — obtain ATC taxi clearance before moving.

explain.t: Coordination item — obtain ATC taxi clearance before moving. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-10, p. 1/4.

sop_intro.t: Taxi is the during-taxi set-up and the pre-takeoff configuration scan, merged. As PM you confirm the ATC clearance, check the FMS flight plan and speeds, set the FCU altitude (and heading only for a radar vector), check both FDs on and the P-F-D/ND, then run the numbered scan: AUTO BRK MAX on, TERR ON ND as required, ATC code/mode set for takeoff, engine mode selector as required, weather radar/P-W-S on with the radar displays set, press T.O CONFIG to TEST and confirm T.O CONFIG NORMAL, then check the T.O MEMO shows no blue. As PF you check the P-F-D/ND, confirm the departure briefing (only changes since the bay briefing), receive the cabin report, and call for the Taxi Checklist. Let's drill it.

items.why: Confirm the current ATC clearance matches planned routing/altitude.

explain.t: Confirm the current ATC clearance matches planned routing/altitude. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-10, p. 2/4.

Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-10, p. 4/4 (rationale § DSC-32-30-10).

correct_line.t: Correct. Weather Radar and Predictive Windshear, ON.

correct_line.t: Correct. Tee-cas mode selector, TA only or T-A R-A.

explain.t: T-A R-A is the default so TCAS can issue both traffic and resolution advisories; TA ONLY is used only at specific airports/procedures where an RA would be unwanted, such as closely-spaced parallel or converging runways. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-11, p. 1/4.

Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-11, p. 2/4 to 3/4.
