sop_intro.t: Line-Up flow at/approaching the holding point (deck p178/180): confirm takeoff runway and approach path clear, set TCAS, packs, exterior lights, advise cabin crew, then the Line-Up C/L.

items.why: Advise the cabin crew that takeoff is imminent so they are seated and secured.

explain.t: Advise the cabin crew that takeoff is imminent so they are seated and secured. Source: F-COM § PRO-NOR-S-O-P-11, p. 1/4.

flows.name: Visual Circuit / Visual Pattern
flows.source: A320 Tutorials with UPRT - Flow Patterns - Cockpit Panels (January 2024)

sop_intro.t: Before the takeoff roll for a visual circuit you preset the pattern targets on the FCU: the circuit altitude in the altitude window and the runway heading in the heading window, so heading and altitude are ready to pull once airborne. Then fly the takeoff as normal.

items.why: Set the planned circuit/pattern altitude in the FCU ALT window before takeoff so it is ready to pull/level at on the climb-out.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('When lined up: Set circuit ALT on FCU')

reveal.t: The answer is SET ON FCU.

correct_line.t: Correct. Circuit altitude, SET ON FCU.

explain.t: Set the planned circuit/pattern altitude in the FCU altitude window before takeoff so it is ready to pull/level at on the climb-out. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('When lined up: Set circuit altitude on FCU').

sop_intro.t: Before the takeoff roll for a visual circuit you preset the pattern targets on the FCU: the circuit altitude in the altitude window and the runway heading in the heading window, so heading and altitude are ready to pull once airborne. Then fly the takeoff as normal.

items.why: Preset the runway heading in the FCU HDG window so HDG can be pulled to selected immediately after liftoff for the climb-out track.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('Preset RWY HDG on FCU')

reveal.t: The answer is PRESET ON FCU.

correct_line.t: Correct. Runway heading, PRESET ON FCU.

explain.t: Preset the runway heading in the FCU heading window so heading can be pulled to selected immediately after liftoff for the climb-out track. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('Preset runway heading on FCU').

sop_intro.t: On the climb-out you pull heading to fly the runway/downwind track, level the circuit altitude with speed and auto-thrust, then pull S speed and select Flaps 1 as you set the downwind heading. The FMA walks MAN FLX SRS runway TRK on the roll, then LVR climb and altitude-star into altitude as you level. PF announces each FMA change, PM answers CHECKED.

items.why: Pull the FCU HDG knob to engage selected HDG (the preset runway heading) for the climb-out / downwind track. PULL = engage selected per SOP-90.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('Pull HDG'); SOP-90_standard_callouts.md (PULL = selected)

reveal.t: The answer is PULL. Callout: PULL heading.

explain.t: Pull the FCU heading knob to engage selected heading (the preset runway heading) for the climb-out / downwind track. PULL = engage selected per S-O-P-90. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('Pull heading'); S-O-P-90_standard_callouts.md (PULL = selected).

sop_intro.t: On the climb-out you pull heading to fly the runway/downwind track, level the circuit altitude with speed and auto-thrust, then pull S speed and select Flaps 1 as you set the downwind heading. The FMA walks MAN FLX SRS runway TRK on the roll, then LVR climb and altitude-star into altitude as you level. PF announces each FMA change, PM answers CHECKED.

items.why: Establish speed/auto-thrust for the level-off at circuit altitude; FMA shows LVR CLB then ALT-star (capture). Deck upper FMA strip.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('Speed, Auto thrust'; FMA MAN FLX+45 / LVR CLB / ALT*)

reveal.t: The answer is SELECT. Callout: SPEED, AUTO THRUST.

explain.t: Establish speed/auto-thrust for the level-off at circuit altitude; FMA shows LVR climb then altitude-star (capture). Deck upper FMA strip. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('Speed, Auto thrust'; FMA MAN FLX+45 / LVR climb / altitude*).

sop_intro.t: On the climb-out you pull heading to fly the runway/downwind track, level the circuit altitude with speed and auto-thrust, then pull S speed and select Flaps 1 as you set the downwind heading. The FMA walks MAN FLX SRS runway TRK on the roll, then LVR climb and altitude-star into altitude as you level. PF announces each FMA change, PM answers CHECKED.

items.why: Pull for selected S speed on downwind so the aircraft holds the slats-retract manoeuvre speed at Flaps 1.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('Pull S Speed')

reveal.t: The answer is PULL. Callout: PULL S SPEED.

explain.t: Pull for selected S speed on downwind so the aircraft holds the slats-retract manoeuvre speed at Flaps 1. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('Pull S Speed').

items.why: Select Flaps 1 for the downwind configuration on a visual circuit (King schedule: Flaps 1 on downwind, not at green dot).

reveal.t: The answer is ORDER. Callout: FLAPS 1.

explain.t: Select Flaps 1 for the downwind configuration on a visual circuit (King schedule: Flaps 1 on downwind, not at green dot). Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('Flaps 1').

sop_intro.t: On the climb-out you pull heading to fly the runway/downwind track, level the circuit altitude with speed and auto-thrust, then pull S speed and select Flaps 1 as you set the downwind heading. items.why: Set the downwind heading once turned and established parallel to the landing runway.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('Set downwind heading')

reveal.t: The answer is SET. Callout: SET DOWNWIND HEADING.

explain.t: Set the downwind heading once turned and established parallel to the landing runway. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('Set downwind heading').

sop_intro.t: Established on downwind at 1500 feet above airfield level you transition to hand-flying the visual pattern: autopilot off, both flight directors off, BIRD (flight path vector) on, set the downwind track when necessary, activate the approach phase on the MCDU, manage speed when necessary, and run the approach checklist. On the N-D use ROSE NAV, range 10 nautical miles, and the dotted range arcs to hold the runway about two and a half miles abeam.

items.why: Disconnect the autopilot to hand-fly the visual pattern. p418 (authority) lists AP OFF on the downwind cluster; p412 omits it.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p418 ('AP OFF') — authority over p412 which omits it

reveal.t: The answer is OFF. Callout: AP OFF.

explain.t: Disconnect the autopilot to hand-fly the visual pattern. p418 (authority) lists AP OFF on the downwind cluster; p412 omits it. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p418 ('AP OFF') — authority over p412 which omits it.

On the N-D use ROSE NAV, range 10 nautical miles, and the dotted range arcs to hold the runway about two and a half miles abeam.

On the N-D use ROSE NAV, range 10 nautical miles, and the dotted range arcs to hold the runway about two and a half miles abeam.

condition.t: Condition: When necessary

items.why: Set/maintain the downwind track as required to hold the 2.5 NM offset from the runway.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p418 ('Set downwind track when necessary'); p413 (ROSE NAV, 10 NM, 2.5 NM offset)

explain.t: Set/maintain the downwind track as required to hold the 2.5 NM offset from the runway. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p418 ('Set downwind track when necessary'); p413 (ROSE NAV, 10 NM, 2.5 NM offset).

sop_intro.t: Established on downwind at 1500 feet above airfield level you transition to hand-flying the visual pattern: autopilot off, both flight directors off, BIRD (flight path vector) on, set the downwind track when necessary, activate the approach phase on the MCDU, manage speed when necessary, and run the approach checklist. reveal.t: The answer is ACTIVATE. Callout: ACTIVATE APPROACH PHASE.

explain.t: Activate the approach phase on the MCDU performance page so the F-M-G-S targets approach speeds for the pattern. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p418 ('Activate Approach Phase').

sop_intro.t: Established on downwind at 1500 feet above airfield level you transition to hand-flying the visual pattern: autopilot off, both flight directors off, BIRD (flight path vector) on, set the downwind track when necessary, activate the approach phase on the MCDU, manage speed when necessary, and run the approach checklist. On the N-D use ROSE NAV, range 10 nautical miles, and the dotted range arcs to hold the runway about two and a half miles abeam.

condition.t: Condition: When necessary

items.why: Manage speed when necessary (p418 qualifies 'when necessary'; p412 shows it unqualified — p418 is authority).
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p418 ('Manage speed when necessary')

explain.t: Manage speed when necessary (p418 qualifies 'when necessary'; p412 shows it unqualified — p418 is authority). Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p418 ('Manage speed when necessary').

sop_intro.t: Established on downwind at 1500 feet above airfield level you transition to hand-flying the visual pattern: autopilot off, both flight directors off, BIRD (flight path vector) on, set the downwind track when necessary, activate the approach phase on the MCDU, manage speed when necessary, and run the approach checklist. items.why: Call for the approach checklist on the downwind leg.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p418 ('Approach checklist')

reveal.t: The answer is CALL. Callout: APPROACH CHECKLIST.

explain.t: Call for the approach checklist on the downwind leg. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p418 ('Approach checklist').

sop_intro.t: Abeam the threshold you start the chronometer and extend the downwind leg by three seconds per hundred feet above airfield level — forty-five seconds for fifteen hundred feet — corrected one second per knot of head or tailwind. Compare TAS and groundspeed: TAS minus GS gives the wind component; a tailwind shortens the leg, a headwind lengthens it.

items.why: Start the chronometer abeam the threshold; this is the reference for the timed downwind extension.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p414 ('Extend the downwind leg by 3 s per 100 ft above airfield level')

reveal.t: The answer is START ABEAM THRESHOLD. Callout: ABEAM.

explain.t: Start the chronometer abeam the threshold; this is the reference for the timed downwind extension. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p414 ('Extend the downwind leg by 3 s per 100 feet above airfield level').

sop_intro.t: As the timed leg runs out you configure for the turn to final: ten seconds before turning final select Flaps 2, and three seconds before turning final set the runway track.

condition.t: Condition: 10 s before turning final

items.why: Select Flaps 2 ten seconds before turning final on the timed visual circuit.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('10 s before turning final: FLAPS 2')

reveal.t: The answer is ORDER. Callout: FLAPS 2.

explain.t: Select Flaps 2 ten seconds before turning final on the timed visual circuit. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('10 s before turning final: FLAPS 2').

sop_intro.t: As the timed leg runs out you configure for the turn to final: ten seconds before turning final select Flaps 2, and three seconds before turning final set the runway track.

condition.t: Condition: 3 s before turning final

items.why: Set the runway track three seconds before turning final to roll out aligned with the landing runway.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('3 s before turning final: Set runway track')

reveal.t: The answer is SET. Callout: SET RUNWAY TRACK.

explain.t: Set the runway track three seconds before turning final to roll out aligned with the landing runway. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('3 s before turning final: Set runway track').

sop_intro.t: Turning final you initiate a shallow descent, call gear down and Flaps 3, then Flaps Full to be stabilized at V app by five hundred feet above airfield level, and run the landing checklist.

items.why: Initiate a shallow descent on the turn to final to capture the visual glide path.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('Initiate a shallow descent')

explain.t: Initiate a shallow descent on the turn to final to capture the visual glide path. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412 ('Initiate a shallow descent').

condition.t: Condition: Stabilized by 500 feet AAL

sop_intro.t: At the minima the PM announces ONE HUNDRED ABOVE at a hundred above and MINIMUM at the minimum; these are pin-programmed automatic callouts the PM monitors and only voices if the auto-call is inoperative. With sufficient visual reference the PF calls CONTINUE, then SET FDs OFF, SET RUNWAY TRACK, and AP off; without sufficient visual reference the PF calls GO AROUND, FLAPS.

items.why: At 100 ft above the MDA/DH the PM calls ONE HUNDRED ABOVE; PF answers CHECKED.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p243; SOP-90_standard_callouts.md:402-404

correct_line.t: Correct. Landing checklist, READ or RESPOND.

explain.t: PM runs the landing checklist challenge-response (the existing LANDING C/L). Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p412; LANDING C/L per S-O-P-90.

sop_intro.t: At the minima the PM announces ONE HUNDRED ABOVE at a hundred above and MINIMUM at the minimum; these are pin-programmed automatic callouts the PM monitors and only voices if the auto-call is inoperative. With sufficient visual reference the PF calls CONTINUE, then SET FDs OFF, SET RUNWAY TRACK, and AP off; without sufficient visual reference the PF calls GO AROUND, FLAPS.

items.why: PM announces (or monitors the auto-callout of) ONE HUNDRED ABOVE at 100 ft above the MDA/DH.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p243; SOP-90_standard_callouts.md:402,:414 (auto-callout footnote)

reveal.t: The answer is ANNOUNCE. Callout: ONE HUNDRED ABOVE.

explain.t: PM announces (or monitors the auto-callout of) ONE HUNDRED ABOVE at 100 feet above the M-D-A/D-H. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p243; S-O-P-90_standard_callouts.md:402,:414 (auto-callout footnote).

sop_intro.t: At the minima the PM announces ONE HUNDRED ABOVE at a hundred above and MINIMUM at the minimum; these are pin-programmed automatic callouts the PM monitors and only voices if the auto-call is inoperative. With sufficient visual reference the PF calls CONTINUE, then SET FDs OFF, SET RUNWAY TRACK, and AP off; without sufficient visual reference the PF calls GO AROUND, FLAPS.

items.why: PM announces (or monitors the auto-callout of) MINIMUM at the MDA/DH.
items.cite: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p243; SOP-90_standard_callouts.md:403-404,:414

reveal.t: The answer is ANNOUNCE. Callout: MINIMUM.

explain.t: PM announces (or monitors the auto-callout of) MINIMUM at the M-D-A/D-H. Source: A320 Tutorials JAN24 deck p243; S-O-P-90_standard_callouts.md:403-404,:414.
